> **âœ… M1 DONE (this commit):** the SHARED `find_tools` in ai-gateway (`federation/find-tools.ts` â€”
> ported the proven `tool_discovery.py` search to TS; `handleListTools` prepends the find_tools
> meta-tool; `handleCallTool` intercepts find_tools LOCALLY, no provider route). Tests: find-tools 11
> + handlers 13 = 24 green; nest build clean.
> **âœ… M2 DONE (this commit) â€” the per-SESSION state machine.** `session/tool-activation-store.ts`
> (Redis SADD+sliding-EXPIRE per session, keyed by session_id=key_id; in-memory fallback so it
> always works) + `session/tool-activation.ts` (fail-soft service: a store blip â†’ minimal surface,
> never throws). `tool-policy.ts`: `find_tools` is the always-allowed discovery meta-tool (passes
> the request gate + survives the scope filter, any scope). `scope-filter.ts`: `filterListResponseText`
> takes an `activated` set â†’ collapses the scoped list to `find_tools` âˆھ activated (a FRESH session
> shows ONLY find_tools); `scopeFilterFindToolsResult` intersects ai-gateway's full-catalogue matches
> with the key's scope (anti-oracle) + harvests the in-scope names; `isFindToolsCall`. Controller:
> on a find_tools call â†’ scope-filter the result + `activate()` the matches into the session; on
> tools/list â†’ collapse to the session surface. **No scope widening** (gateRequestBody unchanged;
> activation governs VISIBILITY, not permission). Tests: tool-activation 18 new + 2 controller
> updated; full mcp-public-gateway suite 200/200; both gateways nest-build clean.
> **âœ… /review-impl DONE â€” 1 MED found + FIXED.** Anti-oracle hole: `find_tools` smuggled inside a
> JSON-RPC **batch** bypassed `scopeFilterFindToolsResult` (`isFindToolsCall` is single-only), so a
> batched `[find_tools]` relayed its FULL-catalogue matches unfiltered (out-of-scope tool *names*
> leak â€” discovery, not callability; gateRequestBody still denies the call). Fix: `scope-filter.ts`
> extracts `filterOneFindToolsResult` (shared by single+batch) + new `findToolsCallIdKeys` /
> `scopeFilterFindToolsBatch` (id-matched, so a mixed batch's other-tool results are untouched);
> controller adds the batch branch. Tests: +4 (id-harvest, batch filter, mixed-batch-untouched,
> wildcard/empty/single short-circuit); full suite **204/204**; nest build clean. 2 accepted: find_tools
> absence bricking discovery (LOW â€” upstream always prepends it) + stale confidence note (COSMETIC).
> **âœ… LIVE SMOKE DONE (`D-PMCP-LAZY-LIVE-SMOKE` CLEARED).** Both gateways rebuilt (`PUBLIC_MCP_ENABLED=true`),
> minted a real SCOPED key (`read`+`domain:book`) via auth-service, drove an external-agent session
> against mcp-public-gateway :8219: (1) fresh `tools/list` â†’ **only find_tools**; (2) `find_tools` â†’
> exactly the 3 in-scope book reads (no out-of-scope leak); (3) re-`tools/list` â†’ **grew** to find_tools
> + the 3 activated; (4) `book_list` â†’ relayed through ai-gateway â†’ book-service, real books returned.
> **The live smoke caught a real bug my unit tests missed** (the recurring lesson): ai-gateway COLLAPSES
> a single-element batch REQUEST into a single OBJECT response, so the adversary's `[{find_tools}]` (the
> batch-bypass) returned an object my array-only `scopeFilterFindToolsBatch` skipped â†’ out-of-scope leak.
> Fixed (handle object-or-array, id-matched) + re-smoked: BATCH cross-domain intent â†’ leaked NONE; BATCH
> book intent â†’ 3 reads, no write-tier leak. Suite 205/205.

> **âœ… SHIPPED THIS SESSION â€” #32 LLM-call full logging (XL, provider-registry + usage-billing).** "Not every call is logged + input/output not stored." **RC was wrong (4th this session):** usage-billing's store ALREADY persists encrypted request+response payloads + `request_status` â€” the gaps were pure plumbing in provider-registry: (a) `usage_outbox` only for `status=="completed"` + relay hardcoded `request_status:"success"` â†’ failures unaudited; (b) `UsageOutbox` had no payload fields â†’ empty `{}` stored. **Fix** (user chose full build, payloads capped): migration adds `usage_outbox.request_status/request_payload/response_payload`; `FinalizeWithUsageOutbox` `RETURNING â€¦, input` + emits for EVERY terminal status (gate now `usage != nil`; cost 0 for failures so the cost SUM is unchanged â€” usage_logs is audit-only) with UTF-8-safe-truncated request+response payloads (cap env `LLM_USAGE_PAYLOAD_CAP_BYTES`=16384); relay carries status+payloads; usage-billing `parseUsageEvent` reads them â†’ existing `writeUsageLog` encrypts. `usageLogParams.Input/OutputPayload` widened `mapâ†’any` (map for /record, string for jobs). Forward-compatible stream. **VERIFY:** both services full go test/vet green (provider-registry DB-mock INSERT-12/SELECT-13 + wire-contract; usage-billing payload/status parse). Plan [docs/plans/2026-06-28-llm-call-full-logging.md](../plans/2026-06-28-llm-call-full-logging.md). **Deferred `D-32-FULL-LOGGING-LIVE-SMOKE`** (full-stack real+forced-fail job round-trip). Bug doc #32 â†’ `[x]`. **/review-impl ran â†’ 2 fixes:** HIGH â€” user-cancel goes through `Cancel` (not the worker finalize), so cancelled calls were still unaudited â†’ `Cancel` now emits the usage_outbox audit row (shared `insertUsageOutbox`); MED â€” stripped the plaintext payloads from the campaign_usage stream (`campaignFields`). +3 tests, full provider-registry suite green.

> **ًں”ژ INVESTIGATED (not bounded) â€” #19 planner "stuck on gemma despite my selection".** Full STATIC trace found NO defect: both paths are wired end-to-end â€” session pickerâ†’`patchSession`â†’gateway transparent proxyâ†’chat-service persist (`model_fields_set`)â†’`stream_service.py` read+inject as `model_ref`â†’glossary `toolPlan`; AND Settings `DefaultModelsCard`â†’`defaultModelsApi.set('planner')`â†’provider-registry `user_default_models`â†’`internalResolvePlannerModel` (reads `planner` default first, else best tool-calling chat â†’ gemma when none set). No hardcoded literal, no passthrough drop. All 3 symptoms unify into ONE runtime cause: the planner is an **in-flight MCP call** that captured its resolution at turn start and **stopping the turn doesn't abort it** (~39s, finishes on whatever it resolved). â†’ **Deferred `D-PLANNER-INFLIGHT-ABORT`** (gate 2 structural cross-service cancellation feature: chat-turn abort â†’ cancel in-flight ai-gateway MCP call â†’ provider call; gate 4 blocked on a live `model_ref` capture). **PARTIAL FIX SHIPPED** (/review-impl caught a static gap the trace missed): `glossary_plan` exposes `model_ref` to the LLM, and chat-service only injected the session pin when the model OMITTED it â€” a weak model filling `model_ref` overrode the user's session AND Settings planner. Fix: chat-service is now AUTHORITATIVE ([stream_service.py](../../services/chat-service/app/services/stream_service.py) ~705 â€” session pin always wins; else strip the model's guess so the Settings default applies); 3 tests + 35 existing green. In-flight-abort remains deferred.
>
> **âœ… SHIPPED THIS SESSION â€” #36 extraction LLM-call estimate undercount â†’ feed the planner REAL chapter sizes (M, translation-service).** **The Explore RC was WRONG** (verified by probe): `plan_kind_batches` correctly caps at 3 kinds/batch (30 kinds â†’ 10 batches, not "overloaded"), and the fallback already uses kind-derived `batches_per_chapter`. The REAL cause: both estimate call-sites fed the windowing-aware planner a HARDCODED `[{"text_length": 8000}] * N` â€” every chapter a fake 4000-token stand-in that NEVER windows, so the estimate collapsed to `chapters أ— batches` and was blind to real chapter size, while the executor windows large chapters (`windows أ— batches`). **Fix:** new best-effort `book_client.build_chapters_meta()` ([book_client.py](../../services/translation-service/app/book_client.py)) fetches real per-chapter `word_count_estimate` from the existing `GET /internal/books/{id}/chapters` (knowledge-service uses the same for its estimate) and feeds genuine `text_length`; both [extraction.py](../../services/translation-service/app/routers/extraction.py) (HTTP/FE) and [mcp/server.py](../../services/translation-service/app/mcp/server.py) (agent) now use it. Best-effort: unfetchable size â†’ legacy 8000 default; any fetch error â†’ all-default (never blocks job creation). **VERIFY:** 8 new tests (word-counts filter/paginate/best-effort-error/empty + build-meta real-sizes/all-default + planner size-sensitivity probe-as-test) + admission/book_client green; 3 modules byte-compile; client field reads verified against the live book-service handler. **Deferred:** `D-EXTRACT-ESTIMATE-SCHEMA-PERBATCH` (separate opposite-direction over-count: schema of ALL kinds added to every per-batch unit â€” inflates INPUT/cost, coupled into windowing, needs its own analysis), `D-EXTRACT-ESTIMATE-SIZES-LIVE-SMOKE`. Bug doc #36 â†’ `[x]`. **Lesson:** [[feedback_verify_explore_rootcause_before_fixing]] â€” caught a 3rd misdiagnosed Explore RC this session.
>
> **âœ… SHIPPED THIS SESSION â€” #9 Build-KG progress "1/100" â†’ server-computed real denominator (M, FS آ· knowledge-service + frontend).** The progress bar showed a meaningless "1/100": `items_total` came from the FE's OPTIONAL `StartJobRequest.items_total` (a placeholder), trusted verbatim. **Fix:** new shared `_count_scope_items()` ([extraction.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/extraction.py)) returns the real per-scope counts (chapters published-gated + pending chat turns + glossary entities), reused by the cost **estimate** AND both job **create** paths (`start` + `rebuild`) â€” so the stored denominator equals the work done and matches the preview. `StartJobRequest.items_total` is now **deprecated/ignored** (kept on the schema for back-compat, value discarded). Best-effort: a transient count failure stores NULL â†’ FE indeterminate bar, never blocks the job start; counts run OUTSIDE the tx (H1). Public `start`/`rebuild` inject `book_client`+`pending_repo`+`glossary_client` (so test `dependency_overrides` apply); the internal-dispatch callers fall back to the per-worker singletons. Bonus: a correct `items_total` also tightens the bug-#37 `estimated_llm_calls` budget. **FE:** i18n label أ—4 â†’ "{{processed}} / {{total}} items (chapters + chat + glossary)". **VERIFY:** lifecycle test + **2 new** (server-compute ignores a client `99999` â†’ stores `45+100+200=345`; best-effort NULL when glossary count raises) + 25 estimate/caller-pays tests green; 4 locale JSON valid, no test pins the old label. **Deferred `D-KG-ITEMS-TOTAL-LIVE-SMOKE`** (full-stack real extraction job). Bug doc #9 â†’ `[x]`.

> **âœ… SHIPPED THIS SESSION â€” #8 translation glossary truncation â†’ content-aware output budget (S, translation-service).** "Translation glossary usually fails" on large entities: the glossary-translate worker hard-capped `max_tokens=4096` per entity, blind to glossary size â†’ a long-description / many-attribute entity exceeded it â†’ the structured JSON was truncated mid-output â†’ `parse_translation_response` failed â†’ entity marked failed. **Fix:** new `entity_output_budget(attributes)` ([glossary_translate_prompt.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/glossary_translate_prompt.py)) mirrors the REAL payload â€” each attribute's actual `original_value` token count (CJK-aware via `estimate_tokens`) أ— a 3أ— CJKâ†’Latin expansion + per-key JSON overhead â€” replacing the flat 4096 in the worker ([glossary_translate_worker.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/glossary_translate_worker.py) ~206). Clamped to `[4096 floor, env GLOSSARY_TRANSLATE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS=32768 ceiling]`: the floor keeps the old default for small entities (no regression), the budget scales above it so a large entity is no longer cut off. Per-attribute **chunking across calls** for a pathologically huge single entity stays **#26**'s scope (the ceiling caps it for now). **VERIFY:** 21 translation tests pass â€” 5 new budget unit (floor, grows-past-floor, ceiling-clamp, None/missing-safe, CJK-expands-more-than-Latin) + 2 new worker-wiring (large entity â†’ max_tokens >4096; small â†’ ==4096 back-compat) + 14 existing green. **Deferred `D-GLOSSARY-TRANSLATE-BUDGET-LIVE-SMOKE`** (lm_studio + a real large-entity job consumed off RabbitMQ â€” single-service, disproportionate now; worker test proves the budget reaches the LLM call). Bug doc #8 â†’ `[x]`.

> **âœ… SHIPPED THIS SESSION â€” On-demand (lazy) MCP-tool loading is now the STANDARD for every agui chat surface (L, chat-service).** Root cause of the **empty/dead book-chat turn** (diagnosed from the user's lm_studio log `n_keep: 35791 >= n_ctx: 32000`): the **book/editor chat surfaces dumped the ENTIRE federated catalog â€” 145 tools â‰ˆ ~35k tokens â€” on every turn**, overflowing qwen2.5-7b's 32k context (Gemma-4 has 131k, so it worked â†’ the "model communication" red herring). Discovery mode (`find_tools` + per-pass `{core}âˆھ{active}` advertisement) already existed but was gated to the *universal* surface only (the `else: tool_defs = catalog` branch shipped everything to book/editor). **Fix (user-directed "do both A and B, make it standard"):** discovery is now the standard transport for ALL agui non-admin surfaces (fresh + resume paths), with a **per-surface HOT SET** seeded into the discovery active-set on pass 1 = the domain(s) the surface's injected SKILL names directly (so the skill works with zero find_tools hop); every other domain is lazy via `find_tools`. **Live-proven against the real 145-tool catalog:** universal 145â†’**7** (95%â†“, pure discovery), book-scoped 145â†’**51** (all 42 `glossary_*` + core + glossary frontend), editor 145â†’**52** (= book + `propose_edit`). All three now fit 32k. **Key files:** [tool_discovery.py](../../services/chat-service/app/services/tool_discovery.py) (`surface_hot_domains`/`hot_tool_names` â€” prefix-scoped, new-domain-services lazy-by-default; **`propose_edit` demoted from always-on core â†’ editor-frontend-only**, it's meaningless without an open chapter), [stream_service.py](../../services/chat-service/app/services/stream_service.py) (`discovery_eligible` replaces `universal_surface`; `discovery_seed_names` threaded fresh+resumeâ†’`_emit_chat_turn`â†’`_stream_with_tools`; gateway-down agui still advertises frontend tools), [glossary_skill.py](../../services/chat-service/app/services/glossary_skill.py) (hint: use `find_tools` for off-glossary asks). **VERIFY:** **437 chat-service tests pass** (1 pre-existing unrelated collection error `test_working_memory.py` = a mount-depth `parents[3]` artifact, NOT my code); 32 discovery tests incl. 9 new (hot-set scoping, lazy-tail, pass-1 advertisement); the previously-passing `test_book_context_advertises_glossary_edit_not_propose_edit` validated the propose_edit demotion. **Live smoke:** real catalog scoping (above) + chat-service boots healthy with the fix loaded. **Reverted** the prior session's wrong-premise SSE-keepalive change (it was uncommitted; based on the disproven cold-start theory). **Note:** this was a newly-discovered design defect, not one of the 40 numbered bugs.
>
> **ًں”ژ /review-impl ran (1 fix-now applied, 1 documented):** **FIXED** â€” the `discovery_seed_names` threading (`stream_response â†’ _emit_chat_turn â†’ _stream_with_tools`) was unguarded: every integration test used a 1-tool catalog so the seed was always âˆ…, indistinguishable from a dropped param. Added `test_book_surface_seeds_glossary_hot_set_and_lazy_tail` (multi-domain catalog â†’ asserts glossary seeded, book/translation lazy, both at the loop boundary + the first-pass payload). **Considered & REJECTED** â€” adding `active_tool_names` to the `find_tools` exclude: it would turn "you already have this tool" into an empty "no match" â†’ the agent falsely denies a capability. Current re-suggest behavior is strictly safer; left as-is.
>
> **â–¶ Deferred (this fix):**
> - ًںں، `D-CHAT-RESUME-SURFACE-SEED` â€” `resume_stream_response` seeds the editor-superset `{glossary}` hot set for EVERY resume (it can't know the original surface â€” sets `editor_context={"resumed": True}`). A *universal*-surface suspend (generic `confirm_action`) thus resumes with 42 glossary tools it never had on the fresh turn (~10k wasted tokens, harmless). **Conscious tradeoff** (confirm cards are overwhelmingly glossary, so seeding glossary HELPS the dominant "confirm kind â†’ propose attributes" follow-up). **Gate reason 2 (structural):** the precise fix stores the surface's hot domains in `chat_suspended_runs` and re-applies on resume â€” a schema/payload change. Low priority.
> - ًںں، `D-PROVIDER-STREAM-ERROR-SURFACE` â€” when a provider returns a 200 SSE that carries an `{"error": {...}}` frame (e.g. lm_studio context-overflow), `streamOpenAICompat` ([streamer.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/provider/streamer.go)) decodes only `choices`/`usage` â†’ the error is swallowed into a silent empty turn instead of surfaced. **Gate reason 4 (blocked):** I could NOT reproduce the wire format â€” lm_studio is currently loaded with a larger context, so an overflow `data:` error frame never appeared on the wire (the `[ERROR]` is server-side log only). Adding a speculative `error`-field parser would be unverified. **Trigger:** revisit when a provider error frame is actually captured on the wire (load a small-`n_ctx` model + overflow it), then parse + surface it. Low blast radius â€” the root-cause tool-dump fix already prevents the overflow.
> - ًںں، `D-CHAT-LAZY-LOAD-LIVE-SMOKE` â€” the unit+integration tests + the real-catalog scoping smoke cover the gating; a full **chat-UI** turn (open book â†’ glossary "Ask AI" on qwen2.5-7b â†’ confirm a non-empty answer with the small tool payload) wasn't driven (the overflow no longer reproduces since lm_studio has a big context now, so it's not a discriminating smoke). Optional: drive it with a small-`n_ctx` model to see the before/after directly.

> **â–¶ IN FLIGHT â€” Confirm-card server-coalesce (#27/#29/#30/#18), XL.** Plan: [docs/plans/2026-06-28-confirm-card-server-coalesce.md](../plans/2026-06-28-confirm-card-server-coalesce.md). Root cause VERIFIED: the chat run lifecycle honours ONE confirm card per turn (`chat_suspended_runs` single `pending_tool_call` PK run_id; tool loop `break`s on the first frontend confirm, dropping the tail; first confirm deletes the run row). User chose **full server coalesce**. **P1 SHIPPED (this session):** new deterministic `glossary_propose_batch` MCP tool (explicit ops â†’ `ValidatePlan` â†’ ONE `execute_plan` card â†’ existing deterministic executor, NO planner LLM) + skill hardened (one-card-per-turn HARD rule, no-loop prohibition, glossary_plan â‰¤1/turn). VERIFY: glossary `go build`+`vet` clean; **3 new propose-batch tests green against live test DB** (round-trip mintâ†’confirmâ†’2 kinds created via multi-op tier-ordered executor; unknown-op rejected; empty-ops rejected); 2 chat-service skill-prompt regression tests pass. **â–¶ P2 NEXT (load-bearing):** run-loop auto-coalesce of stray multi-card turns â€” chat-service accumulates every confirm_token minted in a turn; at the suspend point, if >1, bundle into ONE `glossary.batch` card; new glossary `POST /actions/confirm-batch` (+`/preview-batch`) loops the EXISTING per-descriptor effects, claims each jti, returns one aggregated summary. FE routes the batch descriptor. Then cross-service live smoke + mark #27/#29/#30/#18 `[x]`.

> **UX BUG TRACK** (`docs/bugs/2026-06-27-critical-ux-bugs.md`): fixing ~40 critical UX bugs. **All 40 triaged: 15 fixed `[x]`, 24 investigated `[I]` (RC+Fix written), 0 unknown.** Done this session: #34, #37, #35, #17, then the full glossary/KG INVESTIGATION (6 clusters, `14edaede`), then the **data-safety milestone**: **#14** KG-rebuild confirm-guard + AWS typed-confirm (`bf30eed3`), **#33** kind-descriptionâ†’extraction prompt (`73f87476`), **#31** entity-GET returns attribute options (`26012411`).

> **TRACK:** expose MCP to external/third-party agents over the internet with a NEW security model. Full doc set: [docs/specs/2026-06-26-public-mcp/](../specs/2026-06-26-public-mcp/) (README + 01 feature-catalog آ· 02 interface-matrix آ· 03 security-design+edge-cases+spikes آ· 04 plan آ· 05 tool-scope-map).
>
> **WHAT SHIPPED (P0 â€” the serial keystone):** new **`services/mcp-public-gateway`** (TS/NestJS) â€” the PUBLIC security edge. An external agent connects (via api-gateway-bff `/mcp`) with its OWN bearer credential; the edge authenticates it, **strips the entire inbound `x-*` namespace + mints a fresh envelope** (PUB-9 â€” a smuggled `X-Admin-Token`/`X-Internal-Token`/`X-User-Id` is discarded), and relays to **`ai-gateway/mcp` ONLY** (no `/mcp/admin` route, holds no admin token). Refuses a key in the query string (H-R). OFF unless `PUBLIC_MCP_ENABLED=true` (Q-GATE kill-switch). P0 uses a static test credential (`MCP_PUBLIC_TEST_KEY`â†’`MCP_PUBLIC_TEST_USER_ID`); read tools only, nothing priced/written.
> - **Wired:** BFF `/mcp` proxy ([gateway-setup.ts](../../services/api-gateway-bff/src/gateway-setup.ts) â€” unversioned, matched before /v1; optional+defaulted url so existing callers/tests unchanged) آ· docker-compose service `mcp-public-gateway` (:8219â†’8211, depends ai-gateway healthy) آ· `language-rule.yaml` row (typescript) آ· ai-gateway `X-Internal-Token` compare made **constant-time** ([util/auth.ts](../../services/ai-gateway/src/util/auth.ts), both /mcp + /mcp/admin).
> - **VERIFY:** mcp-public-gateway `nest build` clean + **8/8 unit tests** (auth gate, Q-GATE flag-off, H-R query-reject, PUB-9 strip/admin-isolation, 502 on upstream down, helpers); ai-gateway **55/55**; api-gateway-bff **115/115** (also fixed 2 PRE-EXISTING broken test fixtures missing learningUrl/compositionUrl). **âڈ³ NOT yet live-smoked** end-to-end on a stack-up (external clientâ†’BFFâ†’edgeâ†’ai-gatewayâ†’provider) â€” `D-PMCP-P0-LIVE-SMOKE`.
>
> **WHAT SHIPPED (P1 BACKEND â€” credential subsystem, run via /loom, size L):** the "new security setting" exists end-to-end on the BE.
> - **auth-service:** `mcp_api_keys` table (migration in [migrate.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/migrate/migrate.go) â€” Argon2id `key_hash`, `key_prefix` lookup, scopes, spend_cap, rate_limit_rpm, `allow_self_confirm`, status, expiry; CASCADE on user delete + active-prefix index). [mcp_keys.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/api/mcp_keys.go): `GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /v1/account/mcp-keys` (JWT owner-only; **secret shown once** on create; creation Q-GATE-flag-gated â†’ 403 when off) + `POST /internal/mcp-keys/resolve` (X-Internal-Token; prefix lookup â†’ constant-time Argon2id verify â†’ **account_status='active' check (H-L)** â†’ returns {user_id,key_id,scopes,allow_self_confirm,caps}; uniform 401 anti-oracle; **per-prefix attempt cap before Argon2 (H-H)**). Reuses `authpwd.Hash/Verify` (Argon2id).
> - **mcp-public-gateway edge:** `KeyResolver` swapped from the static key to a real `POST ${AUTH_SERVICE_URL}/internal/mcp-keys/resolve` call with a ~45s in-mem TTL cache (bounds the hot path + revocation lag); identity is auth-derived, never client-supplied. Dev static key retained as an optional smoke convenience.
> - **Wired:** compose â€” edge gets `AUTH_SERVICE_URL` + `depends_on auth-service healthy`; auth-service gets `PUBLIC_MCP_ENABLED` passthrough.
> - **VERIFY:** auth-service api suite green incl **3 real-PG tests** (createâ†’resolveâ†’tampered-rejectâ†’list-no-secretâ†’revoke; H-L deleted-accountâ†’401; flag-offâ†’403) against infra postgres:5555; edge **10/10** (incl real-key auth-resolve + PUB-9 strip); provider-gate OK; go build+vet clean. **live smoke: real-PG resolve flow.** âڈ³ full externalâ†’edgeâ†’authâ†’ai-gateway stack-up deferred â†’ `D-PMCP-P1-LIVE-SMOKE`.
>
> **WHAT SHIPPED (P1 FE â€” Settings â†’ MCP access tab, run via /loom continuous-flow + /review-impl):** the credential UX is live.
> - **FE:** new tab [McpAccessTab.tsx](../../frontend/src/features/settings/McpAccessTab.tsx) (list w/ status/scopes/last-used/expiry + revoke-confirm), [McpCreateKeyDialog.tsx](../../frontend/src/features/settings/McpCreateKeyDialog.tsx) (two-phase formâ†’**copy-once** secret reveal; secret lives only in dialog state, dropped on close â€” never localStorage), [useMcpKeys.ts](../../frontend/src/features/settings/useMcpKeys.ts) (controller hook), `mcpKeysApi`+types+`MCP_SCOPES` in [api.ts](../../frontend/src/features/settings/api.ts). Tab is **flag-gated** ([SettingsPage.tsx](../../frontend/src/pages/SettingsPage.tsx) â€” only shown when `public_mcp_enabled`; deep-link to `/settings/mcp` when off â†’ redirect). New keys default to **`read` scope only** (Wave-A safe). i18n أ—4.
> - **Flag carrier:** `GET /v1/account/profile` now returns `public_mcp_enabled` ([handlers.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/api/handlers.go) `getProfile` â€” read-only system flag, mirrors `cfg.PublicMcpEnabled`); FE reads it off the cached profile (fail-closed: undefined/fetch-fail â†’ tab hidden; backend 403s create regardless).
> - **VERIFY:** FE `tsc` clean آ· ESLint clean آ· **3 vitest** (copy-once reveal + secret-dropped-on-close + revoke-only-on-active); auth-service **6 real-PG tests** (the 5 credential + new `TestProfile_ReflectsPublicMcpFlag_PG` flag on/off) green on infra postgres:5555; go build+vet clean. **live smoke: real-PG profile-flag + credential resolve.**
> - **ًں”ژ /review-impl:** 1 LOW fixed (the profile-flag had zero test coverage â†’ added the PG test both ways); 4 LOW/COSMETIC accepted+documented (deep-link bounce until profile cached = fail-safe; FE doesn't pre-validate rate/spend bounds = backend is the gate; zero-scope submit = least-privilege; date-expiry is UTC-midnight). Secret-never-persists + no-unhandled-rejection + contract-alignment verified.

>
> **WHAT SHIPPED (kit pre-step â€” X-Mcp-Key-Id carrier + OD-8 primitive, run via /loom + /review-impl, size M):** the serial keystone the P2/P3 fanout consumes. **Carrier plumbing only â€” no enforcement yet.**
> - **Go kit** [identity.go](../../sdks/go/loreweave_mcp/identity.go): `HeaderMcpKeyID` const + lift in `IdentityMiddleware` + `McpKeyIDFromCtx()` + `OwnerOnlyFromCtx()` (OD-8, true iff a public-key call). **Python kit** [context.py](../../sdks/python/loreweave_mcp/context.py): `mcp_key_id` on `ToolContext` + lift in `build_tool_context` + `is_owner_only()` (duck-typed â†’ works on knowledge-service's separate richer ctx too). **knowledge-service** [executor.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/tools/executor.py)+[server.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/mcp/server.py): `mcp_key_id` on its own `ToolContext` + lift (it doesn't use the kit builder).

Plan: [2026-06-27-public-mcp-edge-scope-filter.md](../plans/2026-06-27-public-mcp-edge-scope-filter.md).

A tool is callable iff key holds its tier **and** `domain:<d>` for EVERY domain it touches. **auth-service untouched** â€” `scopes TEXT[]` already stores `domain:*` verbatim; the FE composes the array.
> - **Edge (`services/mcp-public-gateway`):** new [tool-policy.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/tool-policy.ts) â€” authoritative **allowlist** (~95 tools أ— {tier, domains}) from scope-map آ§2, with H-F cross-domain (`translation_start_extraction`â†’[translation,glossary]; `composition_generate`â†’[composition,glossary,knowledge]; `lore_enrichment_auto_enrich`â†’[lore_enrichment,glossary,knowledge]) + `jobs`/`settings` own explicit domain; admin/secret/`book_delete`/`purge` ABSENT â†’ denied. `isToolAllowed` is **default-deny** (unknownâ†’deny=H-E; `*` dev-key bypass). new [scope-filter.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/scope-filter.ts) â€” `gateRequestBody` (deny a disallowed `tools/call` before relay, fail-closed; whole-batch deny) + `filterListResponseText` (rewrite `tools/list` to in-scope tools only; **drift-log** any federated tool absent from the table; fail-closed empty list on unparseable JSON). [public-mcp.controller.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/mcp/public-mcp.controller.ts) wires both around the relay.

Now a public key reaches a book ONLY as its OWNER, never via an inherited collaboration grant.

> **WHAT SHIPPED (P3 slice 1 â€” edge per-key rate-limit / PUB-8, run via /loom + /review-impl + live-smoke, size L):** a leaked/abusive public key is bounded to its `rate_limit_rpm` via a Redis fixed-window counter, shared across edge replicas, enforced BEFORE any work.
> - **New:** [rate-limiter.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/ratelimit/rate-limiter.ts) â€” driver-free `RateLimiter` over a `RateLimitStore` iface; [redis-store.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/ratelimit/redis-store.ts) â€” ioredis adapter (INCRBY + EXPIRE NX in one multi), config-gated from `REDIS_URL` (unset â†’ limiter DISABLED/fail-open, logged once). **Wired** in [public-mcp.controller.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/mcp/public-mcp.controller.ts): resolve â†’ **rate-limit** â†’ scope-gate â†’ relay; 429 + `Retry-After` + JSON-RPC `-32029`. [scope-filter.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/scope-filter.ts): `isWriteRequest` (fail-policy classifier) + `countToolCalls` (batch weight). compose: `REDIS_URL` + `depends_on redis`.
> - **PUB-8 fail policy:** Redis outage â†’ **reads fail OPEN** (availability), **writes fail CLOSED** (safety). Counter weighted by tool-call count so a **batch can't smuggle N executions** past a per-request limit.
> - **ًں”ژ /review-impl: 1 MED fixed** (batch amplification â†’ weighted increment by `countToolCalls`); verified fail-policy ungameable (unknown "writes" fail-open but scope-gate denies them), bad keys don't consume counters, EXPIRE NX, atomic INCRBY, fast-fail on outage. Fixed-window 2أ— boundary burst = accepted v1 tradeoff.
> - **VERIFY:** edge **56/56** (limiter logic both fail directions, batch weight, isWriteRequest, countToolCalls, controller 429+Retry-After) + build + provider-gate clean. **Live-smoke:** rebuilt edge w/ `REDIS_URL`; key `rpm=3` â†’ calls 1-3 `200`, calls 4-6 `429 / -32029 / retry_after=56 / limit=3` (per-key Redis counter, end-to-end). Single-service (edge only).

> - **A2 auth handlers** ([mcp_approvals.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/api/mcp_approvals.go)): `POST /internal/mcp-keys/approvals` (edge divert ingest, X-Internal-Token), `GET /v1/account/mcp-keys/approvals?status=` (owner-scoped, lazy-expire), `â€¦/{id}/approve` (replays the token to `{domain}/v1/<domain>/actions/confirm` with **X-Internal-Token + X-User-Id=owner + X-Mcp-Key-Id=key_id + X-Mcp-Spend-Cap-Usd** â€” cost attributes to the AGENT's key; 2xxâ†’executed, 409â†’reprice surfaced, 410â†’expired, elseâ†’failed), `â€¦/{id}/deny` (drops token). New domainâ†’URL config map ([config.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/config/config.go)) + best-effort HTTP notification (no AMQP â€” notification-service `/internal/notifications/` ingest, new `mcp_approval` category). NO JWT-mint needed (confirm routes are internal-token+X-User-Id gated).
> - **A3 edge divert** ([propose-detect.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/propose-detect.ts) pure + [approval-client.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/approval/approval-client.ts) + [public-mcp.controller.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/mcp/public-mcp.controller.ts)): a single `write_confirm` propose by a default key â†’ detect `confirm_token` in the relayed response â†’ POST the approval (token-stripped preview) â†’ return the agent only `{status:"pending_human_approval", approval_id}`. **Fail-closed** (queue unreachable â†’ `isError`, never leak the token). A self-confirm key keeps the token (slice B path).
> - **A4 carrier-lift** (A2 money path): `apply_public_key_attribution_headers` kit helper ([context.py](../../sdks/python/loreweave_mcp/context.py)) set/finally-clear around composition's IN-PROCESS confirm effect ([actions.py](../../services/composition-service/app/routers/actions.py)) â†’ `composition.generate`'s in-process `submit_job` tags `job_meta` with the key (the confirm route is not an MCP tool call, so the universal hook never fired there).
> - **A5 FE** ([McpApprovalsPanel.tsx](../../frontend/src/features/settings/McpApprovalsPanel.tsx) + [useMcpApprovals.ts](../../frontend/src/features/settings/useMcpApprovals.ts) + api): a polling pending-approvals panel in the MCP Access tab (Approve/Deny); i18n أ—4.
> - **ًں”ژ /review-impl: NO HIGH** (verified the gate's core assumption â€” every `write_confirm` tool is propose-pattern, confirmed book `toolPropose*` + composition; the `undoResult` is the confirm-EFFECT's return, not the propose tool). 6 LOW accept/defer: batch write_confirm not diverted (token inert â€” `D-PMCP-BATCH-WCONFIRM-DIVERT`); `detectProposeResult` + composition wiring not asserted vs the real wire shape (â†’ raises `D-PMCP-CARRIER-E2E-LIVE-SMOKE` importance); revoked-key approval still executes + 16-min sync approve (idempotent via status latch) documented.
> - **VERIFY:** edge **79/79** (3 divert + 8 propose-detect), kit+attribution **56**, auth full api suite green on **real PG**, FE settings **27/27** (+2 panel), composition `py_compile`, provider-gate clean. **live smoke: approve state-machine + X-Mcp-Key-Id/cap=9/X-User-Id/internal-token attribution replay to the domain confirm proven on real Postgres** (stub domain); cross-ownerâ†’404/0-items; token never echoed; expiredâ†’410. Full `composition_generate`â†’`usage_logs.mcp_key_id` LLM-spend e2e deferred â†’ `D-PMCP-CARRIER-E2E-LIVE-SMOKE`.

> **WHAT SHIPPED (P4 slice B â€” confirm_action + allow_self_confirm, run via /loom + /review-impl, size M):** the OPT-IN headless path â€” a key with BOTH `write_confirm` and `allow_self_confirm` executes a Tier-W action itself (no human queue). PO chose **B-via-auth** (edge gates + reuses slice-A replay).
> - **KEY FINDING:** `confirm_action` was only a chat-service FRONTEND tool ([frontend_tools.py](../../services/chat-service/app/services/frontend_tools.py)) with NO server executor â€” the plan آ§4b-B ("ai-gateway decodes + routes it") was wrong. So B BUILDS the headless executor by reusing slice A's domain-replay.
> - **Auth** ([mcp_approvals.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/api/mcp_approvals.go)): new `POST /internal/mcp-keys/confirm` (X-Internal-Token) replays the token to `{domain}/v1/<domain>/actions/confirm` with `X-Mcp-Key-Id`+cap via the slice-A `replayConfirm`+`lookupKeySpendCap` (NO approval row). Refactored the approve/self-confirm response shaping into shared `confirmReplayLabel`/`writeConfirmReplayResult`.
> - **Edge** ([confirm-action.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/confirm-action.ts) pure + `selfConfirm` on [approval-client.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/approval/approval-client.ts) + [public-mcp.controller.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/mcp/public-mcp.controller.ts)): `confirm_action` is a SYNTHETIC edge tool (not federated / not in TOOL_POLICY) â†’ intercepted BEFORE the scope gate. Gate = `write_confirm` **AND** `allow_self_confirm` **AND** `domain:<domain>` (else anti-oracle `-32601`); forwards `{confirm_token, domain}` to auth self-confirm; injects `confirm_action` into `tools/list` for dual-flag keys.

(2) `confirm_action` is classified a WRITE in `isWriteRequest` so it fails CLOSED on a rate-limit store outage (it triggers a spend). LOW documented: self-confirm trusts the internal-token edge-supplied key/owner pair; 16-min sync confirm is idempotent via the single-use token.

Edge [tool-policy.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/tool-policy.ts): both `write_auto`/`jobs`. So a public agent can abort its OWN runaway job, never another owner's. 18 jobs MCP tests + 8 edge policy tests.

> - âœ… `D-PMCP-WEBSEARCH-BYOK` RESOLVED (verified exposable â€” NO code change needed). **Correction (2026-06-28, per PO): `web_search` is a TOOL, not an LLM/model** â€” the `user_models` row with `capability_flags:{web_search:true}` is a **tool credential** (the test user's is `searxng`, self-hosted/free; Tavily/Brave etc. would be the user's OWN paid API). So the LLM-spend lens (meter tokens â†’ `usage_logs` â†’ per-key cap, "pricing decision") was the WRONG model and does NOT apply â€” there is no token spend to meter/cap/attribute. The correct controls for a tool are already in place + proven: (1) **BYOK / no platform draw** â€” `internalWebSearch` ([server.go:2778](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/api/server.go#L2778)) resolves the *caller's own* explicitly-flagged `web_search` credential, 404 `WEBSEARCH_MODEL_NOT_FOUND` if none, no fallback. **Live-proven:** test user (searxng) â†’ `HTTP 200, provider_model=searxng-default, 3 results`; a user with no cred â†’ `HTTP 404` (no platform draw). (2) **scope-gate** â€” `glossary_web_search` advertised `{tier:'paid_read', domains:['glossary']}` ([tool-policy.ts:105](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/tool-policy.ts#L105)); `paid_read` is a selectable tier in `MCP_SCOPES` (FE create dialog), so a user can mint a `paid_read`+`domain:glossary` key. (3) **per-key rate-limit (PUB-8) + audit (H-O)** already live-proven edge-wide. `paid_read` = "may hit the user's OWN external provider", bounded by the rate-limit (not a token cap). **Optional FUTURE enhancement (NOT a blocker):** if the platform ever wants per-call accounting of paid web-search tools, add tool-call metering â€” but that's a separate product decision, and Wave-B `paid_read` does NOT need it.
> - âœ… `D-TRANSL-REPRICE-THRESHOLD-DRIFT` RESOLVED (P4-C): verified CLEAN â€” no FE file hardcodes the `1.25`/`0.50` re-price threshold; the FE purely reacts to the BE-set 409 `reprice_required` (threshold stays BE-owned). The first-party confirm card + the headless `useMcpApprovals` both just surface the BE signal.
> - âœ… `D-PMCP-BATCH-WCONFIRM-DIVERT` RESOLVED (P4-B target reached) â€” the edge now diverts a `write_confirm` propose that rides inside a JSON-RPC **batch** (not just a single message). For a DEFAULT key (`allow_self_confirm=false`), `divertBatchProposes` ([public-mcp.controller.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/mcp/public-mcp.controller.ts)) walks the relayed batch response, and for each item whose id matches a `write_confirm` `tools/call` request step (`writeConfirmCallsById`, [scope-filter.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/scope-filter.ts)) AND whose result carries a routable propose (`detectProposeInItem`, [propose-detect.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/propose-detect.ts)) it creates an approval row + replaces the item with a token-stripped `pending_human_approval` (fail-closed per item â†’ `isError`, never the token). It sets `text` and **falls through** to the existing list-filter + slice-F annotate (so a mixed `[tools/list, propose]` batch still scope-filters the list â€” the regression the /review-impl pass caught). Self-confirm keys are untouched (tokens flow to slice-B `confirm_action`). **VERIFY:** edge **134/134** (+9: per-item detect/builders/`writeConfirmCallsById` units + batch-divert/fail-closed/self-confirm/mixed-list controller integration), `nest build` clean, provider-gate OK. Single-service edge logic, unit-proven as the P4-A/B divert paths were.

> - âœ… **SLICE 1 SHIPPED â€” discovery + edge audience-verify (S9 foundation).** **auth-service:** `authjwt.SignOAuthAccessToken` ([oauth.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/authjwt/oauth.go)) reuses the admin RS256 `DigestSigner` via a generalized `assembleRS256(claims any)` but with a DISTINCT issuer (`OAUTH_ISSUER`, must â‰  admin `loreweave-auth`) + a resource audience; `GET /oauth/jwks` (RFC 7517 from `signer.PublicKey()`) + `GET /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server` (RFC 8414, PKCE S256 only) ([oauth_meta.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/api/oauth_meta.go)); `EnableOAuth` wired in [main.go](../../services/auth-service/cmd/auth-service/main.go) (same signer as admin) â€” on iff admin-issuance AND `PUBLIC_MCP_ENABLED`; config fail-closed on missing resource ([config.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/config/config.go)). **edge:** [token-verifier.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/oauth/token-verifier.ts) â€” LOCAL RS256 verify (Node crypto, zero deps; JWKS kid-cache + refresh-on-miss for rotation) pinning `iss`==OAUTH_ISSUER + `aud`==`MCP_RESOURCE_URL` (RFC 8707 â€” **rejects audience confusion, S9**) + exp; alg-confusion-safe (rejects non-RS256). [key-resolver.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/auth/key-resolver.ts) branches JWTâ†’local-verify (`keyId`=grant_id, a UUID â†’ uniform with API keys for attribution/audit/rate-limit), `lw_pk_`â†’existing resolve; OAuth `allow_self_confirm=false`, `spend_cap=null`. RFC 9728 PRM ([oauth-discovery.controller.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/oauth/oauth-discovery.controller.ts) + pure [discovery.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/oauth/discovery.ts)) + `WWW-Authenticate` on the 401. **BFF:** routes `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource`â†’edge, `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server`+`/oauth/*`â†’auth ([gateway-setup.ts](../../services/api-gateway-bff/src/gateway-setup.ts)). **VERIFY:** auth authjwt+api+config tests green (incl. OAuth-tokenâ†”admin-token cross-rejection both ways) + `go vet`; edge **155/155** (token-verifier 17: aud/iss/exp/sig/alg/rotation/wildcard-strip + discovery + 4 controller incl. wrong-audâ†’401-no-relay + OAuth relay on-behalf-of); BFF build + **115**; provider-gate OK. **LIVE-SMOKE â†’ `D-PMCP-P5-S1-LIVE-SMOKE`** (deferred to slice 2's e2e â€” the real edgeâ†”auth `/oauth/jwks`+aud-verify needs a token-mint endpoint, which lands in slice 2; slice-1 surface is unit-complete).
> - âœ… **SLICE 2a SHIPPED (backend auth-code + PKCE flow).** **migration** (3 tables, [migrate.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/migrate/migrate.go)): `mcp_oauth_clients` (public PKCE clients, `token_endpoint_auth_method='none'`), `mcp_oauth_grants` (per ownerأ—client, `id`=grant_id that rides `x-mcp-key-id`, downscoped `scopes`, rotating `refresh_token_hash`, `UNIQUE(owner,client)`), `mcp_oauth_codes` (single-use auth codes, stored HASHED, PKCE-bound, short TTL). **handlers** ([oauth_flow.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/api/oauth_flow.go)): `GET /oauth/authorize` (validates client+redirect+S256+resource+scope; **anti-open-redirect** â€” client/redirect errors shown directly, never redirect to an unvalidated URI; else redirects a logged-in user to the FE consent page), `POST /v1/account/oauth/consent` (Bearer JWT owner â€” approves a **downscoped** grant: grantedâٹ†requested + vocab-checked â†’ upserts grant + mints single-use code), `POST /oauth/token` (auth-code: atomic single-use consume `UPDATEâ€¦WHERE consumed_at IS NULL AND expires_at>now() RETURNING` + PKCE S256 verify â†’ access+refresh; refresh-token: rotate). Pure helpers ([oauth_pkce.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/api/oauth_pkce.go)): `pkceVerifyS256` (constant-time), `redirectURIRegistered` (exact match), `splitScopeParam`/`scopesAllKnown`/`scopesSubset`. `POST /internal/oauth/clients` (internal-token seed; slice 3 wraps it as public RFC 7591 DCR). Access token minted via slice-1 `SignOAuthAccessToken` (aud = the request's resource). **VERIFY:** auth build+vet+provider-gate clean; pure-helper units (PKCE/redirect/scope) + **PG-gated FULL-FLOW test PROVEN on real Postgres** (infra:5555 throwaway DB): registerâ†’consent(downscope read+domain:book from a wider req)â†’codeâ†’tokenâ†’access+refresh, single-use code reuseâ†’400, refresh rotation + old-refreshâ†’400, bad-PKCEâ†’400. This clears the **backend leg** of `D-PMCP-P5-S1-LIVE-SMOKE`.
> - âœ… **SLICE 2b SHIPPED (FE scope-aware consent page â€” per-scope downscoping).** New MVC `frontend/src/features/oauth/` â€” [api.ts](../../frontend/src/features/oauth/api.ts) (`oauthApi.consent` â†’ `POST /v1/account/oauth/consent`, `ConsentParams`/`ConsentRequest`), [hooks/useOAuthConsent.ts](../../frontend/src/features/oauth/hooks/useOAuthConsent.ts) (controller: reads the validated authorize params off the query string, owns per-scope grant toggles starting from the full request, approve composes the **downscoped** `granted_scopes` + the full `requested_scopes` then follows the returned client `redirect_uri`; deny bounces to the client with `error=access_denied`; not-logged-in â†’ `/login` preserving `pathname+search` so the query survives the round-trip â€” RequireAuth would drop it), [components/OAuthConsentView.tsx](../../frontend/src/features/oauth/components/OAuthConsentView.tsx) (render-only; tier+domain chips reuse `splitScopes`/`domainScope`). Page [OAuthConsentPage.tsx](../../frontend/src/pages/OAuthConsentPage.tsx) + route `/oauth/consent` ([App.tsx](../../frontend/src/App.tsx), in FullBleedLayout, NOT RequireAuth). i18n `oauth` ns أ—4 ([en](../../frontend/src/i18n/locales/en/oauth.json)/vi/ja/zh-TW) registered in [i18n/index.ts](../../frontend/src/i18n/index.ts). **Backend tweak:** `oauthAuthorize` now forwards `client_name` to the consent URL (display-only â€” the FE otherwise only has the opaque `client_id`). **ًں”ژ /review-impl: 1 MED fixed + 1 LOW fixed.** **MED** â€” the deny path built the redirect **client-side from the raw query string** (`new URL(params.redirectUri)`) with no backend validation â†’ an **open redirect** on a direct link with an attacker-supplied unregistered `redirect_uri` (victim clicks Deny â†’ bounced to evil.com; no token leak but phishing-grade, violates the anti-open-redirect invariant the approve path upholds). **Fixed:** deny now POSTs `action:"deny"` â†’ `oauthConsent` validates the redirect_uri is registered for the client BEFORE bouncing (unregistered â†’ 400, no redirect). LOW â€” `needsLogin` button was mislabeled `consent.allow` â†’ added `consent.sign_in`. Verified safe: no XSS (React escapes the i18next-interpolated text node despite `escapeValue:false`), no scope-widening (UI renders only requested chips + backend enforces subset), approve open-redirect impossible (backend 400s an unregistered URI). **VERIFY:** FE `tsc` clean; **oauth consent 5/5 vitest** (all-scopes-prechecked, downscope omits untoggled from `granted_scopes` w/ `requested_scopes` intact + follows redirect, all-offâ†’blocked+`no_scopes`, **deny routes through backend** w/ `action:deny`, missing-PKCEâ†’`invalid_request`); auth-service api unit suite green + build+vet; **PG-gated deny test** (`TestOAuthConsentDeny_PG`: registeredâ†’access_denied bounce no code, **unregistered redirectâ†’400**) + full-flow test re-proven on real Postgres. **Deploy note:** `OAUTH_CONSENT_URL` must point at the FE origin's `/oauth/consent`; the public host serves `/oauth/consent` from the SPA while routing the OAuth **API** endpoints (`/oauth/authorize|token|jwks`, `/.well-known/oauth-*`) to the gateway (FE nginx/vite proxy only `/v1`,`/ws`,`/languagetool` today â†’ topology-dependent, see deferral).
> - âœ… **SLICE 3 SHIPPED (open Dynamic Client Registration, RFC 7591).** **auth-service only.** New public `POST /oauth/register` ([oauth_register.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/api/oauth_register.go)) â€” a third-party MCP client self-registers a PUBLIC PKCE client (no secret) â†’ completes the fully self-served `registerâ†’authorizeâ†’consentâ†’tokenâ†’edge-read`. Reuses `insertOAuthClient` (stores `created_ip`). **Three locked controls (PO "open DCR behind flag+rate-limit+audit"):** (1) **kill-switch** `OAUTH_DCR_ENABLED` (default ON, [config.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/config/config.go)) â†’ `403 registration_disabled`; (2) **per-IP rate-limit** (`OAUTH_DCR_RATE_PER_HOUR` default 10, dedicated `ratelimit.Limiter` on `oauthDeps`) checked **before any parse/DB write** â†’ `429 rate_limited`; (3) **append-only audit** â€” new `mcp_oauth_client_registrations` table ([migrate.go](../../services/auth-service/internal/migrate/migrate.go), REVOKE UPDATE/DELETE) records every attempt (issued `registered` / `rejected`+reason; `client_id` NULL on reject). Validates redirect_uris (absolute, no fragment, http(s)-needs-host, custom native schemes ok), public-PKCE-only (`token_endpoint_auth_method=none`), grant/response types âٹ† supported, scope vocab. Spec-correct RFC `{error,error_description}` body via `writeOAuthErr`. `registration_endpoint` already in AS metadata (slice 1). **ًں”ژ /review-impl: 1 MED + 2 LOW fixed.** **MED** â€” no input-size caps on the public endpoint â†’ added 16 KiB `MaxBytesReader` + `redirect_uris â‰¤ 10` + `client_name â‰¤ 256` (resource-exhaustion guard). **LOW** â€” AS metadata advertised `registration_endpoint` even when DCR disabled â†’ now conditional on `dcrEnabled` (RFC 8414). **LOW** â€” documented `OAUTH_DCR_RATE_PER_HOUR=0` = UNLIMITED (disable via the flag). Verified safe: open redirect impossible (code is PKCE-bound + consent-gated), audit can't be flooded (rate-gate precedes the write), no secret ever issued. **VERIFY:** `go build`/`vet` clean; `TestValidRegistrationRedirectURI` + `TestOAuthASMetadata_OmitsRegistrationWhenDCRDisabled` + **PG tests** (`TestOAuthDCR_PG`: 201+client_id+no-secret+audit-row, bad-redirectâ†’400+rejected-audit, emptyâ†’400, >10 redirectsâ†’400, long nameâ†’400; `TestOAuthDCR_FlagOff_PG`â†’403; `TestOAuthDCR_RateLimit_PG`: 2 okâ†’429) green on real Postgres; full auth suite green; provider-gate OK. Full self-served browser round-trip folds into `D-PMCP-P5-S1-LIVE-SMOKE`.
> - âœ… **SLICE 4 SHIPPED (catalog_* OD-7 public-discovery read provider) â€” P5 COMPLETE.** **catalog-service:** new MCP server ([mcp_server.go](../../services/catalog-service/internal/api/mcp_server.go)) + 2 read tools ([mcp_tools.go](../../services/catalog-service/internal/api/mcp_tools.go)) â€” `catalog_list_public_books` (query/language/genre/author filters + sort) + `catalog_get_book`. Both delegate to a shared query core (`queryPublicBooks`/`queryPublicBook`, extracted from the HTTP handlers in [server.go](../../services/catalog-service/internal/api/server.go) so MCP + HTTP stay one source of truth). **OD-7:** catalog content is PUBLIC (sharing public gate + lifecycle filter), so the tools are owner-AGNOSTIC â€” `_meta.scope=none` (no kit guard), TierR; NO OD-8 owner gate (no private data). Mounted `/mcp`+`/mcp/*` via the kit `NewStatelessHandler` (X-Internal-Token gate); go.mod gets the kit + grantclient/llmgw replaces; Dockerfile copies the 3 new sdk dirs. **ai-gateway:** `catalog: 'catalog_'` in [DEFAULT_PREFIX_MAP](../../services/ai-gateway/src/config/config.ts) + `catalog=â€¦/mcp` in compose `AI_GATEWAY_PROVIDERS`. **edge:** [tool-policy.ts](../../services/mcp-public-gateway/src/scope/tool-policy.ts) classifies both `catalog_*` = `{tier:'read', domains:['catalog']}` (the `'catalog'` Domain + OAuth `domain:catalog` scope already existed from slice 1). **FE:** `catalog` added to `MCP_DOMAINS` ([api.ts](../../frontend/src/features/settings/api.ts)) + `mcp.domain.catalog` label أ—4 locales. **VERIFY:** catalog `go build`/`vet` + api tests green (incl. new MCP tool tests: public-active-only, owner-agnostic via bare ctx, bad-author/uuid, non-publicâ†’not-found; existing HTTP tests pass = refactor preserved behavior); edge **155/155** + tsc; ai-gateway **63/63** + tsc; FE tsc clean (settings/oauth i18n parity holds; only the pre-existing `world` parity fails). provider-gate OK. **âœ… LIVE-SMOKE (â‰¥2 services â€” rebuilt catalog-service + ai-gateway, restarted):** ai-gateway federation log shows `federating [...catalog]` + no prefix-drop warning; `tools/list` (200) returns BOTH `catalog_list_public_books` + `catalog_get_book` among 147 federated tools; `tools/call catalog_list_public_books` â†’ 200, `isError:false`, **31 public books across MULTIPLE owner_user_ids** (proves OD-7 owner-agnostic discovery â€” called as the test user, returned other users' public books); `tools/call catalog_get_book` â†’ 200, `isError:false`, `visibility:"public"` detail. Edge policy layer is unit-proven (edge not up in this smoke). **ًں”ژ /review-impl: NO HIGH/MED** â€” verified no private-data leak (sharing public gate on both tools), anti-oracle uniform not-found on get, refactor preserves HTTP behavior; 1 LOW closed (added the get live-smoke), 1 LOW accepted (construction test asserts valid meta, not specifically tier=R/scope=none â€” edge tool-policy is the real gate).

> - `D-PMCP-OAUTH-PUBLIC-ROUTING` (LOW, gate-reason 4 blocked-on-topology) â€” if the prod public host is the FE nginx (single front), it must proxy the OAuth **API** endpoints (`/oauth/authorize|token|jwks`, `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server`, `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource`) to the gateway/edge while keeping `/oauth/consent` on the SPA. > - `D-AUTH-RATELIMIT-XFF-AND-UNBOUNDED-MAP` (LOW, gate-reason 1+2 platform-wide+pre-existing) â€” `ratelimit.ClientIP` trusts the first `X-Forwarded-For` hop (spoofable â†’ evades the per-IP cap), and the in-process `Limiter.byKey` map never evicts (each distinct/spoofed IP = a permanent entry â†’ slow memory growth). Shared by ALL auth rate-limits (login/register/reset); the DCR endpoint (slice 3) adds an entry point but doesn't introduce the weakness. Compensating: DCR is inert without consent + audited. Platform fix: the gateway should overwrite XFF to the true peer + add a TTL sweep/LRU to the limiter. Surfaced by slice-3 /review-impl.
> - `D-PMCP-CONSENT-CLIENT-LEGIBILITY` (LOW, gate-reason 1 slice-2b follow-up) â€” now that open DCR lets anyone register an arbitrary `client_name`, the consent page ([OAuthConsentView.tsx](../../frontend/src/features/oauth/components/OAuthConsentView.tsx)) should also show the **redirect_uri host** (and a "this app is not verified" hint) so a user can judge an unverified client before approving. Small FE addition; surfaced by slice-3 /review-impl.
> - `D-WORLD-I18N-PARITY` (LOW, gate-reason 1 out-of-scope, **pre-existing** â€” predates this slice, touches no OAuth file) â€” `frontend/src/i18n/locales/{vi,ja,zh-TW}/world.json` are missing the `graph.*`/`timeline.*`/`populate.addFailed` leaf keys present in `en/world.json` (added during the C21 world-feature work, never translated) â†’ `compositionWorldParity.test.ts` "world" suite red (9 failing assertions across 3 locales). ~30-50 short UI strings to translate; belongs to the world feature, not OAuth. Fix in its own scoped commit.

# (composition branch â€” historical handoff below) â€” **Glossary plan-and-execute (Phase 1) SHIPPED** آ· branch `feat/composition-service` آ· HEAD `b2439fcf` آ· 2026-06-26

> **WHAT SHIPPED:** the glossary assistant can now do **multi-step ontology goals in ONE confirm** â€” the agent calls `glossary_plan(book_id, goal)`, a capable model emits a **typed plan**, and a **deterministic executor** applies it (no ReAct loop). Validated against the literature (typed-plan â‰« ReAct; control-flow-integrity against prompt injection). Built as **reusable SDK kits**, not ad-hoc â€” see [[project_agentic_sdk_lives_in_loreweave_mcp_kit]]: this project will run many agents, so the pattern lives in `sdks/`.
>
> **Specs/plan:** architecture [2026-06-25-glossary-assistant-planner.md](../specs/2026-06-25-glossary-assistant-planner.md) آ· kit mechanism [2026-06-25-plan-action-kit.md](../specs/2026-06-25-plan-action-kit.md) (Part I envelope/executor/error-classes, Part II concrete Go types + glossary op-set + caps + resolved-questions ledger) آ· multi-agent execution plan [2026-06-25-plan-action-kit-phase1.md](../plans/2026-06-25-plan-action-kit-phase1.md).
>
> **The two new SDK kits (`sdks/go/`):**
> - **`loreweave_mcp`** gained the **plan-action kit** â€” `plan.go` (frozen K0 contract: `Plan`/`Op`/`OpOutcome`/`Summary`, sentinel errors, `OpSpec`/`Registry`, `NewRegistry` panics on non-idempotent ops, `ValidatePlan` does dedupe + duplicate-conflict + frozen op-N ids + `MaxPlanOps=50` cap + reject-empty) and `execute.go` (`Execute(ctx,userID,plan,enabledOps,reg) Summary` â€” tier-stable-sort, per-op `errors.Is`â†’outcome mapping, destructive-skip via `enabledOps`, internal-error abort). Constants: `PlanVersion=1`, `DescriptorExecutePlan="execute_plan"`, `PlanTokenTTL=30m`.
> - **`loreweave_llm` â€” NEW Go LLM SDK (the missing piece; user-chosen this session).** Sync streaming client over the provider-registry LLM gateway. `client.go` (`Complete()` accumulates SSE token deltas; reasoning kept separate; internal `/internal/llm/stream?user_id=`+X-Internal-Token OR jwt `/v1/llm/stream`+Bearer), `models.go` (`StreamRequest`, `ReasoningEffort` incl. `none` to disable hidden thinking, `Result{Text,Reasoning,Usage,FinishReason}`), `errors.go` (code-matched sentinels), `sse.go` (8MB-buffer scanner). Zero deps.
>
> **Glossary wiring:** `planRegistry()` ([plan_ops.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_ops.go)) registers **4 additive ops** â€” adopt_genres(t0), create_kinds(t1), add_attributes(t2), edit_attribute(t4); `glossary_plan` MCP tool ([action_plan_tools.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/action_plan_tools.go)) resolves modelâ†’reads ontologyâ†’runs planner (120s + 1 repair round, loose-emitâ†’validateâ†’repair)â†’mints a confirm card; confirm/execute path ([plan_confirm.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_confirm.go) `effectExecutePlan` re-validates then `mcp.Execute`). chat-service `glossary_skill.py` routes multi-step goals to `glossary_plan` (K=2 stop).
>
> **MED-6 (planner auto-resolve) DONE + live-smoked:** provider-registry `GET /internal/planner-model?user_id=` ([default_models_handler.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/api/default_models_handler.go) `internalResolvePlannerModel`) â†’ explicit `planner` default â†’ else best active chat model preferring `tool_calling` â†’ 404 `PLANNER_MODEL_NONE`. Glossary `resolvePlannerModel` calls it; `glossary_plan` works with **zero manual model_ref**. Smoke: endpoint returned `019ebb72â€¦` (Gemma-4 26B, tool_calling) via `source:chat_fallback`.
>
> **VERIFY/smokes:** all 3 Go modules `build`+`vet` green; kit + SDK unit tests pass; provider-gate OK. **âœ… SDK live-smoke** (loreweave_llm â†’ real provider-registry â†’ LM Studio Qwen2.5-7B â†’ valid plan JSON, `in=74 out=48 finish=stop`). **âœ… MED-6 endpoint live-smoke** (real PG). **/review-impl** ran: HIGH-1 (edit_attribute base_version guard dead â†’ dropped from planner vocab + fail-closed) + MED-6 fixed.
> - **âڑ ï¸ڈ NOT yet smoked: the full chat-UI flow** (open book â†’ "design an ontology" â†’ plan card â†’ confirm â†’ kinds created in real DB). The executor-against-real-DB layer is the one unproven leg â€” `D-PLAN-EXEC-LIVE-SMOKE`. The stack is up + code deployed, so this is a user-run browser smoke away.
>
> **Commits (all pushed to `origin/feat/composition-service`):** `4a8f3123` foundation آ· `ae9f755b` recovery (a concurrent history-rewrite dropped the loreweave_llm SDK + G2 edits; recovered + pushed to protect) آ· `820d572e` review fixes آ· `c225f75a` Dockerfile SDK COPY آ· `658a0caa` MED-6.
>
> **âڑ ï¸ڈ Shared-tree hazard (still live):** another developer commits LOOM `composition/*` work into this same branch concurrently â€” one of their commits swept my files in, and a history-rewrite dropped them. **Always stage only the exact files you changed (never `git add -A`); verify the COMMITTED file list, not just staged** ([[feedback_verify_committed_file_list_not_just_staged]]). NEVER commit `composition/*` files.
>
> **âœ… PHASE 2 SLICE 1 SHIPPED (this session) â€” destructive ontology deletes + per-op enable toggle.** Plan [2026-06-25-plan-action-phase2-destructive.md](../plans/2026-06-25-plan-action-phase2-destructive.md). The planner can now emit **`delete_kind` / `delete_genre`** (tier 5, Destructive), and the confirm card renders each destructive op as an **opt-in checkbox (default OFF)** â€” a bare "approve" never deletes (G1).
> - **Ops** ([plan_ops.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_ops.go)): 3 delete OpSpecs reuse the EXISTING soft-deprecate cascade primitives (`cascadeDeleteBookKind/Genre`, `softDeleteBookAttribute`); **deprecation-aware resolvers** split idempotency â€” never-existedâ†’`target_gone` (failed), already-deprecatedâ†’`already_done` (skipped), liveâ†’delete (applied).
> - **The wire** ([action_confirm.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/action_confirm.go) `decodeConfirmToken` now reads `enabled_ops`; [plan_confirm.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_confirm.go) `effectExecutePlan` validates ids (unknownâ†’422 `bad_enabled_op`) and passes the set to `mcp.Execute` â€” **replacing the hardcoded `nil`** that made destructive ops impossible). Preview stamps `op_id`+`destructive` per row + cascade-count notes.
> - **FE** ([ConfirmActionCard.tsx](../../frontend/src/features/chat/components/ConfirmActionCard.tsx)): opt-in checkbox per destructive row + "N will be SKIPPED unless enabled" hint; [actionsApi.ts](../../frontend/src/features/chat/actionsApi.ts) sends `enabled_ops` only when â‰¥1 checked; i18n أ—4.
> - **ًں”ژ /review-impl â€” 1 MED + 1 LOW-coverage FIXED, 1 regression caught:** **MED** â€” `ontologyStateSummary` (the planner's ENTIRE view of current state) listed only kind codes â†’ `delete_genre`/`delete_attribute` referenced codes the planner had never seen (always `target_gone`). Fixed: summary now lists **genres too** (cheap) so `delete_genre` works; **`delete_attribute` dropped from planner vocab** (kept registered+tested â€” the `edit_attribute` precedent â€” since per-attribute context would bloat every plan call AND the GUI already deletes attributes). **LOW** â€” added a real-PG `delete_genre` round-trip (distinct cascade from kind). **Regression** â€” the wider test net caught `TestPlanRegistry_BuildsWithoutPanic` asserting Phase-1's `len==4`/no-destructive invariant; updated to the Phase-2 shape (7 ops, exactly the 3 deletes destructive).
> - **VERIFY:** glossary `go build`+`vet` clean; kit tests pass; **real-PG round-trips** (throwaway DB `loreweave_glossary_p2smoke`): `delete_kind` default-skip(`not_confirmed`, stays live)â†’enableâ†’`applied`(+cascade)â†’re-runâ†’`already_done`â†’bogusâ†’`target_gone`; `delete_genre` applyâ†’deprecatedâ†’`already_done`; existing confirm/preview round-trips **non-regressive** on real PG; FE **18 tests** (15 card + others) green, `tsc` clean. **âڈ³ NOT yet smoked: the full chat-UI flow** (`D-PLAN-EXEC-LIVE-SMOKE`).
>
> **âœ… PHASE 2 SLICE 2 SHIPPED (this session) â€” planner orchestrates the merge action over detected duplicates.** Design+plan [2026-06-25-plan-action-phase2-merge-orchestration.md](../plans/2026-06-25-plan-action-phase2-merge-orchestration.md). **Why this and not "entity ops":** two scouts established that glossary **entities are UUID-identified and their names are non-unique EAV values** â†’ a planner can't safely address an existing entity by name, AND merge/research already exist as class-C confirm actions (`descMerge`/`descDeepResearch`). The user chose the **"planner orchestrates existing actions"** path: the planner *selects* from detected `merge_candidates` (which already carry UUIDs) rather than inventing references.
> - **The op** ([plan_ops.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_ops.go) `merge_candidate`, tier 5 Destructive): params `{candidate_id, winner_id?}` â€” the planner copies a **stable `candidate_id`** (one PK per cluster) from context, never a transcribed member-UUID or ambiguous name. Handler resolves the candidate's CURRENT members at execute time (no staleness), picks winner (`winner_id` if a member â†’ else detector's `suggested_winner` â†’ else `ErrBadParams`), and calls the EXISTING `mergeEntitiesCore` (journals + marks-merged). One op **per candidate** â†’ each gets its own slice-1 enable toggle.
> - **Idempotency made independent of the best-effort `markCandidatesMerged` flip** (a test-driven fix â€” it lags under load): statusâ‰ `proposed`â†’`already_done` fast-path AND **0 losers actually merged â†’ `already_done`**, so a re-run is deterministic. Bogus idâ†’`target_gone`.
> - **Planner context** ([action_plan_tools.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/action_plan_tools.go) `mergeCandidatesSummary`, cap 25 by score, **best-effort** â€” additive, never fails an ontology plan): each cluster's `candidate_id` + member names/ids/link-counts + suggested winner + rationale. Vocab + destructive guidance updated. Preview resolves candidateâ†’winner-kept/losers-merged names.
> - **ًں”ژ /review-impl â€” 1 MED (prompt-injection) FIXED + tested:** the candidate context block embeds untrusted **entity names + detector rationale** (extraction/user content) into the planner's **SYSTEM** prompt â€” the first free-text there (kind/genre codes are slugs). The user-supplied `reference` is fenced as DATA in the user message, but the candidate block was not. Fixed: `safePromptField` runs `sanitize.NeutralizeCanonText` (the canon-boundary defense the deep-research path already uses â€” inert-izes chat-template/role markers + invisibles) + collapses whitespace (a smuggled newline can't forge a block field) + caps length. Regression tests assert a `<|im_start|>` / "ignore previous instructions" / newline-injected name is neutralized. (Blast radius was already bounded by the enable-toggle gate â€” every destructive op is default-OFF + shown â€” but untrusted text in the system prompt is a defense-in-depth gap worth closing.)
> - **VERIFY:** build/vet clean; kit green; **real-PG round-trip**: `merge_candidate` enableâ†’`applied` (loser soft-deleted via `mergeEntitiesCore`)â†’re-runâ†’`already_done`â†’bogusâ†’`target_gone`; +DB-free validate/parse/dedupe + `resolveMergeWinner` + neutralization unit; registry guard now **8 ops** (4 destructive). FE **unchanged** â€” the slice-1 toggle already renders any destructive plan op (merge_candidate rows carry `op_id`+`destructive`). **âڈ³ chat-UI flow still `D-PLAN-EXEC-LIVE-SMOKE`** (now also covers the merge path).
>
> **â–¶ Deferred (plan-action):**

Test kinds deprecated after (book left at 13 active kinds, as found). **âœ… `D-PLAN-PREVIEW-COUNT-DRIFT` FIXED (2026-06-26):** root cause WAS a real preview bug (separate from the planner's optimism) â€” the create_kinds card preview (`previewPlanOp`, [plan_confirm.go](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/plan_confirm.go)) counted "already exist" against the **alias-folded** `loadKindMap`, but `execute_plan`'s create_kinds inserts by **literal book_kind code** and skips only on a unique violation. So an alias of an adopted kind (e.g. `faction`â†’organization) read as "exists" in the preview while the executor created it â†’ the "1 new â€” 2 exist" vs actual "2 new â€” 1 exist" drift. > - ًںں، `D-PLAN-PLANNER-DEFAULT-FE` â€” **PHASE 1 DONE (2026-06-25): global planner default.** User reported the planner "uses a stupid model" â€” root cause: the resolver's fallback is `ORDER BY tool_calling DESC, user_model_id` = an ARBITRARY tool-calling chat model when no `planner` default is pinned, and there was NO UI to pin one (+ `putDefaultModel` rejected any model for `planner`, requiring a non-existent `planner:true` flag). **Fixed:** `putDefaultModel` validates the `planner` default against the **`chat`** flag (planner is a role, not a model flag) + Settings â†’ Default models gained a **Planner** row (`DefaultModelRow` `listCapability` decouples listing[chat] from save-key[planner]). VERIFY: real-PG `TestDefaultModels_PlannerAcceptsChatModel` (chat accepted, embedding rejected, resolver returns the pin as `planner_default`) + `DefaultModelsCard` 3 tests + tsc. **âڈ³ NEEDS provider-registry redeploy** for the BE relax to take effect live. **PHASE 2 DONE (2026-06-25): per-session planner override.** chat-service `chat_sessions` gained `planner_model_source`/`planner_model_ref` (migration mirrors composer); models + create/patch handlers carry them; `stream_service` loads `planner_model_ref` (main + resume paths) and **injects it as `model_ref` into the agent's `glossary_plan` tool call** (just before `mcp_execute_tool`, only when the model didn't choose one) â€” threaded `planner_model_ref` through `_emit_chat_turn` â†’ `_stream_with_tools` like `composer_model`. FE: `SessionSettingsPanel` gained a Planner picker next to Composer (`session.planner_model_ref`, '' = use the per-user default); types updated. VERIFY: py_compile clean; **40 chat-service stream tests pass**; tsc clean. **âڈ³ NEEDS chat-service redeploy + live verify.** **PHASE 3 DONE (2026-06-25): planner VIEW.** New `PlannerPlanView` renders an `execute_plan` confirm card as a numbered step list (humanized op label + rationale + per-op destructive opt-in checkbox + a trailing planner-notes block) instead of the terse `type: op-id` rows; `ConfirmActionCard` switches to it when `descriptor==='execute_plan'` (terse rows kept for other actions; the `enable-op` testid/data-op-id carried over so existing tests pass). VERIFY: `PlannerPlanView` 4 tests + `ConfirmActionCard` 15 green; tsc. **PHASE 4 finding (investigation):** ran `runPlanner` live (real glossary DB + provider-registry + LM Studio) on a COMPLEX 3-kind goal with a weak (Qwen2.5-7B) AND strong (Gemma-4 26B) model â€” **both produced VALID plans** (1 create_kinds op, ~15â€“18 code entries). â‡’ the planner's prompt/contract is SOUND; the reported "planner fails / uses a stupid model" is dominated by the **arbitrary weak-model fallback** (lowest-UUID tool-calling model), which Phases 1+2 now fix by letting users pin a strong planner. The "MCP-descriptions-insufficient" hypothesis is about the chat AGENT's multi-tool flow (separate from the single-shot planner) â€” needs a reproducible failing chat transcript + the live chat stack to diagnose; not pursued. **Note:** "composer" (session) is the prose-delegation model (`compose_prose`), NOT the planner â€” distinct role.

> - âœ… `D-KG-BENCHMARK-STATUS-SCOPE` â€” **CLEARED (2026-06-26): Build-graph button stayed disabled after a PASSING benchmark ("ran it, FE won't update").** Root cause was BE, not FE: the public `GET â€¦/benchmark-status` endpoint used the **project-scoped** `get_latest(user, project_id, model)`, but R1 (D-JOURNEY-KG-BENCHMARK-UX) runs benchmarks on a hidden per-(user,model) **SANDBOX** project â€” so the passing run lives under the sandbox's `project_id`, invisible to a content-project-scoped query. The badge therefore read `has_run=false` forever while the **model-scoped extraction gate** (`get_latest_for_model`) would have passed â€” a status/gate disagreement the endpoint's own docstring promised wouldn't happen. Verified in DB: 5 passing bge-m3 runs (`passed=t, recall=1.0`) all on sandbox `019effffâ€¦`, none on content `019effe4â€¦`. **Fix:** both the public + internal benchmark-status endpoints now use the MODEL-scoped `get_latest_for_model` when a model is supplied (falling back to project-scoped only for the no-model "ever benchmarked?" read), matching the gate. VERIFY: py_compile clean; **14 benchmark router tests** (public + internal) green incl. new model-scoped-lookup + surfaces-sandbox-run cases. **âڈ³ NEEDS knowledge-service redeploy** for the badge to unblock live. **CLEARED + deployed + live-verified 2026-06-26** (endpoint returns `has_run:true,passed:true` for the content project).
