Fixed the negative-index INSERT assertions (the new trailing arg shifted them) + added the fixtures' `thinking_enabled`. **âڈ³ NEEDS translation-service redeploy** (migration + worker) + a translation live-smoke. **Reasoning now available in all 3 paths: glossary-extraction (pre-existing), KG (`129a232d`), translation (this).**
> - ًںں، `D-EXTRACTION-BATCH-CONCURRENCY` â€” **CORE SHIPPED (2026-06-26): the glossary-extraction worker now honors `concurrency_level`.** Root cause of "we can only run 1 LLM call, wastes the GPU": the translation-service extraction worker referenced `concurrency` **zero times** â€” the per-(windowأ—batch) loop was a plain sequential `for`, and the knob never reached it. **Part A (propagate):** translation `create_extraction_job` `CreateExtractionJobPayload.concurrency_level` (â‰¤64) â†’ published in the `extraction.job` message (no migration â€” single publisher, no re-publish-from-row; redelivery carries it). **Part B (parallelize):** extracted the per-unit loop body into a nested `async def _run_unit` (no re-indent â€” nested def keeps the 8-space body; `continue`â†’`return`), driven under an `asyncio.Semaphore(concurrency)` via `gather`; `concurrency<=1` keeps the EXACT sequential fast-path (back-compat). Chapters stay sequential (per-chapter known-entity accumulation); only a chapter's batches fan out. Safe because asyncio is single-threaded: the shared collectors (`_accept`/`all_entities`, `_record_outcome`/`batch_outcomes` keyed by stable `call_idx`, token sums via `nonlocal`, `batch_finish_reasons`) mutate atomically between awaits; cache keys are per-unit; writeback is whole-chapter post-loop. Hard ceiling `_EXTRACTION_MAX_CONCURRENCY=16`. VERIFY: py_compile clean; **18 extraction-worker tests** incl. new `test_batch_concurrency_runs_all_units_via_gather` (4 batches, concurrency=4 â†’ 4 LLM calls, 4 outcomes, 4 entities, tokens summed) + 41 worker/truncation/cache/outcomes green (sequential path byte-unchanged). âœ… **Trigger-side SHIPPED:** the `features/extraction` ExtractionWizard `StepConfirm` gained a **"Parallel LLM calls"** number input (1â€“16, default 1) â†’ `ExtractionJobRequest.concurrency_level` â†’ `extractionApi.startJob` (sent only when >1). tsc clean; ExtractionWizard 3/3 green. So the full chain is live: FE field â†’ payload â†’ `extraction.job` message â†’ worker semaphore. **â–¶ LIVE-SMOKE RAN (2026-06-26) â€” REVEALED A DEEPER BOTTLENECK + a follow-up `D-PROVIDER-CONCURRENCY-CONFIG`.** Redeployed translation-service + worker (migration applied, worker has `_run_unit`+Semaphore). Drove a real extraction at concurrency=4 on the user's book (chapters 61-62) + the user's **Gemma 4 26B** (`019ebb6e`) via `mint_user_jwt` â†’ `POST /v1/extraction/books/{id}/extract-glossary`. Job `019f009f` completed (9 batches all `ok`, 53 entities). **BUT the user reported "still only 1 call in LM Studio."** Root-caused: the worker correctly fans out 4 `submit_job` calls, but `submit_and_wait` is a **gateway job-submit+poll** path, and the gateway (provider-registry) **hardcaps LOCAL provider concurrency to 1** â€” `ratelimit.maxFor`: `if localKinds[kind] { return 1 }` (`localKinds={ollama,lm_studio}`), sizing the queue's per-kind semaphore + the governor gate to 1 for local. So the 4 jobs serialize at the gateway â†’ LM Studio sees 1. **This is an anti-pattern (user call): the platform must NOT bake a local/cloud topology assumption into code.** **DECISION (user): concurrency is a property of the specific PROVIDER CREDENTIAL** (the user's lm_studio endpoint / their OpenAI key+tier), set by them â€” NOT per-kind, NOT per-cloud/local. **â–¶ `D-PROVIDER-CONCURRENCY-CONFIG` (next, M):** (a) **DB** â€” `provider_credentials.max_concurrency INT` (default sensible); (b) **governor/queue refactor** â€” key the semaphore by the **credential** (`concKey(credential_id)`, `semFor(credential_id)`) with the limit from the credential, replacing `localKinds`/`maxFor(kind)`; the queue's `KindResolver` â†’ returns `(credential_key, limit)`; (c) **FE** â€” a "Max concurrent requests" field in the BYOK provider/credential settings (ai-models). The worker-side concurrency (`c37629f1`) + wizard field (`73c7ff3e`) are correct + necessary â€” they're just gated by this gateway cap until it's fixed. (The MCP `translation_start_extraction` agent path can also pass `concurrency_level` now.) (Note: knowledge-service KG-build extraction is a SEPARATE system with its own concurrency_level â€” not this worker.)
> - âœ… `D-PROVIDER-CONCURRENCY-CONFIG` â€” **SHIPPED + LIVE-PROVEN (2026-06-26), provider-registry + FE.** The gateway no longer bakes a local/cloud topology assumption into code; concurrency is a property of the specific **provider credential** (the user's decision), and the **default is UNLIMITED** (request-as-demand â€” neither the platform nor the user knows a cloud backend's real limit, so don't pretend to). **What changed:** (a) **migration** â€” `provider_credentials.max_concurrency INT` NULL (NULL/â‰¤0 = unlimited). (b) **governor** ([governor.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/ratelimit/governor.go)) â€” deleted `localKinds`/`maxFor`/`MaxFor` (the `lm_studioâ†’1` hardcap); `Acquire(ctx, concClass, limit)` short-circuits to a no-op release when `limitâ‰¤0` (never touches Redis). `Guard`/`ConcurrencyGovernor` now take `(concClass, limit)`. (c) **queue** ([queue.go](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/jobs/queue.go)) â€” `KindResolver`â†’`ConcurrencyResolver` returns `(concClass, limit, ok)`; `semFor(class, limit)` returns **nil** (no gate) when unlimited, else a buffered chan of `limit`; `handleDelivery` skips the gate when nil; dropped `cloudMax` from `NewJobQueue`. (d) **resolver** â€” new `repo.ResolveConcurrency` keys by `provider_credential_id` (user_model JOIN credential; platform_model â†’ `platform:<id>`, unlimited); the worker computes `(concClass, concLimit)` once in `Process` (fail-open â†’ unlimited) and threads it through `processChunks`/`streamWithRetry` (replacing `providerKind` in the Guard call); deleted the now-orphaned `ResolveKind`. (e) **CRUD contract** â€” `max_concurrency` in create/list/get + a **present-aware PATCH** (`optionalInt` distinguishes absent=keep from null=clear-to-unlimited via `CASE WHEN $present THEN $val ELSE max_concurrency END`). (f) **FE** â€” "Max concurrent requests" number field (''=unlimited) in `ProvidersTab` edit dialog (the AI-model provider editor) + `ProviderCredential.max_concurrency` type + create/patch payloads; i18n أ—4. **VERIFY:** go build + all go tests green (jobs/ratelimit/api; new `TestGovernor_UnlimitedPassesThroughWithoutRedis`, `TestSemFor_PerCredentialSizing`, `TestResolveConcurrency_*`); FE tsc 0 + settings vitest 19/19. **âœ… LIVE A/B on deployed stack (Gemma/lm_studio, sampling concurrent `running` llm_jobs â€” cache-independent):** cap=1 â†’ **max 1 concurrent** (serialized); unlimited(default) â†’ **max 4 concurrent** (the worker's `concurrency=4` fan-out now lands 4-wide on LM Studio â€” the exact win the user wanted). **âœ… LIVE PATCH round-trip:** set 4â†’`4`, patch unrelated fieldâ†’**keeps 4** (present-aware), clear nullâ†’**unlimited**. Credential left at unlimited. Closes the gateway-cap bottleneck that `D-EXTRACTION-BATCH-CONCURRENCY` surfaced; the worker fan-out (`c37629f1`) + wizard field (`73c7ff3e`) are now unblocked. **Known limitation (conscious):** the QUEUE `semFor` caches a credential's cap on first sight (a buffered chan can't resize), so ANY numericâ†’numeric runtime change (raise OR lower) needs a restart to affect the queue gate; switching TO unlimited is immediate (nil-sem path bypasses the cache). The Redis governor (per-CALL gate in `Guard`) reads the cap fresh each `Acquire`, so it honors a change immediately â€” during a post-change/pre-restart window the effective cap is the TIGHTER of {cached queue cap, fresh governor cap} (safe: never exceeds the new value; a raise just lags). **Also:** cloud/platform models (NULL cap) are now UNLIMITED-by-default â€” the old `GOVERNOR_CLOUD_MAX=8` per-kind cap is gone (the user's explicit "default unlimited"); backstop = the queue consumer pool (`workersâ‰ˆGovernorCloudMax*2`, ~16), so one process still can't exceed ~16 concurrent jobs. **/review-impl fix folded in:** the queue's jobâ†’class resolver now FAILS-OPEN on a transient lookup error (runs ungoverned) instead of ack+dropping â€” fail-closed stranded a `pending` job forever (the truth-sweeper only recovers stuck `running`); only a genuinely-gone model is dropped. Mirrors `Process`'s fail-open.
> - âœ… `D-GLOSSARY-VC-FE-WIRING` â€” **SHIPPED + BROWSER-SMOKED (2026-06-26), FE-only.** The user's "glossary version-control has a backend but was never wired to the FE" was *nearly* right: the entity-revision UI (`EntityHistoryPanel` + `useEntityRevisions` + types + i18n) was **fully built but stranded in dead code** â€” `pages/book-tabs/EntityEditor.tsx`, a sidebar editor **never imported/mounted anywhere**. Users only ever see `components/entity-editor/EntityEditorModal.tsx` (rendered by `GlossaryTab.tsx`), which lacked it. Diff of the two editors: the dead one's ONLY delta was the History button â€” the live modal supersedes it on every other axis (evidence tab, translations, i18n, typed cards). **Fix = migrate-then-delete:** (a) made `EntityHistoryPanel` embeddable (`embedded?: boolean` drops its overlay header + inner scroller so it renders as tab content; `onClose` now optional, default false keeps existing callers/tests unchanged); (b) added a **History tab** to `EntityEditorModal` reusing the panel + hook (lazy fetch on tab open, mirrors the evidences tab; restore â†’ `load()` + `onSaved()` refresh); (c) `modal.tab.history` i18n أ—4; (d) **deleted the dead `EntityEditor.tsx`** (drift hazard). Backend (entity_revisions list/view/restore, async VG-1 projection capture, exact id-preserving reconcile) was already complete + wired. **VERIFY:** tsc 0; **105/105** glossary+entity-editor tests (new embedded-mode `EntityHistoryPanel` test); i18n parity 4 locales; no dangling ref to the deleted file. **âœ… BROWSER-SMOKED on rebuilt frontend image** (test acct, Dracula book `019eeb09`, entity "The Night Journey" `019eeb22` w/ 4 revisions): History tab renders the revision list (localized ops/actors) â†’ **View** expands the full whole-entity snapshot â†’ **Restore** behind confirm â†’ real server-side reconcile (entity reverted to rev #1: name viâ†’en, translations 8â†’0 pruned) â†’ captured as new reversible revision #5 (DB-confirmed) â†’ surfaced in the list on refetch; restored back to #4 (translations re-added â†’ 8) to leave data as found. Lesson saved: [[feedback_feature_wired_into_dead_editor_is_invisible]]. (Wiki revisions â€” a SEPARATE VC system in the same service â€” remain their own FE concern; not in scope.)
> - âœ… `D-KG-TL-SIMPLIFIED-TRADITIONAL-DUP` â€” **SHIPPED via wire-then-wipe (2026-06-26).** **DECISION (user): the existing KG is disposable test data â†’ wipe + re-extract, NO node-merge migration.** So: the multi-language normalizer (`name_normalize.py`) is now **WIRED into `canonicalize_entity_name`/`canonicalize_text`** (SDK `loreweave_extraction.canonical`) as their first step â€” NFKC + Unicode casefold + CJK traditionalâ†’simplified fold (vendored frozen table), accents PRESERVED. Variant spellings of one entity (ه¼µè‹¥ه،µ/ه¼ è‹¥ه°ک, full-width `ï¼«ï½پï½‰`/`Kai`, أں/ss, composed/decomposed accents) now share ONE `canonical_id` instead of spawning duplicates. **Backward-compatible:** all 22 legacy canonical cases unchanged (ASCII casefold==lower, already-simplified CJK/kana/hangul untouched); only true variants fold. **Dry-run proof** (read-only, on ن¸‡هڈ¤ç¥‍ه¸‌ 5705 entities BEFORE wiping): **69 same-source S/T duplicate groups** would collapse (incl. the protagonist ه¼µè‹¥ه،µ+ه¼ è‹¥ه°ک â€” the participant-localization miss). VERIFY: 12/12 SDK `name_normalize` tests + 22 legacy canonical cases + id-collapse + canonicalize_text fold. **âœ… WIPED + DEPLOYED (2026-06-26):** Neo4j KG wiped (`infra-neo4j-1` batched DETACH DELETE, **6315 â†’ 0 nodes**); knowledge-service + translation-service + translation-worker images rebuilt with the SDK change; deployed fold verified live (`infra-knowledge-service-1` canonicalizes ه¼µè‹¥ه،µâ†’ه¼ è‹¥ه°ک, matches ه¼ è‹¥ه°ک). Re-extraction of any book now produces deduped entities at the source â€” no S/T duplicates. Migration design retained in the spec for reference (NOT built). **âœ… LIVE DEDUP SMOKE PASSED (2026-06-26, deployed stack + real Neo4j):** drove the deployed `merge_entity` write primitive with ه¼µè‹¥ه،µ (traditional) then ه¼ è‹¥ه°ک (simplified) â€” both produced the **SAME `canonical_id` â†’ ONE `:Entity` node** (`canonical_name='ه¼ è‹¥ه°ک'`, `aliases=['ه¼µè‹¥ه،µ','ه¼ è‹¥ه°ک']` â€” both surface forms kept as aliases); a control ه…«çژ‹ه­گ stayed a DISTINCT entity (no over-merge); total nodes = 2 as expected; cleaned up. Proves the exact bug fixed end-to-end on real Neo4j (more decisive than an LLM extraction, which on simplified-script source wouldn't exercise the tradâ†’simpl fold). A full re-extraction is the user's normal next step (re-populates the KG with deduped entities). **Phase-1 detail (superseded by this wiring):** Generalized per the user to **multi-language** (not hard-coded CJK). Spec [docs/specs/2026-06-26-multilang-entity-normalization-dedup.md](../specs/2026-06-26-multilang-entity-normalization-dedup.md). **Root cause:** `canonicalize_entity_name` (SDK `loreweave_extraction.canonical`) does lowercase+honorific+punct only â€” no Unicode-equivalence/script folding â€” so variant spellings of one entity get different `canonical_id` (the Neo4j `:Entity` MERGE key) â†’ duplicate nodes (ه¼µè‹¥ه،µ w/ vi vs ه¼ è‹¥ه°ک w/o). **Converter decision (forced by determinism):** the CJK simplified/traditional fold uses a **vendored FROZEN table**, NOT opencc/hanziconv â€” `entity_canonical_id` must be stable forever, and an external lib could change its mapping between versions â†’ silent mass re-keying. NFKC + casefold are stdlib. **âœ… Phase 1 (PURE, no data touched):** new SDK `name_normalize.py` (`nfkc_casefold` â†’ `fold_han_simplified` â†’ `normalize_entity_name`; Han-gated; accents PRESERVED â€” folds equivalence not similarity, so vi `mأ،`â‰ `ma`) + vendored `_han_simplified_table.py` (curated high-confidence Tâ†’S starter set incl. the ن¸‡هڈ¤ç¥‍ه¸‌ cast; documented-incomplete, full OpenCC import is a Phase-2 follow-up, API unchanged when it grows). NOT yet wired into the live id derivation (cutover lands WITH the migration). VERIFY: 12/12 SDK unit tests (ASCII unchanged, full-widthâ†’ascii, أںâ†’ss, ه¼µè‹¥ه،µâ†’ه¼ è‹¥ه°ک, accents kept, kana untouched, guards). **â–¶ Phase 2 (NEXT, needs go):** wire `canonicalize_entity_name`â†’`normalize_entity_name` (NO `canonical_version` bump â†’ surgical: only changed-string entities affected, ASCII untouched) + a per-(user,project) **dedup migration** that groups entities by `(kind, normalize_entity_name(name))`, merges variant duplicates via the existing `merge_entities` primitive (entities.py:2488 â€” rewires relations/facts/evidence) **+ two gaps it predates:** repoint `:Event.participant_entity_ids` (loserâ†’winner) + `entity_alias_map` redirects; updates the winner's `canonical_name` to the new form. **DRY-RUN mode first** (report duplicate groups + planned merges, write nothing) on ن¸‡هڈ¤ç¥‍ه¸‌ `019effe4` before any destructive run, then live-smoke (ه¼ è‹¥ه°ک/ه¼µè‹¥ه،µ collapse â†’ timeline localizes ه¼ è‹¥ه°ک). Risk: re-keys the entity primary key â€” mitigated by no-version-bump + dry-run + the tested merge primitive + per-project scoping + merge journal.
> - âœ… `D-GLOSSARY-ST-DEDUP` â€” **SHIPPED + LIVE-APPLIED (2026-06-26), glossary-service + new Go SDK.** The glossary-side sibling of `D-KG-TL-SIMPLIFIED-TRADITIONAL-DUP` (that one fixed the knowledge-service / Neo4j layer in Python; this fixes glossary's Go/Postgres entity dedup, which holds REAL user data â†’ NO wipe, a journaled merge instead). Spec [2026-06-26-glossary-st-dedup.md](../specs/2026-06-26-glossary-st-dedup.md). Commits `fa8e702b`(M1) `a0ebb61c`(M2) `636c5843`(M3) `9c594865`(review fixes). **M1 â€” new Go SDK `sdks/go/loreweave_extraction`** (`NormalizeEntityName` = NFKC + Unicode casefold + CJK tradâ†’simpl; mirror of the Python `name_normalize`). The Han **T2S table is code-generated from a NEW shared SoT** `sdks/data/han_t2s.tsv`; both the Go table and the frozen Python dict are pinned to it by **parity tests on each side** (Go `TestT2SParityWithSoT` + Python `test_t2s_parity`) so the fold can never silently drift across languages. **M2 â€” glossary `textnorm.Normalize` delegates to the SDK** â†’ the **resolver** (`findEntityByNameOrAlias`, the PRIMARY dedup â€” it compares names IN GO) now folds, so NEW extractions of ه¼ è‹¥ه°ک/ه¼µè‹¥ه،µ resolve to one entity instead of spawning a dup. **M3a â€” `normalized_name` GENERATEDâ†’app-maintained:** migration `0040` `ALTER COLUMN â€¦ DROP EXPRESSION` (preserves values, non-destructive, attgenerated-guarded) â€” Postgres has no native casefold/t2s, so the fold can't live in SQL; `refreshEntityDedupKey(q, entityID)` recomputes it from `cached_name` via the SAME SDK fold, wired into **all 6 audited name-write paths** (createExtractedEntity, mergeExtractedEntity, patchAttributeValue[name/term], applyEntityEdit, reconcileEntityFromSnapshot, rekeyEntityToKind). **M3b â€” internal remediation endpoint** `POST /internal/books/{id}/dedup-name-variants[?apply=true]` (DRY-RUN default): groups live entities by `(kind, folded name)`, merges each >1 group into the richest winner (links+evidence, stable tie-break) via the journaled `mergeEntitiesCore` (reversible; emits the `merged` outbox event that re-syncs the KG), then re-stamps `normalized_name`. **ًں”ژ /review-impl caught 2 HIGH before the destructive apply:** (1) re-stamp ran BEFORE merge â†’ both live group members collided on `uq_entity_dedup` â†’ apply 500s; fixed by merge-first-then-restamp + unique-violation tolerance. (2) the shared test helper `runK2aMigrations` ran `0032`(generated col) but not `0040` â†’ every wired path hit "column can only be updated to DEFAULT"; fixed by running `0040` in the helper + rewriting `TestEntityDedup_UniqueIndexBackstop` to drive the backstop through the app path (the post-M3a invariant: the DB backstop is keyed when the APP stamps the key, not on a raw EAV write that bypasses the app â€” the accepted app-maintained tradeoff). **VERIFY:** Go SDK + textnorm + betterWinner unit tests green; **full glossary api integration suite green** (no generated-column errors). **âœ… LIVE-APPLIED on ن¸‡هڈ¤ç¥‍ه¸‌ `019efae6` (deployed stack):** migration ran on boot (column now plain, ledger `0040`); dry-run found **63 S/T duplicate groups** (Go endpoint exactly matched a read-only Python dry-run â€” cross-confirming the fold); **apply merged all 63 cleanly + re-stamped 4001**, protagonist `ه¼µè‹¥ه،µ`(live winner, normalized_name=ه¼ è‹¥ه°ک) â†گ `ه¼ è‹¥ه°ک`(soft-deleted, merged_into winner); **idempotent re-run â†’ 0 groups** (total 5646â†’5583, exactly âˆ’63). **âœ… Cross-service live-smoke:** 63 `glossary.entity_merged` + 63 `glossary.entity_updated` events emitted â†’ knowledge-service consumed cleanly (winners synced to Neo4j; merges logged "KG nodes absent â€” no-op, reconverges" since the KG was wiped earlier â€” no errors). **âœ… All 3 follow-ups CLEARED (2026-06-26, commit `b2439fcf`, deployed + live-verified):** (1) **`D-GLOSSARY-ST-RENAME-409`** â€” `refreshEntityDedupKey` returns a typed `errDuplicateName` on a `uq_entity_dedup` collision; the 3 user-facing name-write handlers (patchAttributeValue, applyEntityEdit, restore) map it to **409 `GLOSS_DUPLICATE_NAME`** (unit-asserted via `TestEntityDedup_UniqueIndexBackstop`). (2) **`D-GLOSSARY-DEDUP-ENDPOINT-LOCK`** â€” the apply path takes the per-book advisory lock (same key as the extraction writeback, NS `0x45585457`/`1163416663`) on a dedicated conn; try-lock â†’ **409 `GLOSS_BOOK_BUSY`** when extraction holds it (dry-run stays lock-free). **Live-verified**: held the lock in psql â†’ apply returned 409. (3) **Full OpenCC table** â€” `sdks/data/han_t2s.tsv` regenerated from OpenCC `TSCharacters.txt` (**224â†’3149 pairs**, unambiguous 1-to-1 non-identity only â€” multi-candidate context-dependent entries EXCLUDED to avoid over-merge); both the Go table + Python dict regenerated from it, parity tests green. **Live-applied on ن¸‡هڈ¤ç¥‍ه¸‌**: the fuller table unlocked **84 MORE genuine S/T dup groups** the curated 224 missed (88 entities merged, total 5583â†’5495), all verified genuine (`è·،/è¹ںâ†’è؟¹`, `ه‹³/ه‹›â†’ه‹‹`, `و°£â†’و°”`; `ن¹¾ه‌¤` correctly NOT folded â€” ن¹¾ was an excluded multi-candidate, so no ن¹¾/ه¹² over-merge); 88 `entity_merged` events re-synced the KG cleanly. Idempotent re-run â†’ 0 groups.
> - âœ… `D-KG-TL-PARTICIPANT-ANCHOR` â€” **SHIPPED + LIVE-SMOKED (2026-06-26), knowledge-service.** Spec [2026-06-26-kg-timeline-localization.md](../specs/2026-06-26-kg-timeline-localization.md) آ§(c) Option A آ· plan [2026-06-26-kg-tl-participant-anchor.md](../plans/2026-06-26-kg-tl-participant-anchor.md). (b) `Event.participant_entity_ids: list[str] | None` (`Field(exclude=True)` â€” stored on the node, populated for the localizer, NEVER serialized â†’ API wire unchanged); `merge_event` gains `participant_anchors` + builds the array ALIGNED to the deduped participants (glossary id, or `""` sentinel where unanchored â€” Neo4j lists can't hold nulls); `_MERGE_EVENT_CYPHER` writes it ON CREATE + appends new slots ON MATCH via an index-filter with `coalesce($participant_entity_ids[i], "")` (guards the empty-array/legacy case so no null ever enters the list). (d) [timeline_localizer.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/labels/timeline_localizer.py) `localize_participants` now PREFERS the stored anchor (length-guard: trust only an array whose len == participants) and resolves only the RESIDUAL names from un-backfilled events â€” a fully-backfilled page does ZERO read-time resolution; legacy/misaligned falls back to Option B. (e) backfill [backfill_participant_anchors.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/db/migrations/backfill_participant_anchors.py) + internal `POST /internal/projects/{id}/backfill-participant-anchors` (mirrors backfill-orders). >   - `D-EXTRACT-ATTR-MERGE-DEFAULTS` â€” **re-extraction appends evidence only, never updates attributes.** Root cause: every layer defaults the per-attribute merge action to **`fill`** = write-once-if-empty â†’ already-filled attrs skipped (`fill_occupied`, [extraction_handler.go:1376](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/extraction_handler.go#L1376)); FE/worker/DB (`merge_strategy DEFAULT 'fill_if_empty'`) all converge on fill. The engine **already supports `append`(multi-value)+`overwrite`** (tested) but nothing selects them + `append` isn't in the FE enum ([types.ts:29](../../frontend/src/features/extraction/types.ts#L29)). NOT a description/ontology-text issue. **Fix = config/code:** seed per-attribute `merge_strategy` (`append` for aliases/titles/abilities, `overwrite` for location/status, `fill` for identity) + expose `append` in FE; keep INV-8 verified-clobber guard. Reuse the raw cache to re-merge without re-LLM. `field_type=tags`â†’`append`; elseâ†’`overwrite`), CONDITIONAL on the untouched `fill_if_empty` default so author/admin overrides are never clobbered (idempotent). **M2** â€” the resolver ([extraction_handler.go:1341](../../services/glossary-service/internal/api/extraction_handler.go#L1341)) now treats an explicit `"default"` action like an omitted one (defer to authored strategy); the worker default-profile builders ([extraction.py:159](../../services/translation-service/app/routers/extraction.py#L159), [mcp/server.py:729](../../services/translation-service/app/mcp/server.py#L729)) emit `"default"` instead of forcing `"fill"`; FE `AttributeAction` gains `default`+`append`, `StepProfile` auto-selects `default` (required stays `fill`) + adds Append/Default options & bulk buttons; i18n أ—4. **VERIFY:** real-PG `TestMergeStrategy_DefaultSentinel` (defaultâ†’append accumulates, defaultâ†’overwrite replaces) + full extraction-writeback suite green; FE tsc 0 + 8/8; **migration applied LIVE** on real glossary DB (distribution tagsâ†’append 3422, textareaâ†’overwrite 11673, text identity/state split, 1 pre-existing author `append` preserved); workerâ†’glossary `extract-entities` **HTTP 200** on redeployed code (in-flight 4233-ch job 019f00e2 non-regressed). **â–¶ M3 SHIPPED (2026-06-26):** raw-cache re-merge HEAL to unfreeze ALREADY-extracted entities at $0 LLM. `replay_chapter_from_cache(use_authored_strategy=True)` ([extraction_replay.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_replay.py)) sends `attribute_actions={}` so the glossary resolver defers every attr to its authored strategy (append/overwrite), and adds a `"heal"` discriminator to the whole-chapter writeback_key so a chapter already written under the old frozen `fill` isn't deduped against the faithful write (re-running heal stays idempotent via the distinct key + per-item append dedup). New `rerun_merge_book` + endpoint `POST /internal/translation/extraction-cache/rerun-merge` ([internal_dispatch.py](../../services/translation-service/app/routers/internal_dispatch.py)) heals a whole book (or a `chapter_ids` subset), grant-gated EDIT + confirm-gated dry-run, bounded response (status counts + capped problem sample). **VERIFY:** 2 DB-free unit tests (aggregation + 50-cap) green; **LIVE on the user's running book:** dry-run â†’ `mode:heal, would_write:60` for 2 cached chapters; confirm on 1 early chapter â†’ `status_counts:{replayed:1}` (re-merged via translationâ†’glossary under authored strategy, $0 LLM, no race with the in-flight 4233-ch job). **Use for the big book once its extraction is idle** to heal all chapters frozen under the old default.
> - ًںں، `D-LLM-FAILURE-RATE` â€” **NEW (2026-06-26), in progress. DIAGNOSIS CORRECTED via live data.** Initial theory (truncation/thinking burning the output budget) was **disproven on the running stack**: `extraction_raw_outputs` over the live run shows `reasoning_effort=none` + `effort_band=none` on **every** call, **0** outputs containing `<think>`, `finish_reason=stop` everywhere (**no** `length`-truncation), and **151/155 batches clean**. All 3 workers already disable thinking correctly (extraction via `reasoning_fields`, chapter-translate hardcodes `enable_thinking:False` [session_translator.py:925](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/session_translator.py#L925), glossary-translate via `thinking_llm_fields`), and the gateway forwards `chat_template_kwargs` to lm_studio (only strips it for the *default* OpenAI cloud endpoint). So the `completed_with_errors` wall is **overstated** â€” the flag fires on a SINGLE non-clean batch; real rate â‰ˆ5%, cause = **validation rejection on cleanly-finished output** (model occasionally returns wrong-kind / rarely-malformed JSON), NOT thinking/truncation. **Fix = structured-output enforcement (#1).** âœ… **Gateway enabling slice SHIPPED:** `streamRequest.ResponseFormat` + `buildChatStreamInput` thread `response_format` into the adapter input (the `forwardOptionalChatFields` allowlist already lists it but nothing wired it from the request); absent-when-unset; build/vet clean + 3 `buildChatStreamInput` tests (forwards + omits-when-unset). âœ… **Extraction worker slice SHIPPED:** `_entity_response_format(batch_kinds)` builds a LOOSE json_schema (top-level array; item requires `kind`=enum-of-batch-kinds + `name`; `additionalProperties:true` so per-kind profile fields stay free) attached as `response_format` when `_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_ENABLED` (env `EXTRACTION_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT`, default ON); **safe fallback** â€” the call is wrapped so a provider `LLMInvalidRequest` (model/server rejects the shape) **retries ONCE without response_format**, then a 2nd failure propagates as the real permanent error. py_compile clean; 2 new schema-builder tests + 25 existing extraction tests green. **â–¶ NEXT:** (a) same pattern for **glossary-translate** ([glossary_translate_worker.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/glossary_translate_worker.py)) + **chapter-translate** ([session_translator.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/session_translator.py)) â€” their output schemas differ; (b) âœ… **`D-LLM-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT-LIVE-SMOKE` â€” PASSED (2026-06-26, deployed stack).** Verified the deployed provider-registry Go binary carries the `response_format` wiring + translation-worker has `_entity_response_format`. Then a real gateway call (`/internal/llm/stream`, test-account local **Qwen2.5 7B Instruct** `019eb620â€¦`, the loose entity json_schema) returned **HTTP 200 + a clean conforming array** `[{"kind":"location","name":"royal martial arena"},{"kind":"character","name":"Zhang Ruochen"},{"kind":"character","name":"Eighth Prince"}]` (n=3, valid kinds from the enum). The **control WITHOUT response_format reproduced the bug**: malformed `["kind","Zhang Ruochen"]` arrays â†’ PARSE FAILED â€” the exact `validation_rejected`/`completed_with_errors` failure mode. Proves: gateway forwards it, lm_studio honors the json_schema (no LLMInvalidRequest â†’ fallback not even needed for this model), and the schema converts malformed output into clean valid-kind output. Residual: a full extraction-JOB end-to-end (workerâ†’outcomes) wasn't driven (needs a cache-miss chapter), but the worker sends this exact response_format + is unit-tested. âœ… **Glossary-translate slice SHIPPED + object-schema live-verified:** `attr_response_format(expected_codes)` (a JSON **object** of string-valued props keyed by the attribute codes, `additionalProperties:false`) in [glossary_translate_prompt.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/glossary_translate_prompt.py); the worker attaches it (gated by `TRANSLATION_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT`, default ON) + the same retry-without `LLMInvalidRequest` fallback. Kills the "Expecting ',' delimiter" parse failures that failed an entity. 3 schema tests + 102 translate-suite tests green; py_compile clean. **Object-schema live-smoke PASSED** (same gateway call, object json_schema â†’ HTTP 200, valid `{"name":"â€¦"}` object of strings â€” lm_studio accepts the object shape too). â›” **Chapter-translate EXCLUDED from #1 â€” NOT a JSON pipeline:** its output is `[BLOCK N]` marker TEXT ([block_batcher.py:254](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/block_batcher.py#L254) splits on the markers), so a JSON `response_format` would BREAK it; its failures are a different class (block-count/validation) already handled by its retry-with-correction loop. **#1 is COMPLETE for the two JSON pipelines (extraction + glossary-translate).** âœ… **DEPLOY CONFIRMED (2026-06-26, rebuilt stack):** the running `infra-translation-worker-1`/`-service-1` image carries `attr_response_format` + both gate flags `EXTRACTION_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT` and `TRANSLATION_STRUCTURED_OUTPUT` **defaulting to `"1"`** (env unset â†’ ON fleet-wide), so both JSON pipelines emit `response_format` live. â–¶ Remaining (optional only): a full extraction-job re-run on a cache-miss chapter to watch the `completed_with_errors` rate drop on real content (the gateway-level proof already showed the schema converts malformedâ†’clean output; this is corroboration, not a gap). #3 (split-and-retry) deprioritized â€” no current truncation. #2 (context_window) is fine â€” user confirmed lm_studio loads 200K.
> - âœ… `D-PLAN-MERGE-CONTEXT-COST` â€” **CLEARED (2026-06-25).** `loadMergeCandidates` gained a `limit` arg: the planner passes `maxMergeCandidatesForPlan(=25)+1` so the SQL `LIMIT` caps BOTH the candidate rows AND the per-candidate member-detail load (`allMembers` only collects from the loaded set) â€” every plan call now loads â‰¤26 clusters, not the whole `proposed` backlog. Highest-score clusters survive (ORDER BY score before LIMIT); `mergeCandidatesSummary(cands, more)` shows an honest "more candidates exist" note when the cap elided lower-score ones. FE inbox + pipeline-read tool pass `limit=0` (unlimited â€” they want the full set to signal truncation), so their behaviour is unchanged. Kept the LIMIT over the alternative goal-keyword gate (multilingual goals make keyword gating fragile â€” same call as delete-attr-vocab). VERIFY: build/vet clean; real-PG `TestLoadMergeCandidates_LimitCapsLoad` (limit=2 â†’ top-2 by score; limit=0 â†’ all 3) + merge/neutralization family green.
> - `D-PLAN-MERGE-DISMISSED-LABEL` â€” **CONSCIOUS won't-fix (gate 5):** a `merge_candidate` op naming a `dismissed` candidate returns `already_done` (skipped) â€” a slightly loose label (the merge never happened; it was rejected), but the OUTCOME (no merge, safe) is correct and the planner only ever sees `proposed` candidates in context. The kit's sentinel set has no "rejected" reason; not worth a new one.
> **âœ… SLICE 2.5 SHIPPED â€” `dismiss_candidate` (non-destructive sibling of merge).** Same candidate-id addressing; the planner emits `dismiss_candidate {candidate_id}` to REJECT a detected cluster as not-duplicates (flips statusâ†’`dismissed`, no entity touched), reusing the existing `dismissMergeCandidateCore`. **NON-destructive** â†’ applies on a plain confirm with NO enable toggle (the real-PG test asserts exactly this via `f.confirm` without `enabled_ops`). Idempotency: statusâ‰ `proposed`â†’`already_done`; bogusâ†’`target_gone`. Registry guard now **9 ops** (5 non-destructive incl. dismiss, 4 destructive). **ًں”‘ also fixed a latent test-isolation bug:** the candidate real-PG tests were silently depending on another test creating `merge_candidates` first â€” now they run the full idempotent `runMergeMigrations` chain (correct post-G4 `kind_id`â†’`book_kinds` FK), so they pass in isolation.
> - âœ… `D-BATCH-RESEARCH-JOB` â€” **FULLY SHIPPED + e2e-COMPLETE + review-passed 2026-06-25 (M1 + M2 + M3).** **/review-impl (3 fixed):** (1) FE progress tracked `items_processed` (visits, incl. free skips) أ· `items_total` (search cap) â†’ could read ">100% / 13/10"; now `searches_run`أ·cap, pinned 100% on complete. (2) launch-modal estimate `useEffect` re-ran on the unstable `fetchEstimate` closure â†’ re-fetched + **clobbered the user's `max_entities`**; now a run-once ref guard. (3) resuming a `failed` job while another live job exists for the kind tripped the one-live unique index â†’ 500; now mapped to a clean 409. **âœ… FULL E2E on the deployed stack:** recreated `infra-glossary-service-1` with this branch â†’ migration 0038 applied + ledgered + worker auto-started; deployed routes verified (list 200 / estimate 200 / create-bad-kind 404 / no-auth 401 via a minted test-account JWT); **full HTTP cycle**: `POST research-jobs` (owner-grant on an adopted owned book + 1 seeded entity) â†’ `pending` â†’ the **in-container worker** drained it â†’ **`complete`** in 6s with `searches=1, sources=3` (real searxng "Mina Harker") â†’ `GET status` confirmed live. Earlier: worker pipeline e2e (real searxng â†’ evidence â†’ complete on a throwaway DB) + stubbed worker unit tests. **âœ… BROWSER E2E (Playwright, temp `infra-frontend` container on `infra_default` :5199 â€” host vite shadows :5174):** logged in as the test account â†’ book â†’ Glossary â†’ Ontology â†’ Manage â†’ selected the `character` kind â†’ the **KindResearchPanel rendered** ("Web research" / "Research entities" / "No research job yet") â†’ launch modal showed the **live estimate** ("1 of 1 entities آ· ~$0.0100 indicative", max_entities defaulted to the entity count = fix #2 working) â†’ typed a query â†’ Start â†’ the card polled pendingâ†’runningâ†’**Complete** ("Done â€” 5 sources across 1 entities"); DB confirmed `complete searches=1 sources=5` + 5 reference-evidence rows. Also visually confirmed the case-2 genre/kind **delete trash is always-visible**. All artifacts cleaned up; temp container removed. **THE FEATURE IS FULLY e2e-PROVEN INCLUDING THE BROWSER UI.** **M3 (FE):** `researchApi.ts` (6 endpoints + estimate) â†’ `useKindResearch` controller hook (react-query, polls at 2.5s while a job is active, stops on terminal) â†’ `ResearchJobCard` (render-only per-state: pending/running+progress-bar/paused/complete/failed/cancelled with pause/resume/retry/cancel) + `ResearchLaunchModal` (query template with `{name}`, max_entities, max_results, pre-flight entity-count + indicative cost) + `KindResearchPanel` orchestrator, wired into `ManageWorkspace` under the selected kind. i18n أ—4 (`research.*`). VERIFY: `ResearchJobCard` render tests (per-state actions fire the right callbacks; complete is terminal-info; failed shows the error) + tiering suite 19 green; tsc clean; 4 locale JSONs valid. **M2 (FE-prior):** see below. Full design: [2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md](../specs/2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md).
> - ~~`D-BATCH-RESEARCH-JOB` M1+M2 line~~ â€” superseded by the FULLY-SHIPPED line above. **M2 (worker + lifecycle):** in-process `RunResearchWorker` (started in main.go, gated on a web-search URL) drains `pending` jobs one entity at a time â€” claim via `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` + stale-`running` reclaim (crash recovery), per-entity heartbeat/cursor persist (resume from `cursor_entity_id`), paid-search cap on `max_entities` (already-researched / anchorless entities skipped FREE via reference-evidence check), web-search error â†’ `failed` without advancing cursor (resume retries). pause/resume/cancel endpoints + a shared `transitionResearchJob` (404 vs 409, clears error on resume). VERIFY: build/vet clean; real-PG worker tests (cap honoured + evidence attached + cursor; skip-already-researched is free; web-search-errorâ†’failed; pause/resume/cancel/404/409 state machine); **âœ… live cross-service smoke** glossaryâ†’provider-registryâ†’searxng returned 3 real results (the worker's paid leg). Caught a real test bug: uuidv7's first 8 chars are a timestamp â†’ non-unique name suffixes tripped `uq_entity_dedup`. **M3:** `ResearchJobCard` FE (states pending/running+progress/paused/complete/failed) + a "research all entities of this kind" launch on the kind + polling + i18n أ—4.
> - ~~`D-BATCH-RESEARCH-JOB` M1-only note~~ â€” superseded by the M1+M2 line above. M1 = `entity_research_jobs` table (migration 0038, one-live-per-(book,kind) partial unique index) + `researchOneEntity` refactor (the per-entity web-search/neutralize/attach core now SHARED by the sync `glossary_deep_research` and the future worker â€” deep-research tests green) + create/list/status endpoints + a pre-flight `research-estimate`. **BUILD REFINEMENT:** web-search returns no per-call cost (BYOK) â†’ capped by **`max_entities`** (each = 1 search), `est_cost_usd` is indicative only, and the `paused_budget` state is dropped (the entity cap IS the scope). `researchJobMaxEntitiesHardCap=500`. VERIFY: build/vet clean; real-PG `TestResearchJob_CRUD_RealPG` (kind-validate, live count ignores soft-deleted, insertâ†’load round-trip, list, one-live unique constraint, slot-frees-on-complete). **M2:** in-process worker draining `pending` jobs (reuse glossary's `internal/events/*` background pattern), skip-already-researched idempotency, `cursor_entity_id` crash-resume, pause/resume/cancel endpoints, cost-metering, live web-search smoke. **M3:** `ResearchJobCard` FE + launch action on the kind + polling + i18n أ—4. Full design (with superseding M1 note): [2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md](../specs/2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md).
> - ~~`D-BATCH-RESEARCH-JOB` (original design-checkpoint note)~~ â€” **DESIGNED 2026-06-25 (gate 2: large/structural, XL cross-cutting).** The research-orchestration item was scoped: wiring it as a **synchronous plan op** is the wrong vehicle (the `execute_plan` executor runs in-request â†’ a paid N-entity web-search fan-out would be minutes-long, timeout-prone, no progress/cancel; AND deep-research's "agent summarizes the attached evidence" design has no agent in the deterministic executor). **Decision (user, 2026-06-25): build it as an async batch-research JOB** hosted in **glossary-service** (it owns entities/evidence/the BYOK web-search; glossary already runs in-process `internal/events/*` consumers â€” no need to proxy to knowledge like wiki does). Full design: [2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md](../specs/2026-06-25-glossary-batch-entity-research.md) â€” `entity_research_jobs` table + one-live-per-(book,kind) lock, a knowledge-modeled state machine (pendingâ†’runningâ†’paused_budget/paused_userâ†’complete/failed/cancelled) with a per-search `max_spend` ceiling + cursor crash-resume, a `researchOneEntity` refactor that shares INV-6 web-search/neutralize/attach with the existing sync `glossary_deep_research`, 6 owner/grant-gated endpoints + pre-flight cost estimate, and a `ResearchJobCard` FE. Phased M1 (core+schema+endpoints) â†’ M2 (worker+lifecycle) â†’ M3 (FE). Entity create/edit/rename remain **out** (UUID-addressed, names ambiguous â€” GUI/EAV only).

> # â–¶â–¶ (prior epic) **Multilingual KG epic COMPLETE (M1â€“M7)** آ· branch `feat/kg-multilingual` آ· 2026-06-24 â€” ready to PR

> **EPIC: canonical-source + derived-localization multilingual KG.** Design fully clarified + red-teamed + gate-validated. Spec [2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md](../specs/2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md) آ· detailed design [2026-06-23-kg-multilingual-detailed-design.md](../specs/2026-06-23-kg-multilingual-detailed-design.md) آ· plan (7 milestones) [2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md](../plans/2026-06-23-kg-multilingual.md). Design commit `1a448661`.
>
> **Core decisions (locked):** Layer-1 graph stays source-language, NEVER re-extracted from translations; derive a localization layer (L2 labels) + language-aware retrieval (L3) on top. vi content is **index-only + label-only**. **Gates:** V1 PASS (bge-m3 genuinely multilingual â†’ single-vector-space premise holds), V7 PASS-caveat (reranker BYOK-optional â†’ D5 must work WITHOUT it), V6 FAIL (lexical leg not CJK-tokenized â†’ D12 real). D5 uses **soft language-preference weighting** (not hard filter â€” robust to partial translation + dodges Neo4j post-filter limit).
>
> **âœ… M1 FOUNDATION SHIPPED (this session) â€” `source_lang` tag + cost metering + skip-gate.** All additive + dormant (source_lang unread until M4); no behavior change to existing retrieval.
> - **`source_lang`/`mixed`/`content_hash` on `:Passage`** (Neo4j-only â€” DD1: passages have NO Postgres mirror) â€” model + Cypher ON CREATE/MATCH + `upsert_passage` ([passages.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/db/neo4j_repos/passages.py)). New helpers `set_source_lang_for_source` (tag-only backfill) + `get_source_ingest_state` (skip-gate read: hash+canon+chapter_index).
> - **Ingester** ([passage_ingester.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/extraction/passage_ingester.py)): `resolve_source_lang` (declared chapter `original_language` wins, else `detect_primary_language`, "mixed"â†’flag); **content-hash skip-gate** (skip embed+re-bill ONLY when text+canon+chapter_index all match â€” a draftâ†’publish canon flip or reorder still re-ingests); **cost metering** via existing `record_spending` (DD2 â€” fixes the pre-existing embed-token leak; `pool` threaded through the publish handler + backfill); `backfill_source_lang` (tag-only, per-chapter declared lang). `backfill_published_passages` now passes declared `source_lang` + `pool`; both wired at PUT embedding-model ([extraction.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/extraction.py)).
> - **book-service** already returns `original_language` in `/internal/books/{id}/chapters` (server.go:2244) â†’ no Go change; `BookClient.list_chapters` surfaces it verbatim.
> - **VERIFY:** knowledge-service unit suite **3017 passed** (source_lang/cost-metering/skip-gate tests + regression tests for canon-flip, chapter reorder, model-change, regional-subtag). **LIVE-SMOKE deferred â†’ folds into M2's mandatory cross-service smoke** (M2 dual-indexes vi passages through this same ingest path + asserts source_lang) â€” `D-KG-ML-M1-LIVE-SMOKE`.
> - **/review-impl (cold-start adversary) â€” 1 HIGH + 2 MED FIXED, 2 LOW accepted:** **HIGH** â€” the skip-gate ignored `embedding_model`; since the model-set path's `_delete_project_graph` deletes only graph labels (`Entity/Event/Fact/ExtractionSource`), NOT `:Passage`, a model change would have skip-gated re-embedding â†’ stale-dimension vectors. Fixed: `get_source_ingest_state` returns `embedding_model` + the skip condition compares it (re-ingests on model change). **MED** â€” draft-index path (`raw_search.py`) didn't meter embed spend â†’ now passes `pool` (guarded); + `source_lang` now normalized to ISO-639-1 primary subtag (`zh-CN`â†’`zh`) so M4 ranking won't drift. **Accepted+documented (LOW):** `get_source_ingest_state` LIMIT-1 is safe (one ingest writes identical hash/canon/index/model to all chunks); `record_spending` best-effort silent-fail is intentional.
>
 **âœ… M2 EVENT + DUAL-INDEX SHIPPED (this session).** A chapter's ACTIVE translation is now dual-indexed as `source_lang`-tagged `:Passage` nodes â€” index-only (NEVER re-extracts Layer 1, R1).
> - **Producer (translation-service):** `translation.published` outbox event (`aggregate_type='translation'` â†’ `loreweave:events:translation`) emitted at ALL 3 active-version chokepoints (DD5): manual `set_active_version`, human-block-edit `patch_translation_block`, and the auto-promote in `chapter_worker._finalize_chapter` (the one that emitted NOTHING before â€” only when the promote actually changed the active row). One envelope via [translation_events.py](../../services/translation-service/app/translation_events.py).
> - **vi-text retrieval:** new internal `GET /internal/translation/chapters/{chapter_id}/active-text?target_language=` ([main.py](../../services/translation-service/app/main.py)) returns the active version's flat text (translated_body, else assembled from translated_body_json) â€” the translated text lives in translation-service, NOT book-service.
> - **Consumer (knowledge-service):** new [translation_client.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/clients/translation_client.py) + `handle_translation_published` ([handlers.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/events/handlers.py)) resolves project by book_id â†’ fetches vi text â†’ `ingest_chapter_passages(text_override=vi, source_lang=vi, canon=True)`. Registered: stream `loreweave:events:translation` ([consumer.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/events/consumer.py)) + dispatcher ([main.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/main.py)).
> - **Language-scoped passage identity (DD1):** `passage_canonical_id` now includes `source_lang` so vi/zh chunks of the SAME chapter (shared `source_id=chapter_id`, kept clean for hitâ†’chapter mapping) are DISTINCT nodes; `delete_passages_for_source` + `get_source_ingest_state` gained a `source_lang` filter so re-ingesting vi never wipes zh (and vice-versa). vi uses `source_type='chapter'` â†’ the existing retriever finds it (M4 adds language ranking). Empty/'unknown' lang keeps pre-M2 ids byte-identical.
> - **VERIFY:** knowledge unit **3022 passed** (canonical-id distinctness, text_override bypass, handler dual-index/skip-no-text/skip-no-project); translation-service **137 relevant + 2 emit-envelope** tests pass, py_compile clean.
> - **âœ… LIVE-SMOKE PASSED (2026-06-24, on the running stack) â€” clears `D-KG-ML-M2-LIVE-SMOKE` + `D-KG-ML-M1-LIVE-SMOKE`.** Target: Dracula book `019ef35c-36c3-â€¦` / chapter `019ef35c-3f4c-â€¦` (active vi translation, knowledge project `019ef35e-d08f-â€¦`, embed model `019e7f71-â€¦`). **(1) Endpoint:** `active-text?target_language=vi` â†’ 5939 chars assembled from `translated_body_json`. **(2) Event path:** inserted `translation.published` outbox row in `loreweave_translation` â†’ worker-infra relay (`published_at` set) â†’ `loreweave:events:translation` â†’ `knowledge-extractor` group â†’ handler â†’ **6 vi `:Passage`** created (was 0; the 6 originals are `source_lang=null`). **(3) Data model (DD1):** vi passages carry `source_lang='vi'`, clean `source_id=chapter_id`, `embedding_1024` (dim=1024, V1), `canon=TRUE`, `content_hash` set. **(4) Idempotency:** re-emit (relayed + consumed, lag 0) â†’ still **6** vi passages, no dupes/re-embed. **âڑ  The smoke caught a real defect mock-VERIFY can't:** the deployed knowledge-service image was **stale** (cached Docker `COPY` layer) â†’ running consumer subscribed to only 3 streams (no `translation`) â†’ the event would've been silently dropped. Fixed via `--no-cache` rebuild + `--force-recreate`; confirmed the consumer now subscribes to all 4 streams. (Cost-metering `record_spending` is best-effort/non-blocking â€” lightly covered live; phantom-pricing tracked separately in `D-KG-ML-EMBED-PRICING`.)
>
> **âœ… M3 READER-LANGUAGE STORAGE SHIPPED (this session) â€” book-service only.** Per-(user,book) reader-language preference, server SSOT + cross-device (DD3).
> - **Table** `user_book_prefs(user_id, book_id, reader_language, updated_at, PK(user_id,book_id))` in book-service schema ([migrate.go](../../services/book-service/internal/migrate/migrate.go)) â€” no FK (matches `reading_progress`; existence gated at handler).
> - **Public endpoints** `GET/PUT /v1/books/{book_id}/reader-language` ([reader_language.go](../../services/book-service/internal/api/reader_language.go)) â€” view-gated via `canViewOrPublic` (grant OR public book, so a reader of a shared/public novel keeps a pref; non-grantee private book â†’ 404, no oracle). PUT validates a BCP-47-ish tag (`langTagRe`, â‰¤35 chars); empty/whitespace â†’ clears (delete â†’ null). NOT localStorage (CLAUDE.md). Registered NOT in the mutating-grant table (per-user data = view-tier write, like favorites); GET added to `grant_mapping_test` readRoutes.
> - **Internal resolver** `GET /internal/books/{book_id}/reader-language?user_id=` (token-gated, user_id param like `getBookAccess`) â€” the cross-service source for M4 retrieval + M7 consumers. The FALLBACK chain (reader-pref â†’ detected query lang â†’ source lang) lives at the CONSUMER (M4) where query+source langs are known â€” M3 only exposes the stored value.

Full book-service suite green â€” also fixed a **pre-existing** stale `config.TestLoadValidation` (didn't set the now-required `CONFIRM_TOKEN_SIGNING_SECRET`; one-line, same-service, fix-now).

> **âœ… M4 LANGUAGE-AWARE RETRIEVAL SHIPPED (this session) â€” D5 soft-weighting (cross-service: book + knowledge).** The core user-facing win: a reader's language preference softly re-ranks hybrid search so they get content in their language first.
> - **Both legs carry `sourceLang`:** semantic from the passage prop ([retriever.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/search/retriever.py) `passage_to_hit`); lexical from the chapter's `original_language` â€” book-service `lexical-search` now selects `c.original_language` in all 3 SQLs + threads it through `buildLexicalHit` ([search.go](../../services/book-service/internal/api/search.go)).
> - **Soft language ordering, [hybrid_fusion.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/search/hybrid_fusion.py) `apply_language_preference`** (revised by /review-impl â€” see below): a **stable matched-first partition** applied as the FINAL ordering pass (post-rerank, post-floor, pre-cap). Reader-language hits move to the front, preserving the upstream rerank/RRF order within the matched + unmatched groups; nothing is dropped (soft, robust to partial translation). `"mixed"` matches any pref. `normalize_lang` = ISO-639-1 primary subtag (mirrors M1 `resolve_source_lang`). Scale-independent (no weight to tune).
> - **ًں”ژ /review-impl â€” 2 HIGH FIXED (both masked by the original smoke):** (1) the boost was **inert under rerank** â€” applied pre-rerank, but `pool_n â‰¥ len(fused)` always, so the cross-encoder re-sorts the whole list by relevance and discards it â†’ moved the language step to the FINAL pass + made it scale-independent (the additive `w_lang`/`settings.lang_pref_weight` is removed). (2) the RRF fusion key `_hit_key` **omitted `sourceLang`**, so a chapter's vi + zh/en passages (same `chapterId`+`surface`+`chunkIndex`) collided and one language was dropped BEFORE ranking â†’ added `sourceLang` to `_hit_key` (mirrors `passage_canonical_id`/DD1). Both have regression tests (rerank-on ordering; both-languages-survive-fusion). Re-smoked live (rerank default-on path): `language=en`â†’6 en then 6 vi (**n=12, both survive**); `language=vi` & reader-prefâ†’6 vi then 6 en.
> - **Resolver chain** ([raw_search.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/routers/public/raw_search.py)): `?language=` â†’ the CALLER's stored reader-language (M3, via new `BookClient.get_reader_language` â†’ `/internal/books/{id}/reader-language`) â†’ `detect_primary_language(query)` â†’ None. It's the SEARCHER's pref (caller), not the resolve-to-owner project user. `run_hybrid_search` gained `pref_lang` (None default â‡’ wiki in-process path unchanged).

(Confirmed the M2 dual-index is bug-free: a clean re-index yields exactly 6 vi; an earlier "12 passages" was smoke-data pollution from consumer replay-from-0 + repeated manual emits, now reset.)

> **âœ… M6 SHIPPED (this session) â€” CJK lexical leg via a Neo4j `cjk` full-text index (D12 / V6).** A short Chinese/Japanese/Korean keyword (the pg_trgm-FAIL case) now recalls the right passage.
> - **V6 probe (decisive):** `db.index.fulltext.listAvailableAnalyzers()` â†’ Neo4j ships a built-in **`cjk`** analyzer (bi-grams); Postgres `pg_available_extensions` â†’ only `pg_trgm` (NO `zhparser`/`pg_jieba` â†’ those would need a custom image = infra change). **Decision (per the plan's "pick by probe + infra cost"): Neo4j `cjk`** â€” zero infra change, and `:Passage` already carries `source_lang` (M1/M2). The book-service Postgres `language`-param/`pg_jieba` route from the plan is therefore moot; book-service trigram stays as the script-agnostic fallback (its `ILIKE` substring still finds exact CJK, just unranked/unindexed).
> - **Schema:** `CREATE FULLTEXT INDEX passage_text_cjk_ft â€¦ OPTIONS {indexConfig:{fulltext.analyzer:'cjk'}}` over `:Passage(text)` ([neo4j_schema.cypher](../../services/knowledge-service/app/db/neo4j_schema.cypher), idempotent, applied on startup).
> - **Repo:** `find_passages_by_fulltext` + `lucene_escape` + `PASSAGE_CJK_FT_INDEX` ([passages.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/db/neo4j_repos/passages.py)) â€” global FT index â†’ oversample + post-filter on `user_id`/`project_id`/`source_lang`/`canon` (mirrors `find_passages_by_vector`); raw query Lucene-escaped (CJK runs kept) so a stray `+`/`:` can't break the parse; blank â†’ no call.
> - **Retriever:** new `query_has_cjk` (codepoint test â€” a bare proper noun is too short for language detection) + a `_cjk_lexical` leg ([retriever.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/search/retriever.py)) fused as a THIRD RRF input, gated on `mode!=semantic AND query_has_cjk`. **Additive â€” empty for Latin queries, so non-CJK retrieval is byte-identical to pre-M6.** Best-effort (FT index missing / query error â†’ `degraded['cjk_lexical']`, never 500). Needs no embedding model (works on an un-embedded project).

> **Anti-drift interview-practice roleplay â€” the POC for the future roleplay-service â€” is DONE end-to-end (M1â€“M7).** Spec [2026-06-23-interview-roleplay.md](../specs/2026-06-23-interview-roleplay.md), plan [2026-06-23-interview-roleplay.md](../plans/2026-06-23-interview-roleplay.md). Brain-analogy anti-drift: `working_memory` = pinned **charter** (frozen goal, written only by the goal authority) + **state** (executive-rewritten, safe-when-wrong); `anchoring` = pin (primacy) + tail (recency); `executive` = out-of-band state updater. Goal-authority seam = the world-model swap point for roleplay later.

Route `/interview` + sidebar entry (en/vi nav). 9 FE tests; typecheck/lint clean.

# Session Handoff â€” `feat/extraction-knowledge-architecture` آ· **EXTRACTION RE-ARCH â€” FND+RE+PROV+OBS SHIPPED; MERGE/PLAN/CACHE remain** آ· (prior: design complete; `feat/glossary-assistant-coverage` â€” 26/26, PR #39) آ· 2026-06-22

> **â–¶ NEXT SESSION STARTS HERE â€” extraction re-arch epic COMPLETE. ًںژ‰ All 7 lanes + every follow-on shipped: FNDâœ… REâœ… PROVâœ… OBSâœ… MERGEâœ… PLANâœ… CACHE (slice1 cache-gate / 3a retention / 3b replay / 3c MinIO offload)âœ… + `D-CACHE-PLANNER-WIRING` Part 1 (split-aware cost estimate)âœ… + Part 2 (planner feasibility gate)âœ…. `D-SDK-DISTRIBUTION-SPLIT` RESOLVED (PR #41 â†’ main; planner ships in the rebuilt runtime â€” live-verified in-container). Nothing extraction-epic remains â€” even `D-EXTRACTION-FND-E2E-SMOKE` is closed (the unplannable-path live E2E ran green on the real stack; both translation images rebuilt `--no-cache`). NEXT = a NEW track: either the KG-ontology consolidated live E2E (the â–¶ block below) OR the architecture-audit Crit/High backlog (see `docs/analysis/`, memory `architecture-audit-2026-06-20`). Ask the user which.**
> - **âœ… D-CACHE-PLANNER-WIRING Part 2 â€” planner feasibility gate (commit `a37c0527`; DESIGN [2026-06-22-planner-executor-wiring-part2.md](../specs/2026-06-22-planner-executor-wiring-part2.md), Option B).** KEY REFRAME: `plan()` is a token-budget STRUCTURE planner (estimates, not text payloads) â†’ it CAN'T replace the windower (which makes prose); its value is the `Unplannable` signal. So Part 2 wires it as a **pre-flight feasibility GATE** over the existing windowing, NOT a replacement: build a Unit per (windowأ—batch), run `plan()`, and any unit it refuses (an oversized block that can't fit even alone) â†’ record a new `unplannable` batch-outcome + **skip its LLM call** (no truncation, no tokens) â†’ chapter derives `completed_with_errors` (INV-F15) so the block is VISIBLE. The unit id IS the `{window_idx}:{batch_idx}` coordinate â‡’ **zero remap** onto the cache-gate/OBS/provenance seams (my earlier "structural XL" fear was wrong â€” the gate touches none of them). Fires only when input won't fit with the minimum output reservation = exactly when the executor would truncate (no false-positive skip â€” a flagged window couldn't extract anyway). Best-effort: any planner failure â†’ ungated (prior path). New OBS taxonomy `unplannable` (distinct from `truncated`). VERIFY: 83 extraction unit tests + **FULL LIVE E2E** (`D-EXTRACTION-FND-E2E-SMOKE` â€” see below); provider-gate clean.
> - **âœ… D-EXTRACTION-FND-E2E-SMOKE (unplannable path) â€” DONE live on the real stack.** Auth (test acct) â†’ created a 348k-char single-block chapter in the Dracula book â†’ `POST /v1/extraction/.../extract-glossary` â†’ worker â†’ **`extraction_batch_outcomes.status='unplannable'`** + chapter `completed_with_errors`, job done in <1s with **NO LLM call**. Clean before/after: the SAME job on the pre-gate image produced `llm_error` (the oversized window hit `LLMInvalidRequest` context-overflow â€” the exact S1â€“S5 failure); the gated worker produced `unplannable` (graceful skip). **GOTCHA caught (memory `docker-stale-copy-layer-false-smoke`):** the first rebuild served a STALE app COPY layer (old code, no gate) while the SDK layer was fresh â€” so my initial "in-container `plan()` snippet" smoke was a FALSE pass (it tested the SDK, not the worker's gate). Fixed with `build --no-cache` + `up --force-recreate`; re-verified the gate code is in the container before re-running. Both translation images are now rebuilt `--no-cache` (current code live). Test chapter cleaned up. The broader full-pipeline-WITH-entities E2E (needs a live LLM producing entities) remains nice-to-have, not blocking.
> - **âœ… CACHE D-CACHE-PLANNER-WIRING Part 1 â€” split-aware cost estimate (commit `30c7549d`).** `estimate_extraction_cost` ([extraction_prompt.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_prompt.py)) now models each (chapter أ— kind-batch) as a `chunk`-splittable planner Unit (`max_units_per_call=1` so the per-unit input sum == real per-call input â€” extraction injects window text PER batch call, never shared) and splits oversized chapters into windows, so the quote tracks the executor's real fan-out (+ surfaces `unplannable`/`model_fit_warning`). Extracted the worker's context resolver into shared [extraction_model.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_model.py); the HTTP route + MCP start-extraction tool resolve the REAL model context and thread it in (so the quote uses the SAME budget the executor will). ANY planner failure (ImportError or plan() error) degrades to the flat heuristic â€” a cost estimate must never 500 job creation. VERIFY: 4 cost + worker 11 + mcp 36 + extraction subset 32 green; provider-gate clean.
> - **âœ… CACHE Slice 3a â€” retention (commit `36193284`, not pushed).** `purge_stale_raw_outputs` ([extraction_cache.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_cache.py)) keeps the latest K content-hash GENERATIONS per (owner, book, chapter) + purges older (cache growth bounded by edit-history depth, not edit count) + `POST /internal/translation/extraction-cache/retention` (the scheduler seam). Destructive-op safety: current gen is always rank-1 (never purged even at keepâ†’floored-1); rows join on content_hash so a gen is never half-purged; DENSE_RANK over-keeps on ties; partition keyed by owner_user_id (INV-9). VERIFY (live PG): keep-K / scope / tenant-isolation test; 66 extraction tests green; provider-gate clean.
> - **âœ… CACHE Slice 3b â€” replay (commit `0f097422`, not pushed).** New [extraction_replay.py](../../services/translation-service/app/workers/extraction_replay.py) `replay_chapter_from_cache` re-drives the glossary writeback from a chapter's CACHED parse at **$0 LLM** (recovery: failed writeback / cleared entities / re-materialize) + `POST /internal/translation/extraction-cache/replay`. Faithful-by-construction: proceeds only when CURRENT text still hashes to the cached `content_hash` AND the source job's profile still hashes to the cached `profile_hash` (else no_cache / profile_unavailable â†’ fresh extraction) â€” so re-stamped evidence offsets + links + attribute-actions are all valid. <details><summary>Design-complete handoff (FND now built â€” kept for context)</summary>

> **â–¶ extraction/LLM-pipeline re-architecture, BUILD via the parallel plan.** Branch `feat/extraction-knowledge-architecture` (off the glossary work). The 26-scenario test surfaced 5 architectural gaps; this session did the **full design**, now COMPLETE and build-ready:
> - **Design docs (committed):** [architecture rev 2](../specs/2026-06-21-extraction-pipeline-architecture.md) (آ§7â€“آ§11 = a 4-lens adversarial evaluation: concurrency/failure/scale/tenancy, with the scenario matrix + hardening contracts), [reasoning-effort](../specs/2026-06-21-reasoning-effort-control.md), [**detailed design**](../specs/2026-06-21-extraction-pipeline-detailed-design.md) (every open question RESOLVED â€” decisions table آ§1 + DDL آ§2 + interfaces آ§3 + 15 enforceable invariants آ§4), [**parallel plan**](../plans/2026-06-21-extraction-pipeline-plan.md).
> - **The 5 gaps â†’ one missing layer:** an explicit `PLAN â†’ EXECUTE+CACHE â†’ VALIDATE â†’ WRITEBACK â†’ OBSERVE` spine. P1 observability (services exist, unwired), P2 unified planner (scattered across 5+ modules), P3 evidence provenance (columns exist, unpopulated), P4 merge/append policy (no append, silent skips), P5 raw cache (spec'd, was deferred).
> - **Key design verdicts to honor in BUILD:** the **two-ledger model** (raw-output cache â‰  writeback-coverage log â€” else a failed writeback permanently masks un-landed entities = data-loss amplifier); **concurrency contract** (glossary has NO `UNIQUE(book,kind,name)` today â†’ concurrent jobs dup; need constraint + per-book advisory lock + transactional whole-chapter writeback + idempotency key + content-hash precondition); **batch-outcome taxonomy** (empty_valid â‰  validation_rejected â‰  truncated â€” the literal 26-scenario bug); **two-phase planner** (split-down, not just pack-up; extraction has no chapter-chunking); **provenance trust** (model offsets are INV-6 untrusted, validate not trust); **verified-clobber guard** (merge never overwrites human-verified); **tenancy** (owner_user_id + grant gate + INV-6 + redaction on every new table/event). 4 HIGH findings are design-blocking â†’ resolved in the detailed design.
> - **Build = 7 parallel lanes (plan آ§2):** FND (M0 finish_reason â†’ M1 concurrency/two-ledger â€” the blocker), RE (reasoning-effort + the wiring no-op fix), PLAN (planner SDK), OBS, PROV, MERGE, CACHE. Critical path FNDâ†’PROVâ†’MERGE; RE+PLAN start at minute 0. `extraction_handler.go` is the hot file â€” FNDâ†’PROVâ†’MERGE sequence on it, never concurrent.
> - **Gate decision (PO-reversible):** Option A â€” pipeline-first, build in translation+glossary now, cache behind an interface (physical re-home to knowledge deferred = `D-EXTRACTION-REHOME-KNOWLEDGE`). 3 tracked deferrals: that + `D-GLOSSARY-MULTIROW-ATTR-VALUES` + `D-RAWCACHE-MINIO-OFFLOAD`.
> - **First BUILD action next session:** start lane **FND** (M0 finish_reason propagation, then M1) + lane **RE** in parallel. The RE wiring fix (chat thinking toggle is a live no-op today) is a standalone user-visible win.

</details>

# â–¶ Dracula end-to-end JOURNEY verification (live, 2026-06-22) â€” steps 1â€“8 PROVEN; 9â€“11 remain

> **The real product scenario, driven live on the actual Dracula book** (`019eeb09`, test account) instead of clearing micro-deferrals. Journey: create book â†’ agent web-search+suggest ontology â†’ approve â†’ import â†’ extract glossary â†’ translate glossary+chapter to vi â†’ build KG â†’ build wiki â†’ enrich â†’ write chapter.
> - **âœ… 1 Create book / âœ… 2 Agent-suggested ontology (book-optimized: `vampire/hunter/victim/werewolf/witch/artifact` + defaults) / âœ… 3 Approve (12 kinds, 52 attrs adopted) / âœ… 4 Import (**4 published chapters now** â€” fetched the full public-domain novel from Gutenberg #345 since the repo ships only ch01, split it, bulk-ingested ch2â€“4 via `POST /v1/books/{id}/chapters/bulk`; a stray `OVERSIZED unplannable E2E` draft sits at sort 99) / âœ… 5 Extract glossary (30 entities) / âœ… 6 Glossaryâ†’vi (233 attr translations) / âœ… 7 Chapterâ†’vi (`vi/completed`).** All verified by live DB state, not handoff claims.
> - **âœ… 8 Build KG â€” DONE this session (was the key blocker).** Drove `POST /v1/knowledge/projects/{id}/extraction/{estimate,start}` live. **Neo4j now holds the Dracula graph: 65 Entity (Count Dracula, Jonathan Harker, Castle Dracula, Carpathians, Londonâ€¦), 17 Event, 3 Fact, 10 Passage, 15 ExtractionSource + 60 EVIDENCED_BY + 21 RELATES_TO** (job complete, 65 items, $0.06, gemma + bge-m3, scope=all). **ًں”´ BLOCKER SURFACED (real UX gap â†’ fix): build-KG is gated behind the K17.9 embedding benchmark, which had NEVER been run here** (0 benchmark runs). The non-obvious trip: `min_runs: 3` â€” a `runs=1` benchmark returns `passed:false` DESPITE perfect recall@3=1.0/mrr=1.0 (bge-m3 passes cleanly at runs=3; 1024-dim neg-control override 0.70 applied). The agent's `kg_build_graph` mints a token fine but extraction 409s `benchmark_missing` until the user benchmarks their embedding model â€” the journey never surfaces this prerequisite. Also: changing the project embedding model returns `extraction_status:disabled` + deletes prior nodes (vector-space reset). **`D-JOURNEY-KG-BENCHMARK-UX`** (tracked; ANALYSIS DONE â†’ [docs/specs/2026-06-22-kg-benchmark-ux-gap-analysis.md](../specs/2026-06-22-kg-benchmark-ux-gap-analysis.md)). **Verdict: primarily a DESIGN gap (an unreconciled contradiction) + 2 implementation gaps.** The benchmark WAS designed as a visible "Run benchmark" step gating Confirm (RAID brief `05_build-graph-gates.md`, UX review P0 KN-1/BL-16) and the FE wires that button â€” but: **(a) DESIGN contradiction** â€” the gate keys benchmark to `(user, project, embedding_model)` (extraction.py:696) and the FE button is on the REAL project, yet the runner REFUSES any project that already has chapter/chat/glossary passages (`runner.py:223-227` `not_benchmark_project`); all three can't hold once the project has content, so on a non-trivial project the UI's only remedy structurally can't clear the gate (the RAID brief scoped "NO backend changes" assuming the endpoint fit â€” it didn't). I only cleared it because change-embedding had just emptied the project. **(b) IMPL** â€” `core.py:159` short-circuits `runs<min_runs` BEFORE metrics â†’ `runs=1` returns `passed:false` at `recall@3=1.0` with no reason + a misleading FE "low-quality results" badge (FE always sends runs:3, so only API/agent callers hit it â€” i.e. exactly my drive). **(c) DESIGN** â€” a successful run leaves ~10 synthetic `benchmark_entity` passages, no cleanup (pollution). **(d) IMPL** â€” `kg_build_graph` warns but there's NO `kg_run_benchmark` MCP tool â†’ agent dead-end. **Fix breakdown:** R1 decouple benchmarkâ†’(user,embedding_model) on an auto-sandbox (root cause, needs a plan + contract change); R2 add `fail_reason` + stop FE mislabelling a perfect short run; R3 clamp/reject `runs<min_runs` on the interactive endpoint; R4 add `kg_run_benchmark` MCP tool (MCP-first). R2+R3 are fix-now-small; R1+R4 need a plan. **â–¶ BATCH-FIX BUILD (plan: [2026-06-22-kg-benchmark-ux-fix.md](../plans/2026-06-22-kg-benchmark-ux-fix.md)) â€” BACKEND M1â€“M3 DONE 2026-06-22 (commits 7f3502af, ccf52484, 0d8306e2, 17c89fa0):** **M1/R1** re-keyed the gate to `(user, embedding_model)` (`BenchmarkRunsRepo.get_latest_for_model`) + routed the run to a hidden per-(user,model) sandbox (`get_or_create_benchmark_sandbox`; migration `is_benchmark_sandbox`; list-excluded) â€” **live-proven:** ran the benchmark on the content-bearing Dracula project (65 entities) â†’ passed, **NO `not_benchmark_project`** (old code 409s), sandbox created with the fixtures, real project's `benchmark_entity` count stayed 10 (**zero new pollution**), sandbox absent from list API. **M2/R2+R3** `gate_failures()` named codes (`insufficient_runs` â‰  a quality fail) threaded to the response/to_json + the runner clamps `runs` up to `min_runs` â€” **live-proven:** `runs=1` â†’ `passed=True, runs=3, gate_failures=[]` (the perfect-but-failed trap is gone). **M3/R4** `kg_run_benchmark` MCP tool (handler + arg model + definition + shim; OWNER-only, direct, runs on the sandbox) â€” catalogue unit-green (TOOL_DEFINITIONS 26). Full knowledge unit suite **2988 green**; provider-gate clean. **REMAINING: M4 (FE) + M5 (contract)** â€” R2 badge copy (`insufficient_runs` â†’ "inconclusive, needs â‰¥3 runs" not "low-quality"), R5 `BuildGraphDialog` prerequisite checklist, R6 model-swap warning copy + the OpenAPI `gate_failures`/tool additions. The FE is isolated copy/checklist on a now-working backend; the journey's KG-build dead-end is ALREADY cleared by the backend.

Another un-surfaced journey prerequisite (like the KG benchmark) â†’ **`D-JOURNEY-WIKI-FLAG` âœ… FIXED 2026-06-22 (961536c9):** the consumer now ALWAYS runs (it's idle/free until a user-triggered, cost-gated job arrives â€” gating it off-by-default only produced silent eternal pends); spend stays bounded per-request (`max_spend_usd` + the cost-gated trigger), not a platform env. `wiki_gen_enabled` deprecatedâ†’default True, no longer gates the consumer. **Live-proven:** restarted knowledge-service with `WIKI_GEN_ENABLED=false` â†’ the wiki-gen consumer still started.

Now caught as a per-gap skip (runner.py). **(C) `D-JOURNEY-ENRICH-VERBATIM`** â€” the gen prompt said "use ONLY the excerpts"+"don't invent" but never "synthesize", so gemma safely **copied the source verbatim**, which our OWN anti-plagiarism gate then auto-rejected (self-defeating loop); both language templates now demand paraphrase/re-creation (generate.py). **(B) `D-JOURNEY-ENRICH-COST-UNITS`** â€” `spent=8409` is **8409 TOKENS not $8409** (the cap is token-denominated per the C1 PO ruling, but columns/logs say `*_cost_usd`); relabelled the log, the DB/API `*_usd`â†’tokens rename is structural (migration+FE) and tracked. **Live-proven (re-ran after the fix):** job **COMPLETED** (was `failed`), **4 proposals `review_status='proposed'` NOT `rejected`** â€” gemma now PARAPHRASES (Borgo Pass هœ°çگ†"è؟‍وژ¥و¯”و–¯ç‰¹é‡ŒèŒ¨ن¸ژه¸ƒç§‘ç»´ç؛³çڑ„ه±±é—´è·¯ه¾„" â€” synthesized, not the verbatim English source), clearing the gate. Confidence 0.30 is the H0 quarantine floor (awaiting human review), not a rejection.
> - **âœ… 11 Write new chapter â€” DRIVEN AGENT-SIDE 2026-06-22 via the new `composition_generate` MCP tool.** Through the gateway: `composition_create_work` â†’ `composition_outline_node_create` (chapter+scene; both kinds require a real book `chapter_id` per the `outline_chapter_required` constraint, so a new book chapter `019eef4c` was created first) â†’ `composition_generate` (propose, mints a `composition.generate` token) â†’ confirm (`/v1/composition/actions/confirm`) â†’ the confirm effect ran `engine.generate_chapter(persist=True)` IN-PROCESS â†’ **gemma drafted a full gothic chapter (real Stoker-voice prose) â†’ PERSISTED to the book draft (draft_version 2), status `completed`.** (`grounding=False`/`canon=skipped_no_cast` because the minimal scene carried no `present_entity_ids` â†’ voice-only grounding; a scene with cast would pull KG/glossary canon.) **The grounded cowrite ENGINE is now agent-reachable.** Step 10 was likewise re-driven agent-side via the new `lore_enrichment_auto_enrich` MCP tool (job `019eef45` â†’ 2 synthesized `proposed` proposals: Borgo Pass / Bistritz, paraphrased not verbatim).
> - **Scenario status:** the 2 tool-gaps are closed and BOTH new tools are real-execution-proven agent-driven on the Dracula book. **FULL FRESH-BOOK AGENT-DRIVEN RUN done 2026-06-22** (fresh book `019eef55â€¦`, KG project `019eef5dâ€¦`, all through the gateway MCP surface unless noted):
>   - âœ… **1 create** (`book_create`) آ· âœ… **2 ontology** (`glossary_web_search` + baseline adopted + `vampire`+`hunter` custom kinds via `glossary_propose_new_kind`â†’glossary confirm; verified in `book_kinds`) آ· âœ… **3 import** (`book_chapter_bulk_create` real Dracula ch.1 + publish) آ· âœ… **4 extract** (`translation_start_extraction` proposeâ†’confirm â†’ **26 entities**) آ· âœ… **5 translateâ†’vi** (`translation_start_job` proposeâ†’confirm â†’ completed).
>   - âœ… **6 KG build** â€” `kg_build_graph` proposeâ†’`/v1/kg/actions/confirm` â†’ **26 Entity nodes in Neo4j** for project `019eef5dâ€¦`, `extraction_status=ready` (benchmark via REST `benchmark-run` PASSED, embedding model via PATCH â€” config steps with no MCP surface). **ًں”´ NEW GATEWAY GAP `D-GW-XPROJECT-NOT-FORWARDED`:** project-scoped KG tools resolve the project from `X-Project-Id`, but **the ai-gateway federation DROPS `X-Project-Id`** â†’ through the gateway they 'no project in scope'; DIRECT to knowledge `/mcp` (X-Project-Id set) they mint fine. So KG/wiki were driven **direct on knowledge `/mcp`** (still MCP tool-calls; the new composition/enrich tools are immune â€” they take `project_id`/ids as explicit args). Re-confirmed `D-JOURNEY-KG-BENCHMARK-UX` (fresh project â†’ embedding set â†’ `extraction_status=disabled` until the benchmark runs; `kg_run_benchmark` MCP tool absent from the deployed gateway catalog).
>   - â—گ **7 wiki** â€” `kg_build_wiki` propose mints + the confirm reaches the effect, but **422 "no entities â€¦ extract the glossary first"**. **âڑ ï¸ڈ ROOT CAUSE CORRECTED 2026-06-22 + FIXED (M2 above, `D-WIKI-ENTITY-FREQ-GATE`):** this was NOT draft-vs-active. The `known-entities` Go handler IGNORES `status` and gates on `COUNT(chapter_links) >= min_frequency` (default **2**); the wiki effect called it at the default, so a single-chapter book (every entity freq 1) returned 0. Proven live: all 26 are `draft`, yet `status=active&min_frequency=1` â†’ 26 and `&min_frequency=2` â†’ 0. (The OLD Dracula book worked because it has 4 chapters â†’ freq â‰¥2, not because its entities were active.) Wiki resolver now passes `min_frequency=1`.
>   - âœ… **8 enrich** (`lore_enrichment_auto_enrich` â€” the NEW tool, **through the gateway**) â†’ real synthesized proposal on the fresh book once KG embeddings existed (e.g. Borgo Pass هœ°çگ†/features, `review_status=proposed`). First pass before KG produced 0 (retrieval needs the book embeddings the KG build creates). آ· âœ… **9 write** (`composition_generate` â€” the NEW tool â€” proposeâ†’confirm â†’ **real gothic chapter generated + persisted to the fresh book draft, draft_version 2**).

> **â–¶ NEXT SESSION â€” branch `feat/glossary-assistant-coverage`. Active work: the GENREآ·KINDآ·ATTRIBUTE RE-ARCHITECTURE (supersedes the old SS-5â†’SS-7 plan). Then the epic was re-architected: the lost-since-March primary model recovered â€” genre is a first-class TIERED level (system/user/book) alongside kind; attributes keyed by (kind أ— genre أ— code); System/User = standards (templates, no entities), a Book = a sovereign instance scaffolded from standards by copy-down; every reference is a PLAIN per-tier FK (no polymorphism). > - **ًں”¨ G1 â€” schema rebuild + seed BUILT (this session, additive).** `migrate.go` `UpGenreKindAttr` (12 new tables: `system/user/book_genres`, `system/user/book_kind_genres`, `system/user/book_attributes`, `book_kinds`, `book_active_genres`, `entity_genres` â€” plain per-tier FKs, gen_random_uuid PKs, `content_hash`/`source_ref`/`deprecated_at` for Sync+boundary-independence) + `SeedGenreKindAttr` (derives system standards FROM the seeded system_kinds: O3 genre vocab incl universal/xianxia/mystery, O4 universal-linkage on every kind, attrs lifted to (kind,universal)). Wired in main.go after UpUserKinds. **REFINEMENT vs plan: G1 is ADDITIVE** â€” legacy `genre_tags[]`/`genre_groups`/`system_kind_attributes` + entity-FK repoint + wiki/KG clear all move to **G4** (so each milestone's suite stays green; FE-broken-window still per R3). > - **ًں”¨ G2a+b â€” genre OpenAPI contract + genre-tier CRUD BUILT (this session, `f2901ec8`).** Authored the FIRST glossary REST OpenAPI `contracts/api/glossary-service/kinds_genres_attributes.yaml` (11 paths/11 schemas: genres + attributes + kind-genres; system read-only, user CRUD, attach-by-code; book/sync extend in G3/G5).
