Distinct from legacy per-book `genre_groups` (retires G4).
> - **ًں”¨ G2c â€” attributes + kindâ†”genre links BUILT (this session).** `attribute_def_handler.go`: system attributes READ-ONLY (`GET /system-attributes?kind_id=&genre_id=`) + user attributes CRUD (`/user-attributes`) with **attach-by-code** (kind_id/genre_id must be the caller's live user-tier rows â†’ 422 otherwise; `ownsUserKind`/`ownsUserGenre` predicates; content_hash computed in Go matching the seed's md5 format). `kind_genre_link_handler.go`: `/user-kinds/{id}/genres` list/PUT-set(replace, validates every id owned)/PUT-one/DELETE. VERIFY: 3 api tests green â€” SystemAttributes read-only+populated, **AttachByCodeAndTenancy** (system kind/genreâ†’422, Bâ†’A's kindâ†’422, dupâ†’409, B patch/delete A's attrâ†’404), **LinkTenancy** (PUT set, add/del, B's genreâ†’422, Bâ†’A's kind linksâ†’404); full `go test ./... -p 1` green (api 21.9s); gofmt clean.
> - **â–¶ G2 COMPLETE.** **ًں”¨ G3a â€” book adopt (copy-down) + ontology read BUILT (this session).** `book_adopt_handler.go`: `POST /v1/glossary/books/{book_id}/adopt {genres:[code],kinds:[code]}` copies the picked System genres/kinds (+ their kindâ†”genre links + attributes) into the book tier â€” `universal` always adopted+active (O4), `unknown` kind always adopted (E6), source_ref/source_hash recorded; idempotent (per-book `pg_advisory_xact_lock` + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING). `GET â€¦/ontology` = book-LOCAL single-tier read (book_* only, no system_*/user_* join â€” the spike's Moment B). Manage-gated adopt / View-gated read. VERIFY: 2 api tests green â€” CopyDownAndOntology (universal+unknown present, char 'name' attr book-local, idempotent re-adopt, adopt-more grows set, ontology==adopt) + NonOwnerForbidden (403 on both); full `go test ./... -p 1` green (api 21.5s); gofmt clean.
> - **ًں”¨ G3b â€” book-tier CRUD BUILT (this session, `D-GKA-BOOK-CRUD`).** `book_ontology_handler.go` + `book_ontology_patch.go`: the Manage workspace's editing surface for a book's adopted/native ontology. `/v1/glossary/books/{id}/ontology/{genres|kinds|attributes}` create/patch/delete + `PUT â€¦/active-genres` (matrix columns) + `PUT â€¦/kinds/{id}/genres` (matrix row); matrix read = the existing `GET â€¦/ontology` `kind_genres`. All writes **Manage-gated** (shared `requireBookManage`/`bookOntologyTarget`); **DELETE = soft `deprecated_at` cascade** (genreâ†’its attrs + active/link/entity_genres rows; kindâ†’its attrs + links â€” boundary independence + Sync). Book-native rows carry `source_ref=NULL`; attribute create enforces the **book-local FK guard** (kind_id/genre_id must be live rows of THIS book â†’ 422, blocks a cross-book ref the raw FK would accept). Contract extended (`book-ontology` tag, 11 paths incl. retroactive adopt/ontology, 11 schemas, `Forbidden` resp). Generic spec-driven PATCH plumbing (`scanPatchFields`/`applyBookUpdate`, table/col are constants â†’ no SQLi). **VERIFY:** 3 new api tests green â€” FullLifecycle (create/patch/delete all 3 levels + active replace-set + kind-genres row + cascade-on-delete asserts), NonOwnerForbidden (403 on every write), **EditCollaboratorDeniedManage** (an *edit* grantee reads /ontology=200 but every write=403 â†’ proves the gate is Manage, not mere auth); full `go test ./internal/api -p 1` green (22.8s); gofmt/vet/build/provider-gate clean. Single-service change (glossary + contracts) â†’ no cross-service live-smoke token needed. /review-impl-grade self-review folded a `requireBookManage` extraction (killed 7 `_=userID` + 4 duplicated preambles).
> - **ًں”¨ D-GKA-ADOPT-USER-TIER BUILT (this session, caller-scoped â€” PO chose (a)).** Adopt now merges **System â†’ the adopting CALLER's User tier**: for each picked code, the caller's user-tier row shadows the System one (user rows inserted FIRST, System with `ON CONFLICT (book_id,code) DO NOTHING` fills the rest). All 3 levels â€” genre/kind/attribute (+ kindâ†”genre links unioned). No new contract (ownerâ‰ˆcaller under the Manage gate; the person setting up uses their own customizations â€” arguably more correct than strict owner-scoping). `source_ref='user:<id>'` distinguishes the provenance; user genres/attrs carry their `content_hash`, user kinds get an inline md5 (no content_hash col); `is_hidden=NOT is_active`, `sort_order=0` (user_kinds has no sort_order). VERIFY: new `TestBookAdopt_UserTierShadowsSystem` (xianxia+universal genres + character kind + a 'name' attr all user-customized â†’ user-sourced; romance not customized â†’ system-sourced) + existing adopt/CRUD suites green; full `go test ./internal/api -p 1` 22.9s; gofmt/provider-gate clean.
> - **ًں”¨ G4 â€” DESTRUCTIVE CUTOVER DONE (this session, user-approved big-bang; built via a 9-agent Workflow + human foundation/verify).** The entity layer now lives on the BOOK tier. **Migration** (`migrate.go`): `UpGlossaryCutoverG4` (TRUNCATE entities CASCADE; repoint `glossary_entities.kind_id`â†’`book_kinds`, `entity_attribute_values.attr_def_id`â†’`book_attributes`; rewrite `recalculate_entity_snapshot` to book tier, source `'book'`) + `UpMergeCandidatesG4` (merge_candidates.kind_idâ†’book_kinds) + `UpGlossaryCutoverG4Cache` (restore cached_name/search_vector maintenance on the book-tier snapshot) + `UpGlossaryDropLegacyG4` (DROP `genre_groups`, `system_kind_attributes`, `system_kinds`/`user_kinds`.`genre_tags`). Wired LAST in main.go + the test chains. **Seed rewrite:** `SeedGenreKindAttr` now derives system_genres/kind_genres/attributes from `domain.DefaultKinds` in Go (no longer reads the dropped table/column). **Handlers retargeted to book-local:** extraction (loadKindMap/loadAttrDefMap[universal-genre DISTINCT ON]/createExtractedEntity/mergeExtractedEntity/findEntityByNameOrAlias/getKnownEntities/writeExtractionProfile[genre_groupsâ†’book_active_genres]), entity detail, glossary_translate, internalTranslationGlossary, listKinds (â†’system_kind_genres/system_attributes), and **O2** â€” `confirmSchema`/`createKindFromParams`/`createAttrDefFromParams` now mint into the **BOOK** tier (token-bound bookID, book-scoped, `errNotAdopted`â†’422; a user's assistant can no longer mutate the shared catalogue). Legacy per-book `genre_groups` routes/handlers RETIRED (`genres_crud.go`/`genres_handler.go`/`domain/genres.go` deleted). **E3 KG clear:** `reset_glossary_anchors` added to knowledge-service (manual maintenance fn, not auto-run). **Precondition:** a book MUST be adopted before entities can be created in it. **VERIFY (human-run, not trusted from agents):** full `go test ./... -p 1 -count=1` GREEN across api(53s)/events/migrate/sanitize/shortdesc; `go build`+`go vet` clean; no production request-path reader of the dropped objects remains (grep-verified â€” all refs are migration DDL/seed/comments/DefaultKinds struct); provider-gate OK; gofmt clean; knowledge entities.py compiles. **Dev-DB cutover NOT run** â€” it's a deploy step; only the throwaway `loreweave_glossary_test` was migrated.
> - **ًں”¬ /review-impl on G4 (this session) â€” found + fixed a CRITICAL data-loss bug.** The cutover's `TRUNCATE glossary_entities CASCADE` was UNGUARDED; migrations re-run every service boot (execGuarded has no ledger), so every production restart after cutover would have wiped ALL entities + wiki. Tests never surfaced it (they want a clean slate). FIXED: the TRUNCATE now runs only while the OLD `glossary_entities_kind_id_fkey` (â†’system_kinds) still exists â€” i.e. exactly once per DB, pre-cutover. Regression: `TestGlossaryCutoverG4_GuardPreventsRepeatTruncate` (migrate pkg, ephemeral DB: cutover â†’ seed entity â†’ re-run cutover â†’ entity SURVIVES + FK repointed). Other findings accept+documented: revision-restore would break on a pre-cutover snapshot (impossible under full-reset â€” cutover cascades entity_revisions); extraction on an un-adopted book silently skips all entities (`D-GKA-EXTRACT-UNADOPTED-GUARD`, LOW). Writer audit otherwise clean (createEntity was the only miss, fixed in 222786b4).
> - **ًں”¨ G5 â€” book SYNC (diff/apply) DONE (this session, + D-GKA-HASH-REFRESH resolved).** `book_sync_handler.go`: `GET /v1/glossary/books/{id}/sync/available` (View-gated diff â€” compares each adopted book row's frozen `source_hash` vs the live source's current content hash: `update_available` when divergent, `source_retired` when the source was deleted/purged; up-to-date rows omitted) + `POST â€¦/sync/apply` (Manage-gated, per-row `keep_mine`|`take_theirs`; `take_theirs` overwrites the SEMANTIC/hash surface [genreâ†’name; kindâ†’name+description; attributeâ†’name+description+field_type+is_required+options] + refreshes `source_hash`; `keep_mine` bumps `source_hash` only; validate-all-before-mutate + same per-book advisory lock as adopt; cross-book id â†’ 0 rows, no leak). **D-GKA-HASH-REFRESH fixed**: `createUserGenre`/`patchUserGenre`/`patchUserAttribute` now (re)compute `content_hash` (was never set on genres / never refreshed on patch â†’ sync was blind to user-standard edits). Contract: `sync` tag + 2 paths + 5 schemas. **VERIFY:** full `go test ./... -p 1` GREEN (api 42s + 5 new G5 tests: diff/apply all-choices [kind+attr take_theirs full-surface, genre keep_mine], source-retired, user-edit-refreshes-hash end-to-end, patchUserAttribute hash, View/Manage grant gates; events/migrate/sanitize/shortdesc); gofmt/vet/provider-gate clean; contract YAML valid. **/review-impl: 2 MED coverage gaps fixed in-loop** (kind take_theirs + attr full-surface were unasserted; patchUserAttribute hash untested) + invariant doc at `attrContentHash`. Single-service (glossary + contracts) â†’ no cross-service token needed.
> - **ًں”¬ /review-impl ALSO found + FIXED a pre-existing G4 PROD TIME-BOMB (this session).** The migration chain leaked a `system_kinds` `pg_attribute` slot **per run**: base `Up` did `ALTER TABLE system_kinds ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS genre_tags` (re-add) while G4e `DROP COLUMN genre_tags` removed it â€” Postgres never reclaims dropped-column slots without a table rewrite, so each service boot leaked one slot toward the 1600-column ceiling (after which the service can't start). It had already exhausted the shared test DB (1590 dropped slots â†’ blocked the events+migrate test packages). **FIX (`migrate.go`):** `genre_tags` FULLY RETIRED from the additive chain â€” removed from `system_kinds` CREATE + the re-add ALTER + the `unknown`-kind seed + `Seed()` insert, and from `user_kinds` CREATE; G4e's `DROP COLUMN â€¦ IF EXISTS` kept as a ONE-TIME legacy cleanup (no longer fights a re-add). `genre_tags` was write-only mid-chain (only `Seed` wrote it; `SeedGenreKindAttr` derives `system_kind_genres` from `DefaultKinds` in Go, never read the column; handler `genre_tags` are output aliases from `system_kind_genres`). **PROVEN:** repeated full-suite migration runs now hold the dropped-slot count steady at 1590 (no growth) and the full suite is green; a FRESH DB from the fixed code carries 0 dead slots.

> - **âœ… SYSTEM-TIER-ADMIN + ADMIN CMS DONE + LIVE-SMOKED (`D-GKA-SYSTEM-TIER-ADMIN` + `D-GKA-SYNC-HASH-ON-ADMIN-EDIT`, 2026-06-20; commits `3d1763e3` Lane A, `f90cb1fc` Lane B+C; pushed).** The admin write surface for the platform System tier + a separate thin CMS to drive it. Built fan-out (BE myself for the auth boundary; 2 background agents scaffolded+wired the CMS). **Lane A â€” glossary admin API (`3d1763e3`):** `requireAdminScope` verifies an RS256 admin JWT via shared `contracts/adminjwt` (`ADMIN_JWT_PUBLIC_KEY_PEM`, base64-or-PEM), scope `admin:write`; POST/PATCH/DELETE for `system-genres`/`system-kinds`/`system-attributes-admin` with `content_hash` recompute (clears `D-GKA-SYNC-HASH-ON-ADMIN-EDIT`); `universal`/`unknown` guarded un-deletable. A valid HS256 USER token is rejected (RS256-only) â†’ a regular user can never mutate System tier. Unset key â†’ 503 fail-closed. Test: `TestSystemTierAdmin_*` (CRUD, 401 user-token, 403 scope, 503 unconfigured, hash recompute). **Lane B â€” auth admin-session exchange (`f90cb1fc`):** `POST /v1/admin/session` â€” a logged-in user who is an ACTIVE `admin_principal` SELF-mints an RS256 admin JWT (subject from their own token, authority only from the DB row; non-admin 403, disabled 404, rate-limited, audited). New `authjwt.LocalKeySigner` = production-safe in-process RSA signer for DEV/self-hosted (no KMS); `main.go` selects KMSâ†’localâ†’disabled; `AdminIssuanceEnabled` now true for either path. Gateway proxies `/v1/admin`â†’auth. Tests `TestAdminSession_*`. **Lane C â€” `cms-frontend/` (NEW, separate from `frontend/`):** Vite+React+TS+Tailwind thin admin UI on :5175â†’gateway `/v1`; login does the admin-session exchange + stores the admin token; Genres/Kinds/Attributes CRUD panels wired to the admin endpoints (13 vitest; tsc+build green). **infra:** compose passes admin keys through (disabled by defaultâ†’fail-closed) + a `cms-frontend` service; `gen-admin-dev-keys.ps1` mints a dev keypair; auth+glossary Dockerfiles now `COPY contracts/adminjwt`. **LIVE-SMOKED** (`admin-cms-live-smoke.ps1`, full stack, dev local-RSA â€” auth signs + glossary verifies, **kid matched** `bd90034eâ€¦`): login â†’ `/v1/admin/session` (role=admin) â†’ System genre CREATE/PATCH/DELETE through the gateway; plain USER token â†’ 401; non-admin â†’ 403. **/review-impl (auth boundary, no HIGH/MED):** user-token bypass impossible (RS256 vs HS256, proven), no self-escalation (authority from `admin_principals` only), scope-gated, key-identity-pinned (kid/iss/aud/exp), fail-closed everywhere. LOW/accept: adminSession doesn't check session-revocation (consistent with every access-token endpoint), break_glass not distinguished (pre-existing R3), admin token in CMS localStorage (15m TTL, re-exchanged on 401). **For a persistent dev CMS:** run `infra/gen-admin-dev-keys.ps1` once, export the two vars, `docker compose up -d --build auth-service glossary-service api-gateway-bff cms-frontend`, then seed an `admin_principals` row for your user. **Remaining GKA debt:** `D-GKA-G4-SEED-CLEANUP` â€” now CLEARED by the migration-ledger bullet below.

> - **âœ… MIGRATION LEDGER DONE â€” `D-GKA-G4-SEED-CLEANUP` CLEARED (2026-06-20, this session; L; plan [`â€¦migration-ledger.md`](../plans/2026-06-20-glossary-migration-ledger.md); NOT pushed).** Root-fixed the create-then-drop churn properly: the glossary chain was LEDGER-FREE (every idempotent step replayed each boot â€” most visibly CREATE+seedâ†’DROP of `system_kind_attributes` per boot; harmless recycle but pure churn, and entangled with the extraction_audit_log FK + the pre-cutover snapshot fn's JOINs so it couldn't be removed in isolation). New `internal/migrate/ledger.go` (`schema_migrations` table + `EnsureLedger`/`ApplyOnce`/`RunChain` over an ordered 29-step `chain` `0001_schemaâ€¦0029_glossary_drop_legacy_g4`) makes each step run **exactly once**; `main.go`'s ~155-line unconditional sequence collapses to one `migrate.RunChain`. **Purely additive â€” ZERO migration SQL changed**; every step keeps its own internal guard (the cutover's FK-existence TRUNCATE guard intact as defense-in-depth independent of the ledger). The 2 async background backfills stay OUT of the chain (non-blocking, self-limiting). **Behavior change (documented loudly in code):** seeds are now ledgered â†’ new `DefaultKinds` seed data needs a NEW chain entry, not auto-pickup on deploy. **VERIFY:** `go build`/`vet`/`gofmt` clean; **6 migrate tests** green (3 new: freshâ†’no-churn, idempotentأ—3, apply-once incl. failed-step-NOT-recorded; + the new **`TestRunChain_TransitionPreservesData`**; + 2 pre-existing rename/seed); api test pkg compiles. **LIVE transition smoke on the REAL un-ledgered dev DB** (`loreweave_glossary` â€” already cut over, no ledger): clean boot â†’ **29 ledger rows**, `system_kind_attributes` dropped, **2 entities / 13 system_kinds / 10 book_kinds PRESERVED** (cutover TRUNCATE did not re-fire); 2nd restart idempotent + healthy + ledger stable. **/review-impl: 1 MED + 1 LOW FIXED in-loop** â€” MED: `EnsureLedger`'s bare `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` ran OUTSIDE the migration advisory lock (concurrent-startup race â†’ possible `os.Exit(1)` on one replica; Postgres CREATE-IF-NOT-EXISTS isn't concurrency-safe) â†’ now routed through `execGuarded`; LOW: added the repeatable transition/data-loss regression test (was live-smoke-only). Accept+documented: seed-semantics footgun; no enforcement of append-only chain ordering (standard migration discipline, noted in the `chain` doc). >   - **Dirty semantics (doc):** `dirty` = the segment's SOURCE changed since last translate (or never translated for this lang). NOT glossary-staleness (that's `is_glossary_stale`, M3) and NOT translation-quality (a fallback-to-original block still reads "translated").

> - **Per-site fit analysis (why NOT a clean 7-site swap â€” the recorded B1 over-fit lesson, confirmed per-site):**
>   - `ChaptersTab` â€” **already paginated** (offset + `<Pagination>`) with a **richer DataTable** (language/status/updated/actions). Migrating = downgrade â†’ **skip.**
>   - `ReaderPage` TOC + `ChapterEditorPage` nav â€” need the **full ordered chapter list in memory** for prev/next + reading-progress, not a paginated UI. Their cap fix is "loop-fetch all", NOT this browser. **Misfit.**
>   - `TranslationTab` â€” chaptersأ—languages **matrix** (2D grid), not a flat list. **Misfit.**
>   - `ContextPicker` â€” **multi-book** picker; single-book browser doesn't fit directly. **Won't-migrate** (see cleared note below).
>   - `ChapterRangeStep` (campaign) â€” range-by-`sort_order` predicate (from/to numeric inputs + a count), not a list browser. **Won't-migrate** (see cleared note below). (StepBatchConfig **all/range** were fixed in /review-impl â€” see below.)
>   - **âœ… `D-CHAPTERBROWSER-RANGE-MODE` + `D-CHAPTERBROWSER-CONTEXTPICKER` CLEARED (2026-06-14, won't-fit + real-bug-fixed).** Per-site analysis confirmed neither is a `<ChapterListBrowser>` fit, so the "reusable range mode" / "per-book browser" component work is dropped as over-fit (the B1 anti-pattern). The one genuine harm behind RANGE-MODE â€” campaign `ChapterRangeStep` fetched `limit:5000`â†’clamped 100, so the displayed "N published / N selected" **count under-reported** on >100-ch books (the campaign scope itself is a server-side `chapter_from/to` predicate, never the fetched list â†’ no scope bug) â€” was fixed **in place** with a loop-fetch (no new component). `ContextPicker` requests `limit:100` per book (at the clamp, no silent-20 bug); first-100/book is acceptable for a chat context picker and a per-book browser is the wrong shape (it's a multi-book grouped picker) â†’ won't-fix.

The lock serializes concurrent migration DDL (uncontended/instant at prod startup). **Residual:** migration-DDL-vs-test-DML contention across packages is pre-existing parallel-suite flakiness (the unrelated `grantclient BaseURL` flake also surfaces under parallel runs) â†’ **run the glossary integration suite per-package or with `-p 1`**.

> - **`D-GLOSSARY-LIST-P1-FE`** âœ… **SHIPPED (M2 above)** â€” pagination + page-size + debounced search + sort-UI + raw-search toggle/highlights. `<EntityListBrowser>` extraction NOT done (folded into `D-GLOSSARY-BROWSER-PANEL-REUSE`).
> - **`D-GLOSSARY-SORT-BE`** âœ… **SHIPPED (M1 + M3)** â€” `entityOrderBy` whitelist (name/kind/status/alive/recency + raw-relevance) over `e.cached_name`; **counts-sort (links/evidence) shipped in M3** via denormalized trigger-maintained counters.

Add translation-sim ranking if users search primarily in their display language.
> - **`D-GLOSSARY-BROWSER-PANEL-REUSE`** âœ… **CLEARED â€” Won't-fix (2026-06-14, evidence-based).** Read all 3 panels: none fit `<EntityListBrowser>`. UnknownEntitiesPanel (`listUnknownEntities` triage queue, kind-reassign) + AiSuggestionsPanel (accept/reject, fixed `limit:200`) have NO search/sort/pagination and their endpoints don't support offset/sort; MergeCandidatePanel renders merge **clusters** with winner radios â€” not a flat entity list at all. The browser's reason to exist is the search/raw-toggle/sort/paginated-footer shell, which none of these need. Forcing it = bolting on no-op controls = the B1 "don't force-fit" anti-pattern (UX+code regression, zero value). If these queues ever need scale, the right move is a separate lightweight `ReviewQueue` shell for the 2 similar ones (Unknown+AiSuggestions) â€” NOT this one. Removed from backlog.

> **âœ… B1 (PARTIAL) â€” shared pagination primitives (M, one /loom, 2026-06-14).** User opted into building the deferred shared-chapter-browser epic NOW but scoped to the two non-RAID call-sites (chapter import review + translator modal). Built the genuinely-shared half (pagination) as generic primitives, leaving selection/row/toolbar per-call-site (they differ: import = exclude + title-edit; translator = status-badge + include). **Shipped:** `frontend/src/components/pagination/usePagedList.ts` (page state + clamped setPage + pageCount/start/pageItems) + `Pager.tsx` (controlled â—‚ Page[n]/N â–¸ with jump input, renders null for 1 page, i18n labels). Refactored `ChapterImportReview.tsx` + `TranslateModal.tsx` to compose them â€” removed the duplicated pager markup + slice/clamp in both. Translator's Prev/Next went icon-only (consistent w/ import; words â†’ aria-labels). **VERIFY:** FE tsc 0, vitest **32** (7 pagination + 4 modal + 2 import-review + 11 lib + 8 parse); FE-only, no service. **/review-impl: LOW-1** (no >1-page wiring test in either call-site) **FIXED** (added translator multi-page + import-review pagination tests); LOW-2 (Pager trusts caller's page in-range â€” hook is the contract) + COSMETIC (empty-jumpâ†’page1) accept+documented. **Full B1 still deferred** â€” selection-modes/filters/select-all-N unification waits for more call-sites (ExtractionWizard, ChaptersTab, ChapterEditorPage) + RAID clearing; forcing them now would over-fit the 2 current shapes.

> **âœ… TRANSLATOR WIZARD â€” smart chapter selection + large-book support (L, one /loom, 2026-06-14).** The `TranslateModal` requested `listChapters(limit:200)` â†’ book-service `parseLimitOffset` returned the default 20 (not a clamp) for `limit>100` â†’ wizard showed only 20 of N chapters; it also pre-selected ALL with no per-chapter status, so "2000 translated + 10 new" gave no way to target the 10. **Shipped:** (1) **BE clamp** `server.go parseLimitOffset` `limit>100â†’100` (was fallback-to-20) â€” fixes every chapter-list consumer (closes the B0 half of the deferred shared-browser epic). (2) **`coverageClassify.ts`** (new lib, 11 unit tests) â€” classifies each chapter for the target lang as `untranslated/translated/stale/failed/running` from the coverage matrix; `needsIds` = `untranslated âˆھ stale âˆھ failed`. (3) **`TranslateModal` rewrite** â€” on open fetches ALL active chapters (paged loop limit:100) + `getBookCoverage`, shows a **summary header** (total آ· per-status counts), a primary **"Translate N that need it"** (submits the needs-set, `force=false` â€” the idempotency gate skips fresh-completed), a **paginated** (100/pg, Prev/Next) classified list with per-row **status badge** + checkbox + **quick-select chips** (Needs/Untranslated/Stale/Failed/All/None); keeps the V3 verifier + `force_retranslate` toggle. Default selection on open / language-change = the needs-set (via callback, not useEffect). **VERIFY:** go build+vet 0, FE `tsc` 0, vitest **14** (11 lib + 3 modal); **live smoke** (rebuilt book-service, 105-ch book): `limit=200â†’100` (was 20), `limit=50â†’50`, `offset=100â†’5`. **/review-impl: MED-1** (`pending` queued chapters were classed untranslated â†’ the primary button would dup-submit; the gate only skips `completed` not pending/running) **+ LOW-1** (`fetchAllChapters` total-fallback could truncate to 1 page) **FIXED**; verified the coverage endpoint has **no pagination cap** (full accuracy on 2000+ ch). Accept/doc: the "All"/manual chips can still submit in-flight chapters with force=false (deliberate manual override, pre-existing). **Deferred B1 (shared `<ChapterListBrowser>`) stays open** â€” this was a focused TranslateModal optimization, not the cross-call-site component.

> **âœ… CHAPTER IMPORT UPGRADE â€” folder/large import (L, one /loom, 2026-06-14).** The old `ImportDialog` only took multi-file (no folder), didn't sort (FileList order â‰  numeric â†’ jumbled chapters), had no review, and uploaded `.txt` ONE FILE PER REQUEST (4232 files = 4232 requests = unusable). **Shipped:** (1) folder picker (`webkitdirectory`) + files picker + drag-drop, `.txt`â†’bulk path / `.docx`/`.epub`â†’existing async job; (2) **natural sort** by leading numeric filename prefix (`parseChapters.ts` `naturalCompare`, unit-tested); (3) **paginated review** (`ChapterImportReview.tsx`, page-through Prev/Next + jump, 50/pg, handles 4232) with per-row order#, **inline title edit**, size, **exclude** checkbox + select-all; title previewed client-side from the `ç¬¬Nç« ` header (BE re-derives, FE edit = override); files read with a progress bar; (4) **bulk endpoint** `POST /v1/books/{id}/chapters/bulk` (`bulkCreateChapters`, book-service import.go) â€” array of `{original_filename, content, title?}`, sequential FE batches of 100 â†’ monotonic `sort_order` (max+1) preserves order; title override else `extractChapterTitle` regex; publishes canon (parity w/ single import); **idempotent re-import** (skips existing `original_filename` â†’ resume-safe on partial failure), returns `{chapters_created, skipped_existing}`. **VERIFY:** go build/vet 0, FE tsc 0 + vitest 8 (sort/title/filter); **live HTTP smoke** on rebuilt book-service via gateway â€” order 1/2/3, `ç¬¬Nç« ` title parse + override, and idempotent re-import (2 created â†’ re-run 1 created / 2 skipped, total 3 no dupes). **/review-impl: MED-1 idempotency** (re-import duplicated on retry) **FIXED** (filename-skip). Tradeoffs: bulk = one scene-less chapter/file (no structural scene-split; draft body is canonical); `original_language='auto'` (matches single import). **GAP:** book-service `internal/api` has **no test harness** (0 test files) â€” covered by live smoke; building one is separate infra.
>
> **Deferred `D-C16-NULL-WORK-ROUTE` (RAID C16 â†’ C17, naturally-next-phase):** C16 makes `POST /work` survive a knowledge-service outage by persisting a Work with `project_id=NULL` + a backfill marker (writer not wall-blocked at setup), and the packer tolerates a null project_id â†’ empty grounding (deployed-path live-proven). BUT every generate/grounding/outline route is keyed on `{project_id}` in the URL (and `outline_node.project_id` is NOT NULL), so a STILL-PENDING null-project Work is not yet addressable for end-to-end Generate â€” it becomes fully draftable only after the **backfill** stamps a real project_id (live-proven: retry on recovery â†’ project stamped on the same row). Closing the "draft against a still-pending Work" gap needs an id-keyed Work-resolution route + outline-on-null handling = FE+routing work, owned by **C17 (writer flow polish / guided first-run)**. C16's locked scope is setup-resilience + backfill, which is met. (Adversary BLOCKER reclassified to this row â€” it is a scope boundary, not an in-scope defect; derivative-guard / 4xx-discrimination / backfill / migration all reviewed CLEAN.) **C17 UPDATE (2026-06-14):** C17 was FE-only and built the **happy path** (knowledge UP â†’ real project_id â†’ guided first-run). It guards the null-project_id case in FE (the guided setup `onSuccess` only auto-creates the first scene when `created.project_id` is non-null) but did NOT add the id-keyed BE route / null-outline handling â€” that needs a BE schema+route change, OUT of an FE-only cycle. **D-C16-NULL-WORK-ROUTE stays OPEN** (BE id-route + outline-on-null), now targetable at a future BE cycle. Also flagged this session: a **pre-existing unrelated working-tree build break in `frontend/src/pages/book-tabs/TranslateModal.tsx`** (duplicate `pageCount`/`safePage` from an incomplete `usePagedList` refactor) â€” blocks Vite HMR; NOT a C17 file, NOT staged in the C17 commit; needs a 1-line dedup next session.

> **Deferred â€” shared chapter-list browser + `limit>100â†’20` bug:** `docs/specs/2026-06-14-shared-chapter-list-browser-epic.md`. `book-service parseLimitOffset` returns the DEFAULT 20 (not a clamp) for `limit>100`, so TranslateModal(200)/TranslationTab(200)/ExtractionWizard(500)/ChapterEditorPage(200)/campaign ChapterRangeStep(5000) all silently get **20 chapters** â€” the "translator shows only 20" report. Fix = a 1-line clamp (B0) + a paginated `<ChapterListBrowser>` (B1) reused across ~8 call sites. **B0 SHIPPED** (2026-06-14 â€” `parseLimitOffset` now clamps to 100). **B1 PAGINATION HALF SHIPPED** (2026-06-14 â€” `components/pagination/{usePagedList,Pager}` extracted + adopted by import-review + translator-modal). **B1 remainder still deferred** (the full `<ChapterListBrowser>` with selection-modes/filters/select-all-N across the RAID-owned call-sites â€” composition/* + ChapterEditorPage). The import flow above is separate (client-side file list, not the server browser).

> **âœ… GLOSSARY BULK-ACTIVATE (M, one /loom, 2026-06-14).** Closes the latent trap from the translation work: extracted entities default to `status='draft'` and the translation-glossary query only returns `status='active'`, so a freshly-extracted book translates WITHOUT glossary until entities are activated â€” and there was no bulk way to activate them. **Shipped:** BE `POST /v1/glossary/books/{id}/entities/bulk-status` (`bulkSetEntityStatus`, entity_handler.go) â€” one UPDATE over `entity_id = ANY($::uuid[])`, book-scoped + `deleted_at` guard + edit-grant, validates statusâˆˆ{active,inactive,draft}, drops malformed ids, caps 1000; **no outbox** (status isn't in the wiki-staleness payload). FE multi-select in `GlossaryTab` â€” per-row checkbox + select-all (loaded) + Gmail-style **"select all N matching"** loop-fetch (so activation isn't capped at the 100-row page) + FloatingActionBar Activate/Deactivate; `glossaryApi.bulkSetStatus`. i18n أ—4. **VERIFY:** glossary BE 5 real-DB tests (activate-book-scoped, deactivate, bad-status 422, empty 400, no-auth 401), FE tsc 0 + vitest 70, go vet 0, provider-gate clean. **/review-impl: MED-1** (select-all silently capped at loaded 100 â†’ added select-all-N loop-fetch + action targets full selection) **+ LOW-1** (added inactive test) **FIXED**; verified pgx `[]uuid.UUID`â†’`uuid[]` encoding + cross-book isolation + chi route precedence live. Usage: Glossary â†’ filter status=Draft â†’ select all (â†’ "select all N") â†’ Activate. **NOTE:** the underlying GlossaryTab list still has no pagination UI (hard 100-row view); "select all N" works around it for bulk-status but browsing >100 is a separate pre-existing gap.

> **âœ… TRANSLATION PUBLISH/VERIFIER UX + AUTO-PROMOTE (L, one /loom, 2026-06-14).** Root-caused a "Vietnamese translation is terrible" report (book `019eb60eâ€¦`): the glossary layer was fine â€” the user was reading an **old qwen2.5-7b** version (15â€“41% leftover Han, wrong names) while a clean **gemma-4-26b** re-translation sat **unpublished**, because interactive single re-translation used `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` (only campaigns promoted) AND the publish UI was an **orphaned route** (no nav from the workspace). Investigation + plan: [`docs/specs/2026-06-14-translation-publish-verifier-ux-investigation.md`](../specs/2026-06-14-translation-publish-verifier-ux-investigation.md), [`docs/plans/2026-06-14-translation-publish-verifier-ux.md`](../plans/2026-06-14-translation-publish-verifier-ux.md). **Shipped:** (1) **auto-promote** â€” `chapter_worker.py` `_PROMOTE_ACTIVE_SQL` (extracted constant) now promotes a clean (`unresolved_high_count=0`) re-translation over an existing active version for **both** campaign + interactive, guarded so it **never clobbers a human edit** (`authored_by='human'` subquery in the `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE WHERE`); campaign + interactive paths converged. (2) **TranslateModal** verifier-model + qa_depth + max_qa_rounds (sends `pipeline_version='v3'` when enabled, since verify only runs in v3) + `force_retranslate` toggle (else the idempotency gate skips already-done chapters). (3) **TranslationTab** translated cells are now clickable â†’ `/books/:bookId/chapters/:chapterId/translations?lang=` (publish UI reachable). (4) **data fix:** book `019eb60eâ€¦` ch1 active â†’ gemma v4. i18n: 8 locale files (books+translation أ— en/vi/ja/zh-TW). **VERIFY:** translation **683** آ· FE tsc 0 + vitest **27**; **live smoke:** `_PROMOTE_ACTIVE_SQL` executed on real PG (`test_promote_active_pg.py`, all 4 branches: insert / llm-promote / human-skip / M5b-flag-skip â€” ran live, not skipped). **/review-impl: MED-1** (modal advanced state not reset on open â†’ stray force-retranslate) **+ LOW-1** (stale jobs.py skip-gate comment) **+ LOW-2** (guard had only SQL-string coverage â†’ added real-PG test + extracted SQL constant) **+ bug #5** (ChapterTranslationsPage's re-translate button was dead â†’ wired TranslateModal scoped to the chapter) **all FIXED**. Accept/doc: a human-*published* (not edited) llm version is still overwritten by auto-promote â€” matches the PO's "auto-promote except human-edited" choice.

# Session Handoff â€” `feat/glossary-assistant-coverage` آ· origin/main merged 2026-06-11

> **ًں§µ /warp PARALLEL WORKFLOW MODE (2026-06-12) â€” built + /review-impl'd + dry-run-validated; COMMITTED.** A new opt-in workflow mode: decompose a task into **provably-disjoint slices**, fan them out as `Agent(isolation:worktree)` sub-agents, reconcile at a **human-gated** junction. Fills the empty 2أ—2 cell (parallel + human-gated + lightweight; RAID is parallel-but-autonomous, loom/amaw are serial). **Spec** [`docs/specs/2026-06-12-warp-parallel-mode.md`](../specs/2026-06-12-warp-parallel-mode.md) (positioning آ· TRIAGE 2-stage gate آ· Slice Manifest + machine-checkable disjointness آ· reuse-from-RAID/amaw/loom map). **Built:** `.claude/commands/warp.md` (skill) آ· **safety spine** `scripts/warp/slice-manifest-validate.py` (pairwise path-prefix-disjoint write-sets + frozen-pin + reads-bounded) wired as gate verb **`python scripts/workflow-gate.py slices <manifest> [--verify-frozen]`** آ· `scripts/warp/worktrees.py` (Python worktree lifecycle â€” bash wrappers fail on this box) آ· `scripts/warp/slice-runner-prompt.md` آ· `docs/warp/EXAMPLE-manifest.yaml`. **81 tests.** **/review-impl found+fixed:** HIGH-1 (interior/partial globs gave FALSE-disjoint verdicts â€” `services/*/budget/**` vs `services/campaign/budget/**` read CLEAR â†’ now fail-closed) آ· MED-2 (`_is_dirty` git-error â†’ force-remove of uncommitted work) آ· MED-3 (case-insensitive-FS false-disjoint) آ· LOW-5 (CRLF parse). **Dry-run on a throwaway sandbox PASSED end-to-end** (2 isolated worktree agents â†’ branches persisted + visible â†’ `merge-tree` exit 0 with both files â†’ worktrees.py cleanup; fully torn down). **Finding D1 (fixed):** `isolation:worktree` bases each slice on a COMMITTED ref (HEAD), never the orchestrator's uncommitted edits â†’ `--verify-frozen` is now HEAD-based (BLOCKs an uncommitted frozen path) + warp.md requires commit-before-fan-out. **âœ… `D-WARP-REAL-TASK-VALIDATION` EXERCISED (2026-06-13) â€” surfaced + FIXED `D-WARP-WORKTREE-BASE-FLAKY`.** First real-task fan-out (`clear-defers-codeside`, 5 substantive code slices) FAILED: `Agent(isolation:worktree)` handed out **inconsistent bases** â€” slices 1 & 5 landed on a stale `main` (66 commits behind the committed DESIGN HEAD `e2370823`, missing M1-M5), slices 3 & 4 on the correct HEAD. A slice silently built against missing code. RAID never hit this because `worktrees-create.sh` pins the base explicitly; warp delegated base-selection to the opaque harness. **Fix (committed):** Coordinator captures `BASE_SHA` after the DESIGN commit; every slice's **Step 0** self-heals via new `worktrees.py pin-base --branch <BRANCH> --base <BASE_SHA>` (`git checkout -B <branch> <sha>` â€” immune to the harness base since all worktrees share the object store), unreachableâ†’`BLOCKED(base_mismatch)`; Coordinator re-verifies each DONE slice with `merge-base --is-ancestor`. **Proven against real git: `scripts/test_warp_base_pin.py` + 16 worktrees unit + 64 manifest-validate green.** warp.md + slice-runner-prompt.md + spec آ§10 updated.

**âœ… FULL REAL-TASK RE-RUN COMPLETE (2026-06-13) â€” slices 2-5 cleared + reconciled; surfaced + FIXED a 2nd warp bug.** Re-ran `/warp` fan-out (base `dbc419a9`); **all 4 slices pinned correctly to base (1 commit each, descended-from verified)** â€” the base-flakiness is gone. Reconcile = clean linear cherry-pick (ZERO conflicts, the disjointness dividend) â†’ **per-service suites green post-merge: lore-enrichment 884 آ· worker-ai 228 آ· translation 682 آ· chat 342 آ· provider-registry go build+vet OK.** Slice commits on feat: `8a801fe5` (s2 lore-enrichment race/sweeper/poison) آ· `a49911af` (s3 worker-ai SKIP-LOCKED; D-WX-RUN-SAMPLE-DECOUPLE was already done at base) آ· `edb9e551` (s4 translation flag-coupling/shell-tests/block-obs) آ· `feeec92c` (s5 composer-substream obs + cancel-log noise). **Cleared:** D-M2-COMPOSE-TASK-{RACE,SWEEPER,POISON} آ· D-WX-TRIO-FANIN-RACE آ· D-2B-{DECOUPLE-FLAG-COUPLING,SHELL-UNIT-TESTS,T3A-BLOCK-CHUNK-ROWS} آ· D-M3-COMPOSER-SUBSTREAM-OBSERVABILITY آ· D-CANCEL-FINALIZE-LOG-NOISE. **2nd warp bug found+fixed â€” `D-WARP-PINBASE-PRIMARY-CLOBBER`:** `pin-base`'s `git checkout -B` mutates whichever worktree the cwd resolves to; slice 2's isolation worktree was never created so it ran in the PRIMARY checkout â†’ yanked the main repo into detached-HEAD (recovered: salvaged the commit to its branch ref, restored feat). Fix: pin-base now REFUSES in the primary worktree (`--absolute-git-dir == --git-common-dir` â†’ exit 4 `wrong_worktree` â†’ slice returns BLOCKED), regression-tested (`test_pin_base_refuses_in_primary_worktree`). Warp test suite = **86 green**. `/warp` is now real-task-validated end-to-end.

Verified on a real 5-chapter campaign (ه°پç¥‍و¼”ç¾©, local Qwen3-35B). **ًںگ› Found + fixed a wizard-breaking bug:** `listProjects({limit:200})` â†’ knowledge endpoint caps at 100 â†’ 422 â†’ empty project dropdown â†’ wizard unusable; fixed both callers (BookProjectStep + ModelMatrixStep â†’ limit 100). Also fixed e2e spec contract (activity/chapters return `items` not `rows`). **Remaining (optional):** estimate-screen visual (no wizard-listed book has published chapters; feature contract-proven) + run `campaign-factory.spec.ts` live (`D-FACTORY-E2E-SPEC-LIVE-SMOKE`).

> **ًںڈ— LLM-EXECUTION RE-ARCHITECTURE (2026-06-11) â€” audit + spec + Phase 0 shipped.** A real incident (slow reasoning 35B held the single GPU governor slot; queued calls failed `governor: acquire timeout`; cancelling the campaign did NOT free the GPU) triggered a system-wide review. **Audit** ([`docs/reviews/2026-06-11-llm-execution-architecture-audit.md`](../reviews/2026-06-11-llm-execution-architecture-audit.md), 12-area multi-agent): the LLM seam is **pull-blocking by default** everywhere except campaign-service (the event-driven reference); **cancel is DB-state-only** platform-wide because the job goroutine runs under `observability.DetachedContext` (cancellation stripped) â€” the abort machinery exists (governor/streamer honor `ctx.Done()`) but was never fed a cancellable ctx. 7 ranked gaps (S1 cancel + S2 submit_and_wait pin = Critical). **Spec** ([`docs/specs/2026-06-11-llm-execution-event-driven-rearchitecture.md`](../specs/2026-06-11-llm-execution-event-driven-rearchitecture.md), PO-approved): fire-and-forget + queue-throughout for the **job path** (not interactive SSE), mostly *wiring* (reuse outboxâ†’relayâ†’Redis + campaign consumer pattern); phased, `submit_and_wait` stays a compat adapter. **âœ… Phase 0 DONE** (provider-registry only, M): a cancellable per-job context + `jobIDâ†’CancelFunc` registry ([`job_cancel_registry.go`](../../services/provider-registry-service/internal/api/job_cancel_registry.go)) so `DELETE /v1/llm/jobs/{id}` **aborts the in-flight provider call + frees the GPU slot the instant it's issued** (ctx threads Processâ†’Guardâ†’streamerâ†’`NewRequestWithContext`, so a blocked Read on a silent reasoning model unblocks) + optional wall-clock backstop (`LLM_JOB_WALLCLOCK_TIMEOUT_S`, default off). Cancel-race handled by the existing `rows==0` finalize gate (no double-settle). 6/6 new tests; build/vet clean. /review-impl: no HIGH/MED. **Deferred:** `D-PHASE0-CANCEL-LIVE-SMOKE` (cancel a real slow job â†’ assert slot frees in one tick â€” the incident regression; needs the worker stack).

> **ًںڈ— PHASE 1 IN PROGRESS (2026-06-11)** â€” plan [`docs/plans/2026-06-11-llm-rearch-phase1-queue-and-event.md`](../plans/2026-06-11-llm-rearch-phase1-queue-and-event.md), 3 commits. **âœ… Commit 1 DONE (durable terminal-event contract, provider-registry only):** new `job_event_outbox` table written in the SAME finalize tx (worker `FinalizeWithUsageOutbox` + cancel `Cancel` rewritten to a tx, every terminal status) â†’ `UsageRelay.drainTerminalOnce` XADDs to **`loreweave:events:llm_job_terminal`** (per-job-correlated, at-least-once, dedup on job_id; `result_ref`=job_id). The fire-and-forget RabbitMQ notifier stays for notification-service. **Decision (documented in plan):** terminal-event transport = Redis stream via the existing outboxâ†’relay (durable; campaign already consumes Redis streams), NOT the spec's literal "topic exchange". `kind` left empty for now (consumers key on job_id; Commit 3 populates it for queue routing). 6 new pgxmock/redismock tests; `go build`+`vet`+full service suite green. **âœ… Commit 2 DONE (SDK event adapter, backward-compatible):** `wait_terminal` is now an **event-interruptible poll** â€” when the `LLMClient` is built with `event_redis_url` it blocks on an XREAD of `loreweave:events:llm_job_terminal` between polls (woken the instant THIS job's terminal event lands; filters by job_id on a no-group fan-out stream), and degrades to the poll on any Redis fault or missed event (defense in depth, mirrors worker-ai wake.py). `event_redis_url=None` (default) â‡’ today's pure-poll, **zero caller impact** â€” services opt in during Phase 2. Lazy redis import (no hard dep). `get_job` stays the source of truth (the event only accelerates). 2 new tests (event wakes poll; redis-fault falls back) + 19 existing green (21/21 `test_client_jobs`). *Deferred:* explicit `await_job_event`/`submit_and_await_event` coroutine-release API â†’ add when a Phase-2 caller needs it (`D-SDK-AWAIT-JOB-EVENT`); the real occupancy-decouple comes from Commit 3's queue. (6 unrelated pre-existing SDK suite failures â€” extraction summarize_level prompt fixtures, obs-tracing wiring for chat/knowledge, a video-error test-ordering flake â€” confirmed failing on the clean tree, not from this change.)
**âœ… Commit 3 DONE (durable work queue + consumer pool, behind a default-OFF flag):** `internal/jobs/queue.go` `JobQueue` â€” publish to a durable `llm.jobs` queue + a consumer pool (prefetch=workers) that, per delivery, resolves the provider kind and gates on a **per-kind in-process semaphore** sized `ratelimit.MaxFor(kind, cloudMax)` (localâ†’1 = the single GPU; cloudâ†’cloudMax) â†’ jobs **WAIT** on the semaphore instead of failing acquire (kills the `ErrGovernorTimeout` cascade). **Single queue + per-kind semaphore** chosen over spec D1's per-kind queues (handles any provider kind with no up-front enumeration; same bound + wait-not-fail + durability). `doSubmitJob` enqueues when the queue is live (publish-fail â†’ falls back to direct dispatch, never drops a persisted job); the consumer runs `Worker.ProcessJob` under the Phase-0 cancellable ctx (DELETE still aborts; cancel-before-dispatch handled by the DB status gate). Governor demoted to a safety belt inside Guard. New: `repo.LoadForProcess`/`ResolveKind`, `ratelimit.MaxFor`, `config.LLMJobQueueEnabled` (env `LLM_JOB_QUEUE_ENABLED`, default false). **Redelivery re-runs only pre-`MarkRunning` (pending) crashes**; a stuck-`running` crash is the truth-sweeper's job (آ§5.6, not built â€” `D-PHASE1-RUNNING-SWEEPER`). 5 new unit tests (semaphore sizing, ResolveKind, LoadForProcess, ProcessJob skip/drop); `go build`+`vet`+full service suite green. **Default-OFF â‡’ zero behavior change until enabled** â€” so the `GOVERNOR_ACQUIRE_TIMEOUT_MS=600000` band-aid STAYS until the queue is enabled + live-smoked (`D-PHASE1-QUEUE-LIVE-SMOKE` = enable on a stack-up, induce >poolsize local jobs â†’ assert queue not `acquire timeout`, cancel mid-gen â†’ slot frees one tick; THEN revert the band-aid via `D-REVERT-GOVERNOR-ACQUIRE-BANDAID`).
**âœ… D-PHASE1-QUEUE-LIVE-SMOKE PASS + D-REVERT-GOVERNOR-ACQUIRE-BANDAID DONE (2026-06-11, real stack).** Enabled `LLM_JOB_QUEUE_ENABLED=true` + **reverted the band-aid** (`GOVERNOR_ACQUIRE_TIMEOUT_MS` back to the 30s code default) in compose, rebuilt+restarted provider-registry (consumer started, workers=16). **(1) queue-not-fail:** submitted 6 concurrent local lm_studio chat jobs (semaphore=1) â†’ **6 completed, 0 failed, 0 acquire_timeouts**, no direct-dispatch fallback â€” the queue serialises them so the governor never contends (the incident, now closed at default acquire-timeout). **(2) cancel-frees-slot:** a 4000-token job DELETEd after 2s â†’ 204, DB `status=cancelled` **ran_secs=2.1** (aborted, not the full ~80s), `stream failed: context canceled`, `gov:conc:lm_studio` ZCARD=**0** (slot freed), terminal-event cancel emitted+relayed (also validates Commit 1). Cosmetic: worker logs `finalize failed: context canceled` on the cancel-race (harmless â€” the cancel handler already finalized/emitted/freed via request-ctx) â†’ `D-CANCEL-FINALIZE-LOG-NOISE` (LOW). The compose now defaults the queue ON for dev.

**âœ… Phase 1 FOLLOW-UPS DONE (2026-06-11):**
- **`D-PHASE1-RUNNING-SWEEPER`** â€” `Repo.SweepStuckRunning` + a periodic loop in `NewServer` (env `LLM_RUNNING_SWEEP_TIMEOUT_S` default 1800 / `LLM_RUNNING_SWEEP_INTERVAL_S` default 60; 0 = off) bulk-fails any `running` job whose `last_progress_at` is older than the timeout (a streaming job bumps it per chunk â†’ only genuinely-stalled jobs swept) + writes a durable terminal event per swept job. **Live-verified:** on restart it swept the **7 real orphaned `running` jobs** (incl. the 5h-old `019eb625` from the earlier failed runs) â†’ `LLM_STUCK_TIMEOUT` in one tick (running 7â†’0, swept 0â†’7). 2 unit tests.
- **`D-SDK-AWAIT-JOB-EVENT`** â€” explicit `await_job_event(job_id, timeout_s=None)` + `submit_and_await_event(request, timeout_s=None)` on the SDK client (thin wrappers over the event-driven `wait_terminal` + an optional deadline via `asyncio.wait_for`). 2 tests (returns terminal; times out). 23/23 `test_client_jobs` green.

**Phase 1 (provider-registry + SDK) BUILD COMPLETE + queue live-smoked â€” all 3 commits + follow-ups.**

> **ًں”€ MERGED origin/integration/e0-collaboration â†’ feat (2026-06-11, `e93d6682`).** The E0 collaboration epic (collaboration grants + BYOK caller-pays across knowledge/translation/composition/extraction + book self-adopt + a new `sdks/go/grantclient`). **0 textual conflicts** (orthogonal to the LLM re-arch) AND verified semantically clean: knowledge **2332**, translation **595**, campaign **155**, worker-ai **179**, composition **429**, FE tsc + vitest **1297**, all Go services build, grantclient + loreweave_llm SDKs green. **E0-4b sub-B fix = a SEPARATE deferred task (do later, large).**

> **âœ… Phase 2a DONE (event-resume opt-in, 2026-06-11).** Wired `event_redis_url=settings.redis_url` into the SDK Client in all three consumers' `get_llm_client()` singletons (`worker-ai/app/llm_client.py`, `knowledge-service/app/clients/llm_client.py`, `translation-service/app/llm_client.py`) â€” their wrappers already call `sdk.wait_terminal`, so it transparently becomes **event-interruptible** (wakes on `loreweave:events:llm_job_terminal`, poll-fallback on Redis fault). **No call-site changes.** Coexists with the merged E0 caller-pays (which threads `billing_user_id`/`set_campaign_id` per-call, orthogonal to construction). Unit suites all green. **Live-smoke:** rebuilt+restarted the 3 services, dispatched a real 1-chapter extraction on project `019eb683` â†’ all **4 extraction provider jobs completed** (entity/event/relation/fact, 7/7) via the event-resume SDK, no SDK event-path errors (the redis warnings are worker-ai's pre-existing wake.py noise). **Phase 2b IN PROGRESS (full decouple, serial per /loom).** Design + the code-grounded plan: [`docs/plans/2026-06-11-llm-rearch-phase2b-decouple-design.md`](../plans/2026-06-11-llm-rearch-phase2b-decouple-design.md). **/warp was TRIAGED OUT** (the worker-ai extraction slice must edit `loreweave_extraction`, which knowledge ALSO imports â†’ slices not independent â†’ fall back to /loom serial per warp G6). **Key scope findings:** (1) sync request endpoints (`/translate-text`, summary-regen, wiki) are NOT decouple targets (the handler must block to return) â€” only the **background batch** paths (translation `session_translator`, worker-ai extraction) qualify; (2) both genuine targets are per-chunk/per-op aggregation flows (HARD), BUT translation **de-risks to MEDIUM** because `chapter_translation_chunks` already persists per-chunk `translated_text`/`status`/`compact_memo_applied` â†’ the sequential `session_history` is **RECONSTRUCTED from completed chunk rows**, not serialized in-memory. **âœ… 2b-T1 DONE + pushed (`91345917`):** additive migration scaffolding â€” `chapter_translations += pipeline_stage, provider_job_id`; `chapter_translation_chunks += provider_job_id` (+ resume index); hot-path untouched; 595 unit green; applied+verified on live DB. **âœ… 2b-T2 ENGINE DONE + pushed (`be799f4b`):** the decoupled text-translate state machine. `chapter_translations += resume_state JSONB` (explicit running-state blob â€” persisted, not reconstructed, because compaction is itself an LLM call whose memo isn't recoverable from chunk rows; this superseded the reconstruct-from-chunks idea once that fragility surfaced). `workers/decoupled_translate.py` = a PURE state machine (`new_resume_state`/`decide_next_action`/`apply_translate_result`/`apply_compact_result` â€” stateless-by-construction incl. the compaction transition: history>آ½ctx â†’ `compact`, after compact â†’ history reset â†’ `translate`) + the async shell (`start_chapter`/`resume`/`_submit_next` + DB helpers). `LLMClient.submit_job` added (fire-and-forget sibling that stamps campaign attribution then returns job_id without waiting); the drift guard narrowed to flag the RAW SDK `_sdk/.sdk.submit_job` (calling the wrapper is the sanctioned decoupled path). **8 pure-SM tests (incl. compaction) + 603 translation unit green; resume_state applied on live DB. NOT yet wired** (additive dead-code until the consumer lands â†’ hot-path unchanged).
**Engine made SELF-CONTAINED (uncommitted at session end, then committed):** `resume_state` now carries `msg` + `context_window`, so the consumer (which only sees a job_id) resumes without re-deriving job config; `resume()`/`_submit_next()` read them from the loaded state (signatures dropped those params). 17 engine+guard tests green.

**âœ… 2b-T2 INTEGRATION DONE (2026-06-11, flag-gated, unit-green + partial live-smoke).** The decoupled text engine is now wired end-to-end behind `translation_decouple_enabled` (config, default False; compose env `TRANSLATION_DECOUPLE_ENABLED` on both API + worker).
- **`_finalize_chapter` EXTRACTED** (option a) from `chapter_worker._process_chapter` â€” the shared persist+emit block (status='completed'+body+tokens tx â†’ counter â†’ `active_*` campaign-aware on_conflict â†’ outbox `chapter.translated` â†’ quality/memo/record_stage/chapter_done/job_completion), called by BOTH synchronous pipelines (block+text) AND the consumer's finalize hook. **Idempotent for at-least-once delivery:** the guarded UPDATE `â€¦ AND status <> 'completed'` â†’ asyncpg `UPDATE 0` â‡’ `already_done` â‡’ skip the counter/active/outbox + ALL post-commit emits (neutral for the sync path, which re-marks 'running' upstream). `isinstance(res,str)` guard so test-mocks default to "proceed".
- **Consumer** `app/events/llm_terminal_consumer.py` (modeled on `GlossaryStaleConsumer`): stream `loreweave:events:llm_job_terminal`, group `translation-llm-resume`, `socket_timeout=None`/`id="$"`/BUSYGROUP-safe/drain-pending/bounded-retry. `_handle`: wire `job_id` â†’ `SELECT id,resume_state WHERE provider_job_id=$1::uuid` (none â†’ **ack+ignore**, the dedup for superseded/foreign jobs) â†’ `set_campaign_id(msg.campaign_id)` â†’ `sdk.get_job` â†’ `decoupled_translate.resume(â€¦, finalize_cb)` â†’ ack; `set_campaign_id(None)` in finally.
- **resume() reordered finalize-FIRST then `_clear_resume_state`** â€” crash-safe: a crash between them redelivers, re-folds from the (pre-fold) persisted state â†’ same body â†’ the finalize guard absorbs the dup. (Clear-first would lose the row â†’ chapter stuck 'running'.)
- **`_process_chapter` rewired:** `elif flag and pipeline_version=='v2'` (TEXT path only) â†’ `decoupled_translate.start_chapter(...)` + RETURN; else the sync text path. `start_chapter` stores `chapter_text` in resume_state (finalize quality-feed source). Consumer started in `main.py` lifespan, flag-gated.
- **VERIFY: 608 translation unit + 5 new consumer/idempotency tests green; provider-gate OK.** **Live-smoke (partial, real stack, flag on):** rebuilt both images, dispatched a real v2 chapter â€” it hit the BLOCK path (`has_json_body=True`; real novels always have a Tiptap body), so the decouple branch correctly **did NOT fire** (flag scope proven). The block job **completed via the extracted `_finalize_chapter`** (counter=1 â€” extract-method regression LIVE-CLEARED on a real chapter) AND the consumer **read 26 real provider terminal events** (the block batches), `pending=0/lag=0/0 errors` â†’ the **foreign-job ack+ignore invariant proven live on real events**. **The text-path submitâ†’resume round-trip is NOT yet live-proven** (no text-only chapter exists in the data) â†’ **`D-2B-T2-RESUME-LIVE-SMOKE`** â€” fold into the T3 block live-smoke (block decouple uses real data + the IDENTICAL consumer/finalize machinery). Stack reverted to flag-off default.

**âœ… 2b-T3a DONE (2026-06-11, V2 BLOCK decouple â€” LIVE-SMOKED end-to-end on real data).** The block path is the real-data path (imported novels carry a Tiptap body). New `workers/decoupled_block_translate.py` = a PURE state machine + async shell porting `translate_chapter_blocks` + its `translate_batch_with_retry` validate/correction loop into resumable form: **every batch attempt (incl. each validation retry) is a submitâ†’releaseâ†’resume**, driven by the terminal event. resume_state `mode='block'` carries the serialized batch plan (`{block_indices, combined, input_texts, token_estimate}` per batch), glossary `prompt_block`+`correction_map`, rolling_summary, translated_texts accumulator, batch_idx/attempt, blocks-for-rebuild. Pure fns: `decide_block_action`/`build_batch_messages`/`apply_batch_result` (accept-on-valid-or-out-of-attempts vs retry-with-correction-hint)/`reassemble_blocks`/`memo_from_translated`. The **consumer dispatches by `rs['mode']`** (text â†’ `decoupled_translate.resume`; block â†’ `decoupled_block_translate.resume` + a 4-arg json finalize_cb), reusing `_finalize_chapter` UNCHANGED. `_process_chapter` block branch: flag+v2 â†’ `start_chapter_blocks` (returns `submitted`; False=no-translatable â†’ fall through to sync finalize) + RETURN. **VERIFY: 617 translation unit (608 + 9 block-SM tests) + gate OK.** **âœ… LIVE-SMOKE PASSED (real ن¸‡هڈ¤ç¥‍ه¸‌ ch1, flag on):** worker logged `DECOUPLED BLOCK pipeline` + released; consumer drove the batches off terminal events â†’ chapter `completed`, counter=1, `translated_body_json` 16.7KB, in=5717/out=3410 accumulated across batches, `pipeline_stage=done` (decoupled-finalize proof â€” the sync path never sets it), **0 consumer errors**, **real Vietnamese output**. `active_set=f` correct (force_retranslate on a pre-translated ch â†’ non-campaign first-write-wins). **CLOSES `D-2B-T2-RESUME-LIVE-SMOKE`** (identical consumer/finalize machinery, now proven on the round-trip). Block-2 partial source-echo = the known `D-V3-TRANSLATION-PROMPT-ECHO`/7B issue, NOT a decouple bug.

Resume is via `resume_state`, so this is an **observability** regression only (no correctness/resume impact). Port `_insert_chunk_row`/`_update_block_chunk_row` into the block engine when the decouple flag goes on by default.

**âœ… 2b WX FOUNDATION COMPLETE (2026-06-11, /loom WX-T3 â€” 8 commits, all verified).** The worker-ai extraction decouple's entire seam foundation + pure state machine is built + byte-identical/additive-verified. Per the design doc [`-workerai-extraction-decouple-design`](../plans/2026-06-11-llm-rearch-phase2b-workerai-extraction-decouple-design.md) (now carries the **turnkey WX-T3b build plan**).

- **WX-T2 (`2662b986`) / T2b (`b70c34b0`) / T2c (`78bf13ff`) / T2d (`6320cb3c`) â€” the COMPLETE seam set.** Every extractor + recovery + filter is now fully decomposable: `build_<op>_system` + `build_<op>_submit_kwargs` + `parse_<op>_job` + `apply_<op>_job` (entity/relation/event/fact); recovery `prepare_recovery`/`build_recovery_batches`/`apply_recovery_batch`/`finalize_recovery`; filter `build_filter_category_batches`/`compute_filter_kept`. The sync `extract_pass2` calls them â‡’ **byte-identical** (T2/T2b/T2c) or purely additive (T2d). **VERIFY: SDK extraction 267 (3 pre-existing `summarize_level`) + worker-ai 190 + knowledge extraction units 377 + gate OK** across the set.
- **WX-T3a (`b7469cb9` + revision `9ea15602`):** `decoupled_extract.py` PURE state machine â€” entity â†’ **trio fan-in** â†’ recovery **fan-out** â†’ filter **fan-out** (categoryأ—batch) â†’ persist; all idempotent on a dup terminal event. **14 pure-SM tests.** (The fan-out structure was a WX-T2c discovery â€” filter is concurrent-categoryأ—batch, recovery is N Tier-3 batches.)
- **Scope discoveries (all surfaced + resolved into the foundation):** the decouple is a 4-layer-seam tar pit (T2 call â†’ T2b recovery/filter call â†’ T2c prepare/apply â†’ T2d system/apply) because every layer interleaved analysis/apply around the LLM call.
  - **NEXT BUILD = WX-T3** (the L slice): the decoupled orchestrator in worker-ai (`decoupled_extract.py` â€” the entityâ†’trio fan-inâ†’recoveryâ†’filter state machine over the `build_`/`parse_` seams, â‰¥3 in-flight `provider_job_ids` for the trio fan-in) + a worker-ai `llm_job_terminal` consumer (model on translation's) + finalize-first idempotent persist + the E0-3 `set_billing_user_id` re-bind on resume. Behind `extraction_decouple_enabled`. Then `D-WX-LIVE-SMOKE`.

**âœ… /review-impl ON THE 2b+WX DECOUPLE (2026-06-11) â€” 1 fixed, 5 deferred.**
- **âœ… FINDING 1 FIXED (MED, load-bearing):** `_finalize_chapter`'s `already_done` guard skipped `_check_job_completion` on a duplicate delivery â€” so a crash between the finalize-tx commit and the post-commit emits would, on redelivery, skip job-finalization â†’ the JOB stays `'running'` forever (no recovery for non-campaign jobs; the campaign reconcile backstops campaign jobs). My own `test_finalize_idempotent_*` had **enshrined** the bug (`check.assert_not_awaited()`). Fix: per-chapter telemetry (quality/memo/metric) stays guarded, but `_emit_chapter_done` + `_check_job_completion` (idempotent / harmless re-emit) now run ALWAYS. Test flipped to assert they ARE awaited on a duplicate. 617 + 5 green.
- **Deferred rows (review-impl):**
  - **`D-2B-DECOUPLE-FLAG-COUPLING` (MED):** the worker's decouple branch + the API's consumer key off the SAME `translation_decouple_enabled` â€” a mismatch (worker ON / API consumer OFF) silently stalls submitted chapters (2h sweeper â†’ failed). Compose wires `${TRANSLATION_DECOUPLE_ENABLED}` to both, but it's convention not enforcement. Add a startup log/assert when the worker's flag is on but no consumer is reachable; document the invariant before default-on.
  - **`D-2B-SUBMIT-PERSIST-GAP` (MED):** `_submit_next(_batch)` does `submit_job` THEN `_persist_inflight` â€” a crash between (or, near-impossibly, a job completing before the آµs persist) orphans the new job's terminal event (consumer finds no row â†’ ack+ignore) â†’ stall â†’ 2h-sweeper-as-FAILED (work lost). Backstopped but as a failure. Persist-intent-before-submit if WX/T3b raise the stakes.
  - **`D-2B-T3A-BLOCK-OBSERVABILITY` (LOW, extends D-2B-T3A-BLOCK-CHUNK-ROWS):** the decoupled block path drops per-batch chunk rows AND `record_stage("translation.batch")` metrics; the text path writes `chunk_text=''`. Port all three before default-on.
  - **`D-2B-RESUME-STATE-SIZE` (LOW perf):** block `resume_state` (blocks + batches + chapter_text + growing translated_texts) re-serialized per batch â†’ TOAST pressure on very large chapters. Split an immutable `resume_plan` from the mutable cursor if it bites.
  - **`D-2B-SHELL-UNIT-TESTS` (LOW):** the async shells (block `resume()` failure-fold, consumer bounded-retry, end-to-end correction-retry) are live-smoke-only; add unit tests before default-on. Pure SMs are covered (8+9).

**âœ… WAVE 1a DONE (2026-06-12, debt-paydown roadmap) â€” extraction consumer money-path hardened (unit-verified, flag still OFF).** The two atomic-tx correctness fixes /review-impl flagged on WX-T3b, in `services/worker-ai/app/llm_extract_consumer.py` + `runner.py` (`_record_spending` now conn-aware):
- **`D-WX-PERSIST-DOUBLE-SPEND` FIXED:** `_persist_chunk` now runs cursor-advance + run-emit + chapter_extracted + `_record_spending` + `_clear_resume` in **ONE tx**, gated by a `SELECT â€¦ FOR UPDATE` re-read that **skips the whole finalize when resume_state is already NULL** (a concurrent/redelivered finalize) â†’ no re-spend. `persist_pass2` stays OUTSIDE the tx (idempotent knowledge MERGE; never pin a pooled conn across the HTTP call). Replaced the old `_advance_cursor_and_emit_run` best-effort-fallback call (best-effort advance would have left resume_state â†’ re-spend); the decoupled path now is strict-or-redeliver.
- **`D-WX-TRIO-FANIN-RACE` FIXED:** the trio fold re-reads the row `FOR UPDATE` and persists the MERGED rs **under the lock**, so concurrent relation/event/fact deliveries on >1 replica can't lose an op (the read-modify-write is serialised). The terminal persist runs OUTSIDE the lock (`_persist_chunk` re-locks + is idempotent). Skips cleanly if a concurrent winner already advanced past TRIO.
- **VERIFY: 203 worker-ai unit (7 new `test_llm_extract_consumer.py` money-path tests w/ a fake asyncpg pool/conn harness) + provider-gate OK.** Single-service change; the end-to-end live-smoke is the existing infra-blocked `D-WX-LIVE-SMOKE` (Wave 3).
- **/review-impl (pre-commit) â€” verified CLEAN:** `persist_pass2` is cost-idempotent (Neo4j retract+merge; summary embedding dedups on `summary_input_md5` cache) so the only worker-side spend (`_record_spending`) is the one now guarded â†’ the double-spend claim holds; the seeded resume_state shape matches `_persist_chunk`'s consumption (run_id/chapter_extracted kwargs/cursor/persist_ctx); `fold_trio_op` preserves all rs keys via `dict(rs)` so seeded persist fields survive folds. **2 cheap findings FIXED:** (1, MED) the strict-tx finalize drops the best-effort fallback â†’ a POISON finalize strands resume_state with no sweeper yet â†’ added a loud actionable poison log + **made Wave 1b the gate before default-on** (the sweeper is the designed runtime backstop, not polish); (3, LOW) added `test_real_fold_completes_and_finalizes_with_seed_keys_intact` running the REAL `fold_trio_job` â†’ `_persist_chunk` to pin key-preservation at the finalize boundary (the monkeypatched tests can't). **1 accepted:** (2, LOW) the FOR UPDATE serialisation is asserted by SQL-substring not behaviour (mocks can't lock; the real-replica proof is `D-WX-LIVE-SMOKE`).
- **NOT in Wave 1a (split to Wave 1b for a clean commit):** `D-WX-SUBMIT-PERSIST-GAP` / `D-2B-SUBMIT-PERSIST-GAP` â€” the crash-between-submit-and-persist orphan. Proper fix = a **stuck-resume sweeper** (mirror `D-PHASE1-RUNNING-SWEEPER`): re-drive `extraction_jobs` with non-null resume_state older than a timeout by re-checking each in-flight provider_job_id â€” also backstops PEL-expired transient failures.

**âœ… WAVE 1b DONE (2026-06-12) â€” stuck-resume sweeper (closes `D-WX-SUBMIT-PERSIST-GAP`; the runtime backstop Wave 1a's strict-tx made load-bearing).** `services/worker-ai/app/llm_extract_consumer.py` (`_sweep_once` + `run_resume_sweeper`) + `config.py` (`extraction_resume_sweep_interval_s`=60 / `_timeout_s`=900 / `_batch`=20) + `main.py` (gather'd inside the decouple-flag block) + `runner.py`/consumer (`updated_at=now()` on every resume_state write so idle-detection is accurate).
- A Redis stream gives no redelivery after ack, so a consumer crash/poison, a lost terminal event, or a submitâ†’persist gap can strand a `resume_state` row with no runtime recovery (only a restart drain). The sweeper periodically finds rows idle > timeout (`status IN running/paused`), re-checks each in-flight `provider_job_id`'s terminal status, and replays the consumer's **idempotent `_resume`** (the FOR UPDATE + finalize-recheck from Wave 1a make a concurrent consumer + sweeper safe). A still-in-flight job is left alone (slow â‰  stuck); only TERMINAL jobs are replayed (else `_resume` would fold an incomplete result).
- **VERIFY: 209 worker-ai unit (6 new sweeper tests) + AST main/consumer OK + provider-gate OK.** Only runs when the decouple flag is on (inert otherwise). Single-service; live-smoke = `D-WX-LIVE-SMOKE` (Wave 3).
- **/review-impl â€” verified CLEAN + 2 fixed:** BYOK identity is correct (the sweeper's `billing_user_id or user_id` matches `submit_job`'s billing override = the job owner; a BYOK-identity test pins it); terminal-status matches the SDK exactly. **FIXED:** (#1, LOW drift) replaced the hardcoded `TERMINAL_JOB_STATUSES` set with the SDK's `Job.is_terminal()` (single source of truth); (#4, test) added the BYOK get_job-owner test; also refreshed the two now-stale comments that claimed "no sweeper yet". **Accept-tracked:** (#2, LOW-MED) multi-replica sweep has no `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` â†’ two replicas can double-submit an ENTITY-stage re-drive (trio FOLD is FOR-UPDATE-safe; bounded waste, converges) â†’ folded into **`D-WX-TRIO-FANIN-RACE`** (add SKIP LOCKED when multi-replica is enabled); (#3, cosmetic) a cancelled/failed job leaves resume_state set (sweeper filters running/paused) â†’ orphan DB cruft, not a correctness issue.
- **`D-2B-SUBMIT-PERSIST-GAP` (translation) MOVED to Wave 2** â€” different service/table (`chapter_translations.resume_state`), and translation's finalize kept its `status <> 'completed'` idempotency (no strict-tx gap), so its sweeper batches naturally with the other translation-parity items.

**âœ… WAVE 2a DONE (2026-06-12) â€” translation resume-sweeper (`D-2B-SUBMIT-PERSIST-GAP`, mirror worker-ai Wave 1b).** Plan: [`docs/plans/2026-06-12-translation-resume-sweeper.md`](../plans/2026-06-12-translation-resume-sweeper.md). 7 files, translation-service only, flag-gated.
- **Migration (additive):** `chapter_translations += updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()` (it had none) + a partial index `(updated_at) WHERE resume_state IS NOT NULL` for the sweep scan. `updated_at=now()` bumped in both engines' `_persist_inflight` (block + text) so idle-detection reflects real progress.
- **Sweeper:** `LLMTerminalConsumer.sweep_once` + `run_sweeper` â€” re-drives any `chapter_translations` row with `resume_state IS NOT NULL` idle past the timeout by re-checking its single `provider_job_id`'s terminal status (`job.is_terminal()`) and replaying the **extracted `_resume_loaded`** (the campaign-bind + block/text dispatch the event path now also uses). Config `translation_resume_sweep_interval_s`=60/`_timeout_s`=900/`_batch`=20; main.py wires it inside the decouple-flag block. Translation's finalize keeps its `status <> 'completed'` idempotency, so this is a backstop (not load-bearing like worker-ai's strict-tx).
- **+ /review-impl finding #1 FIXED in-commit â€” `D-2B-TRANSL-RESUME-RACE` RESOLVED (the translation analog of worker-ai's Wave-1a trio race).** Both engines' `resume()` now serialise the fold under a `SELECT â€¦ FOR UPDATE` on the chapter row + re-verify `provider_job_id` still equals THIS job; the next-step submit + its provider_job_id advance happen UNDER the lock so a racing resume (consumer-vs-sweeper, or multi-replica) re-reads the advanced id and **skips â†’ no double batch-submit**. Finalize runs AFTER the lock (idempotent via `status<>'completed'`; nesting `_finalize_chapter` â€” which locks the same row on its own conn â€” inside the tx would **deadlock**). `_persist_inflight`/`_submit_next(_batch)`/`_record_chunk` now take an executor (Pool for the initial submit, the locked Connection during resume).
- **VERIFY: 627 translation unit (4 sweeper + 6 race-guard/updated_at tests; `_resume_loaded` refactor keeps the campaign-bind test green) + AST OK + provider-gate OK.** Migration auto-applies on service start (idempotent).

Only bites IF translation decouple later adopts BYOK (then both the consumer fallback and the sweeper would need the event's `owner_user_id` / a stored billing id). `D-2B-TRANSL-SWEEP-LIVE-SMOKE` â€” the end-to-end strandedâ†’re-drive needs the stack (fold into Wave 3).

> **âœ… RESOLVED â€” `D-EXTRACTION-USAGE-BILLING-VISIBILITY` investigated 2026-06-12 (live DB) â€” NO BUG, working as designed.** The user ran glossary/extraction with a **local LM Studio model** (`user_model 019eb620` = `lm_studio` Qwen2.5-7B, `pricing {input_per_mtok:0, output_per_mtok:0}`) â†’ `total_cost_usd=0` is **CORRECT** (self-hosted = no API cost). The billing pipeline records EVERYTHING correctly: every LLM call â†’ provider-registry computes cost â†’ `usage_outbox` â†’ `usage-billing.usage_logs` with the right `owner_user_id`, real `input/output_tokens`, `billing_decision=recorded`, `request_status=success`, `purpose` (entity/relation/event/fact_extraction, translation, chat). The user-facing surface EXISTS: `GET /v1/model-billing/usage-logs` + `/usage-summary` + `/account-balance` (usage-billing `server.go:106-109`) + FE `frontend/src/pages/UsagePage.tsx` + `features/usage/api.ts`. **Divergence proof:** May usage on OTHER (priced) models shows real cost ($2.51 entity, $6.75 chat); today's Qwen-local runs show $0 â€” same pipeline, different model pricing. **Why the user "couldn't see":** (a) cost is legitimately $0 (local model) so the UsagePage shows $0; (b) the input/output *payloads* are encrypted at rest (`usage_logs.input/output_payload_ciphertext` + `usage_log_details`, decryptable via an audited path) â€” only the *token counts* are plaintext. **No code change needed.** Optional follow-ups if the user wants $-visibility for local usage: set nominal per-mtok pricing on the local model in provider-registry (but $0 is the honest value), OR verify the UsagePage is discoverable/linked in the nav (FE UX). The worker's flat `_DEFAULT_COST_PER_ITEM` estimate is a SEPARATE number (`knowledge_projects.actual_cost_usd`) used for the in-job spend-guardrail, distinct from the authoritative per-token `usage_logs`.

Trace confirms the data IS produced + flows: provider-registry `FinalizeWithUsageOutbox` computes `cost_usd` â†’ `usage_outbox` â†’ Redis `loreweave:events:usage` â†’ **usage-billing-service `usage_logs`** (keyed by `owner_user_id` â€” the per-user store the user expects to view). So the pipeline exists end-to-end. **Hypotheses to check when picked up (in order):** (1) **benign $0** â€” the model was likely a LOCAL provider (`lm_studio`/`ollama`/local Qwen), which `provider-registry/internal/billing/default_pricing.go` sets to `freePricing()` â‡’ `cost_usd=$0` (correct, not a bug); tokens still recorded. (2) **attribution/handling bug** â€” verify `usage_consumer.go` actually writes the `usage_logs` row for that job under the right `owner_user_id` (vs a BYOK `billing_user_id` mismatch) so the per-user view surfaces it. (3) **no surface for extraction** â€” translation-service has NO `/costs` endpoint (knowledge-service has `/v1/knowledge/costs`); job-status returns `total_input/output_tokens` but not `cost_usd`, and FE only shows `cost_estimate`. **First step:** query `usage_logs` (+ `extraction_jobs.total_input/output_tokens`) for yesterday's job by the user's id and confirm whether the row exists / what cost+model it has â€” that disambiguates (1) vs (2) immediately. Then decide: fix attribution (if 2) and/or add an extraction `/costs` endpoint + FE surfacing (if 3).
